RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a short, genetically conserved stem–bulge–loop hairpin found at the 5′ end of every viral transcript.  Functional constructs range from minimal 29-nt fragments (containing the essential bulge and loop) up to the canonical 52–59-nt forms seen in full-length genomes; in all cases the active fold is the same: a double-stranded stem interrupted by a three-base pyrimidine bulge (commonly UCU) and capped by a six-nucleotide loop.  This bulge-and-loop motif first captures an arginine-rich segment of the viral Tat protein, inducing a local conformational switch that allows Tat to recruit the host Cyclin T1–CDK9 (P-TEFb) complex.  The resultant Tat–TAR–P-TEFb assembly converts RNA polymerase II from abortive to highly processive elongation, boosting viral gene expression >100-fold, and the same RNA structure also facilitates strand-transfer during reverse transcription.  Because the bulge–loop signature is indispensable and sequence-invariant across strains, TAR acts as a natural “riboswitch-like” sensor that tunes the balance between transcription and genome replication, offering a structurally small yet functionally critical target for therapeutic intervention.
Small_molecule_information: 2-amino-3-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-N-[2-[3-[2-(methylamino)-1,3-thiazol-4-yl]anilino]-2-oxoethyl]propanamide, also referred to as [N-(2-N-Acetylamino-4-(3-aminophenyl)thiazole)-2-aminoacetamide]-histidinamide (9d), is a complex peptidomimetic structure comprising a Histidine (imidazole ring) fragment, linked through a Glycine backbone to a terminal 3-[2-(methylamino)-1,3-thiazol-4-yl]aniline substituent, and [N-(2-N-Acetylamino-4-(3-aminophenyl)thiazole)-2-aminoacetamide]-histidinamide (9d) is a synthetic nucleobaseamino acid conjugate engineered to occupy the HIV-1 TAR RNA stembulgeloop in place of the viral Tat protein, this linear Histidine-Glycine-substituted aniline structure effectively functions as a tripeptide analog, it replaces the natural ribose-phosphate backbone with a metabolically stable thiazolearyl core and uses a minimal glycine linker (C1) to present an imidazole-functionalized histidine side-chain for hydrogen-bonding and -stacking within the TAR bulge, the imidazole ring provides potential for general acid/base catalysis and metal coordination, while the multiple amide bonds and heterocycles enable extensive hydrogen bonding and polar interactions, in medicinal chemistry, such complex peptidomimetics are often designed as protein-protein interaction (PPI) inhibitors or receptor antagonists for peptide receptors, with their multi-functional structures allowing for precise matching with biological targets, potentially applied in signal pathway regulation and metabolic diseases research, in fluorescence-binding assays 9d exhibits moderate affinity, triggers no global RNA conformational change, and retains high specificity against excess tRNA or dsDNA, despite its favorable specificity, 9d shows no measurable suppression of Tat-mediated trans-activation in HIV-infected MAGIC-5B cells and no cytotoxicity up to a high tested concentration, these properties establish 9d as a metabolically stable, highly specific TAR ligand whose moderate affinity and lack of antiviral activity underscore the need for stronger cooperative interactions in next-generation artificial riboswitch therapeutics. -

RNA information: TERRA (Telomeric Repeat-containing RNA) is a long non-coding RNA essential for telomere maintenance. It forms a canonical RNA G-quadruplex (rG4) structure characterized by three G-tetrads, which is more stable than non-canonical two-tetrad rG4s. In vitro studies using reverse transcriptase stalling (RTS) assays demonstrate that TERRA's rG4 causes significant stalling (~40%) under potassium-rich (K⁺) conditions, confirming its stable formation. This structural integrity is crucial for its role in telomere length regulation and heterochromatin formation. Notably, ligands like PhenDC3 and PDS act as effective molecular chaperones, significantly inducing and stabilizing the TERRA rG4 structure even under unfavorable ionic conditions (Li⁺), highlighting its dynamic nature and functional importance in genome stability.; TERRA (telomeric repeat-containing RNA) is a well-characterized RNA that forms stable G-quadruplex structures, with its sequence featuring repeated G-tracts . It binds selectively to RGB-1, showing a 1:1.2 binding stoichiometry and inducing a Tₘ increase of the RNA G-quadruplex upon binding. As a 5'UTR insert in reporter mRNAs, it mediates translation repression by RGB-1 in vitro and HEK293 cells, serving as a model for studying RNA G-quadruplex-related translational regulation.
Small_molecule_information: 4,16-difluoro-8,12,20-trimethyl-8-aza-20-azoniapentacyclo[11.7.1.0{2,7}.0{9,21}.0{14,19}]henicosa-1(20),2(7),3,5,9(21),10,12,14(19),15,17-decaene, also referred to as PhenDC3, a bisquinolinium G4 ligand (a pyridodicarboxamide derivative), is a highly complex and rigid pentacyclic fused nitrogen-containing heterocyclic quaternary ammonium salt that binds rG4s via stacking on G-quartets, whose structure enables chemical derivatization (e.g., PhenDC3-alk for bioorthogonal manipulations), functionally acts as a molecular chaperone inducing folding of canonical (3-quartet, e.g., TERRA) and non-canonical (2-quartet, e.g., XIST) rG4s, stabilizes rG4s and impedes reverse transcriptase by promoting rG4 formation to aid G4 biology studies, the chemical name clearly outlines a twenty-one-carbon skeleton composed of multiple bridged and fused rings, the two fluorine atoms, strategically positioned at the 4 and 16 positions, are common bioisosteres used to enhance metabolic stability, lipophilicity, and binding affinity to biological targets, the presence of a quaternary ammonium group imparts a positive charge at physiological pH, enabling strong electrostatic interactions with negatively charged biomolecules such as DNA or acidic residues on proteins, and this rigid polycyclic scaffold and its specific three-dimensional structure suggest its potential application as a novel anti-tumor or antiviral agent, possibly functioning as a topoisomerase inhibitor or DNA intercalator in real-world drug discovery.

RNA information: The HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating stem-loop (FSS) RNA is a highly conserved structured element located within the gag-pol transcript that regulates production of the essential Gag-Pol polyprotein through a −1 ribosomal frameshift. It consists of a stable stem-loop positioned downstream of a slippery sequence that induces ribosomal pausing and promotes frameshifting in about 5–10% of translation events. This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Altering FSS activity disrupts the Gag to Gag-Pol ratio, substantially reducing viral replication and infectivity.
Small_molecule_information: N-[(E,1Z)-2-[(2-ethyl-1,2,4a,5,5a,9a,10,10a-octahydrobenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]-1,8-bis[2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-hydroxy-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]carbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-1-(oxiran-2-ylidene)-8-oxooct-4-en-7-yl]-2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carboxamide, also referred to as 1Z (the Z-isomer of the initial scaffold), is a peptidomimetic compound with a highly asymmetric structure, which demonstrates modestly improved performance compared to 1E, has affinity for the HIV-1 FSS RNA, certain selectivity over tRNA, is cell permeable and able to inhibit pseudotyped HIV infection in a dose-dependent manner (though its activity is weaker than N-methylated analogs) and lacks N-methyl substitutions thus not benefiting from the enhanced conformational bias observed in later compounds; the asymmetry of the molecule is notable in two main parts: first, it contains a fully aromatic benzo[g]quinoline group on one side and a multi-saturated octahydrobenzo[g]quinoline group on the other, leading to significant differences in aromaticity and polarity between the two ends, which may result in multi-modal interactions with its target, second, the molecular backbone includes an oxiran-2-ylidene (epoxide) structure, a high-strain, electrophilic three-membered ring, and this electrophilic group suggests its candidacy as a covalent inhibitor, capable of forming an irreversible covalent bond with nucleophilic amino acid residues (e.g., Cysteine, Lysine) in biological targets like proteases or kinases, leading to sustained activity, while its peptidomimetic portion contains a phenylalanine-polyamine derivative, offering potential interaction with the polyamine transport system or DNA, thus this molecule is likely a multifunctional anti-cancer or anti-infective lead compound with a covalent mechanism of action.

RNA information: The r(CCUG)₁₀ sequence models the pathogenic RNA associated with myotonic dystrophy type 2. Structurally, it contains four-nucleotide repeats that also produce hairpins with mismatches, although their geometry differs slightly from CUG repeats. These motifs create similar alternating stripes of electrostatic potential, making selectivity between CUG and CCUG challenging for both proteins and small molecules. Functionally, r(CCUG)₁₀ sequesters MBNL1 in a manner comparable to CUG repeats, contributing to splicing defects. Its inclusion in binding studies demonstrates the difficulty of achieving discrimination between closely related repeat RNAs and emphasizes the need for ligands that exploit subtle structural differences.
Small_molecule_information: (2R,3S,4R,5R,6R)-5-amino-2-(aminomethyl)-6-[(1R,2R,3S,4R,6S)-4,6-diamino-2-[(2S,3R,4S,5R)-4-[(2R,3R,4R,5S,6S)-3-amino-6-(aminomethyl)-4,5-dihydroxyoxan-2-yl]oxy-3-hydroxy-5-(hydroxymethyl)oxolan-2-yl]oxy-3-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxyoxane-3,4-diol, also referred to as Neomycin, is an aminoglycoside antibiotic whose molecular structure is rich in nitrogen (containing amino groups and other nitrogen-based functional groups), and as a natural aminoglycoside it is characterized by multiple cationic amino groups arranged around a polycyclic sugar framework, structurally lacking the specific aromatic stacking or spatial precision of synthetic benzo[g]quinoline derivatives which results in broad activity but little discrimination among different repeat sequences. This structural feature enables it to interact strongly with the (CUG) RNA probe associated with the pathology of myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1), with its binding arising primarily from electrostatic interactions with the phosphate backbone of nucleic acids that makes it a promiscuous and non-selective RNA binder. In affinity capillary electrophoresis (ACE) experiments, its binding constant to (CUG) RNA is significantly higher than that of pentamidine (PTMD) and 1,2-ethane bis-1-amino-4-benzamidine (EBAB), and functionally it can engage RNA targets but does not achieve the selectivity or therapeutic relevance of designed compounds. Moreover, during screening at a high concentration of 1 mM, it can completely eliminate the electrophoretic peak of the (CUG) RNA probe, demonstrating high affinity. However, it should be noted that Neomycin cannot rescue the mis-splicing of affected genes in DM1, so despite its strong RNA-binding activity, it is difficult to serve as a core therapeutic candidate for DM1, and in relevant studies it serves as a control benchmark to highlight advantages of tailored synthetic scaffolds.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: N,N-dimethyl-N-[5-methyl-7-[2-(4-methylmorpholin-4-ium-4-yl)ethoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl]ethane-1,2-diamine diiodide, this compound is a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with long diamine and quaternary ammonium salt side chains. The presence of the quaternary ammonium salt typically confers higher polarity and DNA binding affinity, making it a potential potent anti-tumor agent or a fluorescent probe in biological research.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-7-[(3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl]-1H-quinolin-2-one, also referred to as SMSM73, is a non-planar coumarin analog bearing a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and an imidazopyridine-thiazole DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge, which recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, as confirmed by fluorescence-polarization assays and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[[3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-2-oxochromen-7-yl]-methylamino]ethyl-methylazanium, also referred to as SMSM18, is an open-chain analogue of [1-[3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-2-oxochromen-7-yl]pyrrolidin-3-yl]-methylazanium and [1-[3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-2-oxochromen-7-yl]piperidin-4-yl]-methylazanium, and it is a non-planar coumarin derivative with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge, which binds the single-G bulge of RNA1 through minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, validated by FP and SAR profiling, and displays selectivity over flexible GA-rich RNA; in this compound, the C7 position of the coumarin is linked to an open-chain N-methyl ethyl quaternary ammonium salt substituent, the open-chain structure provides greater conformational flexibility, which may impact its solubility, distribution, and binding kinetics in a biological environment, it retains the features of the coumarin fluorophore, the imidazopyridine active group, and the permanent cationic nature, thus, it is developed as a fluorescent probe for cell imaging, with its activity potentially involving enzyme or receptor modulation.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[4-(3-amino-1-oxo-2,5,6,6a,7,9-hexahydroimidazo[1,5-f]pteridin-8-yl)benzoyl]amino]pentanedioic acid, also referred to as 5,10-Methylene tetrahydrofolate (5,10-methylene THF), is a folic acid analog featuring a highly modified pteridine system, specifically a hexahydroimidazo[1,5-f]pteridine core linked to a glutamate residue via a PABA segment, while 5,10-methylene THF itself is a non-azaaromatic folate derivative with a methylene group (-CH-) linking the 5th and 10th positions of the tetrahydrofolate (THF) pteridine ring that lacks planar azaaromatic features, binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis and serves as a core one-carbon carrier in metabolism transferring methylene units for thymidine synthesis and homocysteine remethylation which is critical for nucleic acid production and amino acid metabolism; structural modifications of the folate skeleton, especially those incorporating fused ring systems, are typically designed to create novel antifolate agents, the activity of such compounds is focused on the inhibition of key enzymes in the folate metabolic pathway, such as dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR) or thymidylate synthase (TS), these enzymes are essential for nucleotide biosynthesis in cancer cells and various pathogens, therefore, the molecule is primarily researched as a potential novel anticancer or antimicrobial drug. 5,10-methylene THF5,10-methenyl THF

Small_molecule_information: 2-(2-chlorophenyl)quinolin-3-ol, also referred to as CP7, is a quinoline derivative (and a hydroxyquinoline derivative with a 2-chlorophenyl substitution) that has a classic intercalator motif which provided RNA binding capability, though its cellular efficacy was not sufficiently highlighted for further progression; the quinoline scaffold of this compound is an important nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic system, very common in natural products and drug molecules, possessing broad biological activity, and its structural features are the hydroxyl group (OH) at the 3-position and the 2-chlorophenyl substituent at the 2-position, with 3-hydroxyquinoline as the core structure of many fluorescent dyes or metal chelating agents because the hydroxyl group and the quinoline nitrogen can form strong intramolecular hydrogen bonds and are capable of forming stable complexes with metal ions (e.g., aluminum, zinc, magnesium), while the 2-(2-chlorophenyl) substituent increases the molecules steric hindrance and lipophilicity, and in real-world applications, such compounds are frequently studied as fluorescent probes, particularly for detecting and imaging metal ions within cells, additionally, its structure bears resemblance to some potential anticancer or anti-inflammatory drugs.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide, also referred to as the saturated analog of DAPI (whose full name is 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide), is a compound featuring a dihydroindole scaffold and bis-imidamide groups, with the formula C16H16N4, while DAPI is a fluorescent stain binding AT-rich DNA regions, widely used in microscopy for nuclear labeling and is cell-impermeant unless membranes are compromised, making it useful for viability assays and chromosome visualization; this compound is the saturated analog of 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide, the saturation of the indole ring removes aromaticity and significantly increases the flexibility and changes the 3D shape of the molecule, this structural change dramatically influences its binding mode and affinity for the DNA minor groove, and this compound is investigated to explore the impact of molecular rigidity/flexibility on the activity of DNA-binding drugs, showing potential for anticancer and anti-infective activity.

Small_molecule_information: Trisodium;5-oxo-1-(4-sulfonatophenyl)-4-[(4-sulfonatophenyl)diazenyl]-4H-pyrazole-3-carboxylate, also referred to as Tartrazine (and known as the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110)), is the chemical name for the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110), a synthetic azo dye with the formula C16H10N2Na3O7[(2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-[(2R,3S,4S,5S,6S)-2-[(1R,2S)-3-[[(2R,3S,4S)-5-[[(2S,3R)-1-[2-[4-[4-[3-[4-[[2-[[2-[[2-[acetyl(propyl)amino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-prop-2-ynylamino]acetyl]amino]butylamino]propylcarbamoyl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]ethylamino]-3-hydroxy-1-oxobutan-2-yl]amino]-3-hydroxy-4-methyl-5-oxopentan-2-yl]amino]-2-[[6-amino-2-[(1S)-3-amino-1-[[(2S)-2,3-diamino-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]-5-methylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino]-1-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-3-oxopropoxy]-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-3-yl]oxy-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-4-yl]carbamate, and Tartrazine is a synthetic yellow azo dye used as a food coloring agent while primarily an industrial colorant, its structure features a pyrazolone core linked by an azo bond (NN) to two benzene rings, both substituted with sodium sulfonate groups, and exists as a trisodium salt, the azo structure imparts a vibrant color, in real-world use, Sunset Yellow is widely employed as an orange-yellow coloring agent in foods, beverages, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics, such as orange juice, sodas, candies, and ice cream, to enhance visual appeal, as a common food colorant, its use is strictly regulated globally to ensure safety within prescribed limits, despite some controversy regarding its potential to affect behavior in sensitive individuals, particularly children.

RNA information: The NRQ is a 18-nucleotide RNA oligomer corresponding to the wild-type, guanine-rich sequence (5′-GGGAGGGGCGGGUCUGGG-3′) located in the 5′-UTR of the human NRAS proto-oncogene mRNA. This sequence possesses the intrinsic ability to fold into a stable, parallel RNA G-quadruplex structure, which has been previously established to act as a translational repressor by inhibiting the initiation of NRAS protein synthesis. In this study, it serves as the primary target structure for the novel MSQ derivatives. Its confirmed G-quadruplex formation, characterized by a specific CD spectrum with a positive peak at 262 nm that is enhanced in the presence of potassium ions, and its functional role make it essential for evaluating the binding affinity and specificity of potential ligands designed to stabilize this structure and further repress translation.
Small_molecule_information: N,N-dimethyl-N-[2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]propane-1,3-diamine, also referred to as the derivative featuring a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core with the p-(methylthio)styryl group at the 2-position and an N1,N1-diethylethane-1,2-diamine side chain at the 11-position, possesses the [1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core linked to an N,N-dimethylpropane-1,3-diamine side chain, and it has been found to exhibit specific binding affinity for the NRQ as well as strong anti-proliferative effects in relevant cancer cells; this diamine structure is characteristic of antimalarial and certain anticancer drugs, designed to accumulate in acidic organelles of tumor cells via the ion-trapping mechanism, the long, flexible propylamine spacer and tertiary amine terminus are crucial for optimizing its binding geometry with nucleic acids and biological targets, and this compound is a structural candidate for enhancing antitumor or antiparasitic selectivity.

Small_molecule_information: 1,2,3,4-tetrahydroacridin-9-amine, also referred to as 1,2,3,4-Tetrahydroacridine-9-amine, is a hydrogenated acridine derivative with the acridine core, where the benzene ring is fused with a dihydropyridine ring, and the 1,2,3,4 positions are hydrogenated, with an amino group at position 9, molecular formula C13H16N2; the compounds core is a partially saturated tricyclic acridine structure with some lipophilicity, and the amino group imparts basicity. In terms of real-world activity, such acridine derivatives, due to their unique planar tricyclic structure and amino side chain, are often studied as DNA intercalators, showing potential in anticancer and antibacterial fields; specifically, this compound shares structural similarities with the well-known Alzheimers drug tacrine (an acetylcholinesterase inhibitor), which makes it a derivative of tacrine with potential acetylcholinesterase inhibitory activity, suggesting its potential for Central Nervous System activity as a candidate for acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitors in neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimers disease treatment, and it may also possess topoisomerase inhibitory activity, making it valuable in antitumor drug development.

Small_molecule_information: 5-methylphenazin-1-one, also referred to as pyocyanin, this compound has a Phenazine structure; pyocyanin is a planar azaaromatic phenazine derivative with a fused bicyclic heterocyclic core (two benzene rings linked by two nitrogen atoms) and hydroxyl/methoxy groups, which binds to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) via non-specific interactions but with lower affinity than potent ligands like chelerythrine, fails to activate riboswitch-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis while naturally acting as a virulence factor in Pseudomonas aeruginosa and aiding in studying riboswitch ligand structure-activity relationships, and Phenazine compounds generally possess broad biological activities, including antibacterial, antifungal, and anticancer effects, and may be developed as new antibiotics or anticancer drugs.

Small_molecule_information: 3-(3-chlorophenyl)-5-fluoro-7-(4-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl)chromen-2-one, also referred to as C25, is a cationic coumarin-class molecule with a 5-fluoro-7-piperazinium coumarin backbone, but it carries a meta-chlorophenyl substituent at C3, and is additionally a non-planar coumarin analog equipped with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and an imidazopyridine-thiazole DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge; this change in the position of the chlorine atom is a regioisomeric difference, which subtly alters the molecules electronic distribution, lipophilicity, and steric fit with the binding pocket of biological targets, while the compound can recognize the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatic interactions as verified by FP and SAR data, acting as a selective RNA ligand, and is investigated as a potential enzyme inhibitor or biological probe, particularly targeting those involved in cancer and inflammation, with its cationic nature allowing for effective electrostatic interaction with anionic biomolecules.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a short, genetically conserved stem–bulge–loop hairpin found at the 5′ end of every viral transcript.  Functional constructs range from minimal 29-nt fragments (containing the essential bulge and loop) up to the canonical 52–59-nt forms seen in full-length genomes; in all cases the active fold is the same: a double-stranded stem interrupted by a three-base pyrimidine bulge (commonly UCU) and capped by a six-nucleotide loop.  This bulge-and-loop motif first captures an arginine-rich segment of the viral Tat protein, inducing a local conformational switch that allows Tat to recruit the host Cyclin T1–CDK9 (P-TEFb) complex.  The resultant Tat–TAR–P-TEFb assembly converts RNA polymerase II from abortive to highly processive elongation, boosting viral gene expression >100-fold, and the same RNA structure also facilitates strand-transfer during reverse transcription.  Because the bulge–loop signature is indispensable and sequence-invariant across strains, TAR acts as a natural “riboswitch-like” sensor that tunes the balance between transcription and genome replication, offering a structurally small yet functionally critical target for therapeutic intervention.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(2-chlorophenothiazin-10-yl)-N,N-dimethylpropan-1-amine, also referred to as Chlorpromazine (CPZ; 2-chloro-10-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-phenothiazine), is a typical example of the phenothiazine class and is an important antipsychotic (neuroleptic) medication used clinically to treat schizophrenia, mania, and severe behavioral disorders, it is also commonly used as an antiemetic and sedative, its mechanism of action primarily involves blocking dopamine receptors in the central nervous system, marking it as a historical milestone in psychiatric medicine, and this phenothiazine antipsychotic has also been re-purposed as a small-molecule ligand for the HIV-1 TAR RNA bulge, it preserves the tricyclic phenothiazine chromophore that intercalates between unpaired A22-U25 bases of the bulge while the protonated dimethylaminopropyl side-chain forms electrostatic contacts with the anionic phosphate backbone, it can disrupt the TatTARcyclin T1 complex in electrophoretic mobility-shift assays and dose-dependently inhibit Tat-driven LTR trans-activation without affecting a heterologous Rev-RRE control in HeLa cells, NMR titrations reveal that it induces large chemical-shift changes exclusively in the 5 bulge even at sub-stoichiometric ratios, confirming RNA-specific recognition analogous to FMN-riboswitch binding, thus serving as a scaffold for developing non-nucleotide, non-peptide antivirals that target viral RNA instead of host proteins.

RNA information: Ruminococcus gauvreauii 134yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function. Its structural characteristics and functional mechanisms show significant diversity and specificity in regulating gene expression. It is composed of 134 nucleotides, forming a five-stem ring structure, including five stem ring structures: P1, P2, P3, P4 and P5.
Small_molecule_information: 1,2-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium, also referred to as Chelerythrine, is a methylated benzophenanthridine quaternary alkaloid featuring a rigid planar aromatic system and a permanent positive charge, and it is also a natural nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound that exhibits significant structural and functional properties when interacting with Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA; it can specifically bind to the binding pocket of 108yjdF RNA, inducing significant changes in RNA structure which are highly similar to those when binding to other nitrogen-containing aromatic compounds such as proflavine, indicating that the binding pocket of 108yjdF RNA has a broad recognition ability for such compounds, and it has a relatively high binding affinity for 108yjdF RNA, which indicates that its interaction with RNA is very strong, in addition, it can also activate gene expression regulated by 108yjdF RNA, which further confirms its functional role in gene regulation, and although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, it, as one of the potential ligands, provides important clues for studying the functional mechanism of this riboswitch; in real-world pharmacology, these structures are notorious for their potent DNA intercalation properties, where they insert themselves between base pairs, disrupting DNA structure and function, this mechanism makes them effective cytotoxic agents with powerful anti-tumor and anti-proliferative activity by interfering with DNA replication and Topoisomerase activity, and it has also been reported as an inhibitor of Multidrug Resistance-associated Proteins (MRPs), offering potential value in overcoming cancer resistance mechanisms.

RNA information: XIST is a long non-coding RNA (lncRNA) crucial for X-chromosome inactivation in female mammalian cells. It functions by coating the future inactive X chromosome and recruiting repressive complexes to silence gene expression. Structurally, XIST contains a non-canonical RNA G-quadruplex (rG4) motif composed of only two G-tetrads, making it less stable than canonical three-tetrad rG4s. In vitro studies, such as reverse transcriptase stalling (RTS) assays, confirm its ability to form an rG4 structure, showing approximately 14% stalling in potassium buffer. Notably, specific G4 ligands like PhenDC3 and PDS can act as molecular chaperones, significantly enhancing the formation and stability of the XIST rG4 structure under unfavorable ionic conditions, highlighting its dynamic nature and regulatory potential.
Small_molecule_information: 4,16-difluoro-8,12,20-trimethyl-8-aza-20-azoniapentacyclo[11.7.1.0{2,7}.0{9,21}.0{14,19}]henicosa-1(20),2(7),3,5,9(21),10,12,14(19),15,17-decaene, also referred to as PhenDC3, a bisquinolinium G4 ligand (a pyridodicarboxamide derivative), is a highly complex and rigid pentacyclic fused nitrogen-containing heterocyclic quaternary ammonium salt that binds rG4s via stacking on G-quartets, whose structure enables chemical derivatization (e.g., PhenDC3-alk for bioorthogonal manipulations), functionally acts as a molecular chaperone inducing folding of canonical (3-quartet, e.g., TERRA) and non-canonical (2-quartet, e.g., XIST) rG4s, stabilizes rG4s and impedes reverse transcriptase by promoting rG4 formation to aid G4 biology studies, the chemical name clearly outlines a twenty-one-carbon skeleton composed of multiple bridged and fused rings, the two fluorine atoms, strategically positioned at the 4 and 16 positions, are common bioisosteres used to enhance metabolic stability, lipophilicity, and binding affinity to biological targets, the presence of a quaternary ammonium group imparts a positive charge at physiological pH, enabling strong electrostatic interactions with negatively charged biomolecules such as DNA or acidic residues on proteins, and this rigid polycyclic scaffold and its specific three-dimensional structure suggest its potential application as a novel anti-tumor or antiviral agent, possibly functioning as a topoisomerase inhibitor or DNA intercalator in real-world drug discovery.

RNA information: cTNT (cardiac troponin T) pre-mRNA is a splicing reporter RNA that contains an alternatively spliced exon 5. It is regulated by the RNA-binding protein MBNL1, which binds upstream of exon 5 and represses its inclusion. In myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1), the toxic r(CUG)exp RNA sequesters MBNL1, leading to increased exon 5 inclusion in cTNT mRNA. Thus, cTNT splicing serves as a functional readout for MBNL1 activity and DM1-associated splicing defects in cellular models .

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (2S)-7-fluoro-2-methyl-6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-10-oxo-4-oxa-1-azatricyclo[7.3.1.05,13]trideca-5(13),6,8,11-tetraene-11-carboxylic acid is a derivative of the fluoroquinolone class of antibiotics. Its structure, featuring a fluorine atom at position 7, a carboxylic acid group, and a piperazine ring, is characteristic of broad-spectrum synthetic antibacterial agents. These drugs function by inhibiting bacterial DNA gyrase and Topoisomerase IV, thereby blocking bacterial DNA replication and repair. This compound is a broad-spectrum antibacterial drug candidate used for treating complex infections caused by both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

Small_molecule_information: (7S,9S)-7-[(2R,4S,5R,6S)-4-amino-5-hydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxy-6,9,11-trihydroxy-9-(2-hydroxyacetyl)-4-methoxy-8,10-dihydro-7H-tetracene-5,12-dione, also referred to as Epirubicin, is the chemical name for, or a very close analogue of, the anthracycline antibiotic Epirubicin, which is an anthracycline chemotherapeutic agent that intercalates into DNA, inhibiting topoisomerase II and causing DNA strand breaks; Epirubicin is a widely used chemotherapy drug in clinical practice for treating various cancers such as breast cancer and ovarian cancer, and its mechanism involves DNA intercalation and Topoisomerase II inhibition.

RNA information: 5′GAG/3′C_C: This motif represents a small asymmetric internal loop, characterized by a GAG sequence opposite two nucleotides with a bulge. It was found to bind RNA-targeted small molecules selectively in combinatorial screening. Although direct antiviral activity linked to this motif was not detailed, its enrichment in binding assays indicates its potential as a privileged RNA element for selective ligand recognition.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[3-[[2,5-bis[[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]carbamoyl]phenyl]carbamoylamino]phenyl]carbamoylamino]-1-N,4-N-bis[4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]benzene-1,4-dicarboxamide, also referred to as Compound 7, is an extremely large, highly branched polycationic compound, and it can selectively bind internal loop motifs such as 5UUA/3A_U and 5UGU/3G_A with submicromolar affinity, with structural studies revealing that its binding is driven by recognition of asymmetric bulges within RNA stems, it can directly interact with HCV 3 UTR stemloops I and II by targeting motifs present in viral RNA, functional assays have demonstrated that it inhibits HCV replication in cell culture without affecting host ribosomal RNAs, and RNase H/ASO mapping has confirmed its intracellular engagement of the predicted binding sites; its intricate scaffold contains multiple phenyl rings, amide bonds, and urea groups, terminated by several 4-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl substituents, structural analysis indicates that this molecule bears at least six imidazolinyl groups, making it a potent hexacationic system, this structural design is an extreme example used to construct supramolecular nucleic acid binders, where the molecular length, rigidity, and multiple charges enable high-affinity interactions with multiple sites on the DNA helix, including the minor groove, major groove, or phosphate backbone, and such compounds are often investigated as non-viral gene delivery vectors (by charge-neutralizing DNA), highly effective anticancer agents, or for use as DNA structural probes, aiming for extremely high binding stability and cytotoxicity.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: Trisodium;5-oxo-1-(4-sulfonatophenyl)-4-[(4-sulfonatophenyl)diazenyl]-4H-pyrazole-3-carboxylate, also referred to as Tartrazine (and known as the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110)), is the chemical name for the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110), a synthetic azo dye with the formula C16H10N2Na3O7[(2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-[(2R,3S,4S,5S,6S)-2-[(1R,2S)-3-[[(2R,3S,4S)-5-[[(2S,3R)-1-[2-[4-[4-[3-[4-[[2-[[2-[[2-[acetyl(propyl)amino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-prop-2-ynylamino]acetyl]amino]butylamino]propylcarbamoyl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]ethylamino]-3-hydroxy-1-oxobutan-2-yl]amino]-3-hydroxy-4-methyl-5-oxopentan-2-yl]amino]-2-[[6-amino-2-[(1S)-3-amino-1-[[(2S)-2,3-diamino-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]-5-methylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino]-1-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-3-oxopropoxy]-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-3-yl]oxy-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-4-yl]carbamate, and Tartrazine is a synthetic yellow azo dye used as a food coloring agent while primarily an industrial colorant, its structure features a pyrazolone core linked by an azo bond (NN) to two benzene rings, both substituted with sodium sulfonate groups, and exists as a trisodium salt, the azo structure imparts a vibrant color, in real-world use, Sunset Yellow is widely employed as an orange-yellow coloring agent in foods, beverages, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics, such as orange juice, sodas, candies, and ice cream, to enhance visual appeal, as a common food colorant, its use is strictly regulated globally to ensure safety within prescribed limits, despite some controversy regarding its potential to affect behavior in sensitive individuals, particularly children.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 7,8,10-trimethylbenzo[g]pteridine-2,4-dione, also referred to as Lumiflavin, is a benzo[g]pteridine (isoalloxazine) derivative and a synthetic flavin analog whose structure is similar to that of natural FMN (flavin mononucleotide) but lacks the ribityl chain and phosphate group, with the core scaffold of riboflavin (Vitamin B2) as its structure: the molecule is substituted with methyl groups at positions 7, 8, and 10, representing the fundamental structure of the flavin family, with the molecular formula CHNO; this simplified structure results in it exhibiting low affinity when binding to the FMN riboswitch, and further evidence from transcription termination assays confirms its limited ability to regulate gene expression, nevertheless, research on it provides an important structural basis for understanding the interaction between flavin analogs and riboswitches, and offers a reference for the development of more effective antibacterial drugs, and it is the basis for Flavin Adenine Dinucleotide (FAD) and Flavin Mononucleotide (FMN), two coenzymes that play a critical role in oxidation-reduction reactions in living organisms.

RNA information: The NRQ is a 18-nucleotide RNA oligomer corresponding to the wild-type, guanine-rich sequence (5′-GGGAGGGGCGGGUCUGGG-3′) located in the 5′-UTR of the human NRAS proto-oncogene mRNA. This sequence possesses the intrinsic ability to fold into a stable, parallel RNA G-quadruplex structure, which has been previously established to act as a translational repressor by inhibiting the initiation of NRAS protein synthesis. In this study, it serves as the primary target structure for the novel MSQ derivatives. Its confirmed G-quadruplex formation, characterized by a specific CD spectrum with a positive peak at 262 nm that is enhanced in the presence of potassium ions, and its functional role make it essential for evaluating the binding affinity and specificity of potential ligands designed to stabilize this structure and further repress translation.

RNA information: 5'CUG/3'CUG is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it folds into an A-form hairpin with characteristic UU mismatches, stabilized by base-pairing. Functionally, it sequesters Muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, disrupting pre-mRNA alternative splicing, which is the core gain-of-function mechanism underlying DM1 pathogenesis.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[[11-[2-(dimethylamino)ethylamino]-5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]ethyl-trimethylazanium diiodide, this compound is a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with long diamine and quaternary ammonium salt side chains.
