RNA information: The HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating stem-loop (FSS) RNA is a highly conserved structured element located within the gag-pol transcript that regulates production of the essential Gag-Pol polyprotein through a −1 ribosomal frameshift. It consists of a stable stem-loop positioned downstream of a slippery sequence that induces ribosomal pausing and promotes frameshifting in about 5–10% of translation events. This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Altering FSS activity disrupts the Gag to Gag-Pol ratio, substantially reducing viral replication and infectivity.
Small_molecule_information: (E,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-2-(propylamino)propanoyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 7 (2012), is a symmetric oligo-peptide analogue featuring multiple stereocenters and an internal (E)-alkene double bond within its oct-4-enediamide core, and it is the E-isomer analog of compound 6 that lacks the benzo[g]quinoline group; this type of peptidic structure, incorporating non-natural amino acid fragments and a geometrically constrained core, is typically pursued as a candidate for proteinase inhibitor or a G-protein coupled receptor (GPCR) ligand, while the absence of the aromatic moiety in this compound (relative to related structures) leads to only modest binding and a complete loss of selectivity among different RNA sequences, and structurally its configuration affects backbone orientation but does not compensate for the missing heteroaromatic system, functionally it shows minimal or no activity in biological assays (reinforcing the observation that effective RNA recognition requires both the aromatic stacking element and appropriate stereochemistry) and serves primarily as a comparative tool in evaluating structurefunction dependencies, meanwhile the rigid E-configuration (trans) of the alkene is critical for locking the molecule into a specific conformation, which is paramount for achieving the necessary molecular recognition and high-affinity binding at the proteins active site, and due to its multiple basic amine groups, the compound is likely polycationic at physiological pH, suggesting potential use in areas like antiviral, anti-inflammatory, or immunomodulatory therapies.

RNA information: The r(CCUG)8 RNA forms an A-form structure with consecutive CU mismatches, sequestering MBNL1 protein, leading to splicing defects and DM2 disease symptoms.
Small_molecule_information: 1-N,3-N-bis[4-(2,4,6-triaminopyrimidin-5-yl)butyl]benzene-1,3-dicarboximidamide, also referred to as Bisamidinium 11, which exhibits moderate inhibition potency and still effectively disrupts MBNL1-r(CCUG) foci in DM2 model cells while maintaining low cellular toxicity indicating good biocompatibility, possesses a sophisticated conformation, combining the non-linear, V-shaped structure of the benzene-1,3-dicarboximidamide core with the flexibility of two longer four-carbon butane chains, which connect the meta-imidamide groups to the terminal 2,4,6-triaminopyrimidine rings. The combination of the longer chain and meta-substitution provides high flexibility and conformational diversity, beneficial for binding to biological targets with specific geometric requirements. The introduction of the butane chain also increases the molecules lipophilicity, potentially aiding cell membrane penetration. As a polycationic ligand with multiple basic imidamide and triaminopyrimidine groups, its research is typically focused on cell membrane interactions or selective nucleic acid binding. In medicinal chemistry, molecules with this structural pattern are considered excellent candidates for broad-spectrum antibacterial agents and cell-penetrating probes.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (2S)-7-fluoro-2-methyl-6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-10-oxo-4-oxa-1-azatricyclo[7.3.1.05,13]trideca-5(13),6,8,11-tetraene-11-carboxylic acid is a derivative of the fluoroquinolone class of antibiotics. Its structure, featuring a fluorine atom at position 7, a carboxylic acid group, and a piperazine ring, is characteristic of broad-spectrum synthetic antibacterial agents. These drugs function by inhibiting bacterial DNA gyrase and Topoisomerase IV, thereby blocking bacterial DNA replication and repair. This compound is a broad-spectrum antibacterial drug candidate used for treating complex infections caused by both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

RNA information: The miR-96 precursor( pri-miR-96) is a structured RNA hairpin that is specifically recognized by a benzimidazole small molecule (compound 1) identified via the Inforna pipeline. Binding occurs at the single-stranded Drosha-processing site within the precursor, thereby blocking its cleavage and reducing mature miR-96 production. This selective interaction relieves miR-96-mediated repression of the tumor-suppressor FOXO1 mRNA, restoring cellular FMN/riboflavin-like homeostasis by rebalancing oncogenic miRNA levels and apoptotic control in cancer cells.; The pri-miR-96 transcript features a critical hairpin structure containing a Drosha processing site. Its key functional element is a UU internal loop and a nearby GG internal loop within this hairpin, which serve as the binding site for the small molecule Targaprimir-96. Binding of the molecule to this specific structural pocket inhibits Drosha cleavage, thereby blocking the biogenesis of mature oncogenic miR-96. This inhibition leads to the upregulation of the FOXO1 protein and induces apoptosis in cancer cells, highlighting the direct link between its specific secondary structure and its oncogenic function.
Small_molecule_information: [(2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-[(2R,3S,4S,5S,6S)-2-[(1R,2S)-2-[[6-amino-2-[(1S)-3-amino-1-[[(2S)-2,3-diamino-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]-5-methylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino]-3-[[(2S,3S,4R)-5-[[(2S,3R)-1-[2-[4-[4-[3-[4-[[2-[[2-[[2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoyl-propylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-[[1-[3-[4-[2,6-ditert-butyl-4-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]propyl]triazol-4-yl]methyl]amino]acetyl]amino]butylamino]propylcarbamoyl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]ethylamino]-3-hydroxy-1-oxobutan-2-yl]amino]-3-hydroxy-2-methyl-5-oxopentan-4-yl]amino]-1-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-3-oxopropoxy]-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-3-yl]oxy-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-4-yl]carbamate, also referred to as Compound 2, is a hybrid molecule of extreme structural complexity, representing the pinnacle of multi-functional drug design, and Compound 2 is a chimeric molecule composed of the RNA-binding small molecule Targapremir-96 (Compound 1) covalently conjugated to the cytotoxic natural product bleomycin A5, its structure incorporates at least three main functional domains: first, a glycosyl or nucleotide derivative part (with multiple oxan rings and hydroxy groups), likely to enhance cell permeability or target specific membrane receptors, second, a benzimidazole-DNA targeting group, where one benzimidazole moiety is modified with a 2,6-di-tert-butylphenoxy group, introducing significant steric bulk and lipophilicity, third, a long and complex peptidomimetic/heterocyclic (pyrimidine, thiazole) linker arm connects the various active units, and this conjugate is designed to selectively bind the primary microRNA-96 (pri-miR-96) hairpin precursor with high affinity and, upon Fe activation, cleave the RNA at adjacent AU pairs near the Drosha processing site, this highly customized molecule is designed for multiple targeting and high specificity, with potential activity in anti-cancer drug development, acting through synergistic mechanisms such as receptor binding, nucleic acid interference, and metabolic pathway modulation, and in triple-negative breast cancer (MDA-MB-231) cells, it induces precise degradation of pri-miR-96, leading to downstream upregulation of the pro-apoptotic protein FOXO1, caspase activation, and selective apoptosis, without affecting healthy breast epithelial cells (MCF-10A), while its DNA cleavage activity and associated DNA damage are significantly attenuated compared to the control Compound 3.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[5-[4-[N-(3-azidopropyl)carbamimidoyl]phenyl]furan-2-yl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as AU-azide, is a highly functionalized probe molecule with the molecular formula C24H23N7O, and AU-azide is a derivative of Compound 2 with an azide group for conjugation; it exhibits even higher AU selectivity and is used to build multivalent compounds like dimers, its core is a planar diaryl-furan (biphenyl-furan) system, which provides hydrophobicity and - stacking ability, the molecule contains two key functional groups: carboximidamide and azido, the carboximidamide group (-C(NH)NH2) is typically positively charged at physiological pH, allowing it to interact strongly with negatively charged biomolecules like DNA; thus, amidine derivatives are often used as DNA minor groove binders with anti-tumor or antimicrobial activity, the azido group (-N3) is a prominent bio-orthogonal chemistry functional group, specifically a core component of Click Chemistry, which permits the molecule to be covalently linked to alkyne-containing partners under specific conditions, therefore, this compound is designed as a bifunctional molecular probe for target identification and bio-orthogonal chemical labeling.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 9-ethyl-3-[(E)-2-[1-methyl-4-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)quinolin-1-ium-2-yl]ethenyl]carbazole, also referred to as Compound 24, is a green-yellow 1-methylquinolinium derivative linking carbazole and quinoline quaternary ammonium salt via a vinyl group, which has a 2-(2-(9-ethyl-9H-carbazol-3-yl)vinyl) group at the quinoline 2-position and a 4-methylpiperazin-1-yl group at the 4-position; its large -conjugated system imparts excellent fluorescence, making it useful as a cationic stain, biological probe, or for OLED materials, and it has been confirmed by SPR and MST to have high affinity for A10-RNA-Wt, can enhance ADAM10 translation in a concentration-dependent manner, boost sAPP secretion, reduce A, and exhibits certain cytotoxicity towards HeLa cells.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 3-chloro-N-[2-[4-[4-[2-(diethylamino)ethoxy]anilino]quinazolin-2-yl]phenyl]propanamide, also referred to as a12, is a quinazoline derivative that includes a 3-chloropropanamide group, suggesting its potential use as a covalent or irreversible enzyme inhibitor; while a12 is only confirmed to bind G-quadruplex sequences in vitro and its screening target hVEGF-A is closely associated with tumor angiogenesis, this compound merely serves as a member of the screening library for potential anti-tumor candidates with no further functional or disease-related validation, and is a candidate for targeted anticancer or anti-inflammatory drugs.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[11-[3-(diethylamino)propylamino]-5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]-N-[3-(dimethylamino)propyl]acetamide iodide is a benzofuroquinoline ([1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline) quaternary ammonium salt derivative. Its structure is based on the benzofuroquinoline scaffold, with the nitrogen at the 5-position quaternized, and long amine-containing side chains attached at the 11- and 7-positions (molecular formula C32H43N5O2I). This compound possesses a planar conjugated system, multiple amine groups, and a positive charge. It is a potential anti-cancer agent known for its DNA or RNA binding properties, with some benzofuroquinoline analogs studied as CDK2/Topoisomerase I dual inhibitors.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(1H-imidazol-5-yl)-2-[[2-[[2-[(5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl)amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]propanoic acid. This is a tripeptide-heterocycle conjugate containing a histidine residue, linked to the cationic benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium scaffold. The imidazole ring of the histidine residue imparts a unique pH-responsiveness, which is significant in biological systems. The practical application potential of this compound lies in utilizing the histidine property to develop pH-sensitive drug delivery systems or diagnostic probes. Due to the lower pH of many tumor environments and lysosomes, the compound may be designed to undergo structural change or release the active moiety under acidic conditions, thus achieving targeting of the tumor microenvironment, representing an advanced concept in chemotherapeutic drug design.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GAC)x6: This RNA contains six consecutive CAG repeats with AA mismatches, forming multiple internal loops. It is a key target for small molecules due to its structural similarity to pathogenic expanded repeats. It shows high affinity and selective binding to compounds such as pyridocoumarin derivatives, leading to conformational changes and thermal stabilization.; The (5'CAG/3'GAC)x6 RNA contains multiple consecutive A-A mismatch loops. This multi-loop structure enhances conformational stability and ligand binding avidity, more accurately mimicking the expanded toxic repeats found in patient-derived RNA and providing a robust platform for biophysical testing.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-(4-methoxybenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 3, shares the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one core with 3-amino-9-bromo-2-(4-methoxybenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one but lacks the bromine atom, and Compound 3 emerged as a lead compound with high affinity and selectivity for r(CAG)exp RNA, its large aromatic and amino-substituted structure suggests high lipophilicity and the potential to interact strongly with biological targets, it is likely investigated as an antiproliferative agent targeting intracellular enzymes or signaling pathways, Compound 3 significantly reduced polyQ aggregation in cellular models, showed low cytotoxicity, and was cell-permeable, localizing primarily in the cytoplasm. RNA

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-(2-aminoethyl)-2-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-5-methoxyphenyl]ethanamine, also referred to as Compound 3 (a tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) biphenyl compound with side chains occupying the bilateral 2,6 and 2,6 positions), is a complex polyamine structure containing four primary amine groups (ethanamine) that is synthesized via palladium-catalyzed Suzuki coupling reactions and was characterized by methods like NMR and SPR; at physiological pH, these primary amines will be protonated to form a tetracationic molecule with extremely high positive charge density, polyamine analogs mimic the action of natural polyamines (like spermine) in cellulo, functionally, this highly charged structure is extensively studied as a non-viral gene carrier or polyamine transfection agent, forming electrostatic complexes with negatively charged DNA or siRNA to aid their entry into cells, while it can also bind to the hairpin RNA (Bh) of the HIV-1 RRE subdomain IIB, and similar to Compound 1, it induces chemical shift perturbations in stem nucleotides outside the loop, with its binding affinity to Bh being very low, having limited specificity, and exhibiting no activity in inhibiting HIV-1 replication in cellular assays, and key to its activity is the length and spacing of the polyamine chains and the overall nucleic acid affinity.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(2-chlorophenyl)quinolin-3-ol, also referred to as CP7, is a quinoline derivative (and a hydroxyquinoline derivative with a 2-chlorophenyl substitution) that has a classic intercalator motif which provided RNA binding capability, though its cellular efficacy was not sufficiently highlighted for further progression; the quinoline scaffold of this compound is an important nitrogen-containing aromatic heterocyclic system, very common in natural products and drug molecules, possessing broad biological activity, and its structural features are the hydroxyl group (OH) at the 3-position and the 2-chlorophenyl substituent at the 2-position, with 3-hydroxyquinoline as the core structure of many fluorescent dyes or metal chelating agents because the hydroxyl group and the quinoline nitrogen can form strong intramolecular hydrogen bonds and are capable of forming stable complexes with metal ions (e.g., aluminum, zinc, magnesium), while the 2-(2-chlorophenyl) substituent increases the molecules steric hindrance and lipophilicity, and in real-world applications, such compounds are frequently studied as fluorescent probes, particularly for detecting and imaging metal ions within cells, additionally, its structure bears resemblance to some potential anticancer or anti-inflammatory drugs.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-7-(pyrrolidin-1-ium-3-ylamino)chromen-2-one, also referred to as SMSM28, is a non-planar coumarin analog bearing a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and an imidazopyridine DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge, which has the coumarin core, with the 8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl group at C3, the C7 position is substituted with the pyrrolidin-1-ium-3-ylamino group, connected by an amine linker, pyrrolidine is a five-membered saturated nitrogen heterocycle that, when quaternized, forms a compact cation, compared to six-membered rings, pyrrolidine offers a smaller, more rigid cationic structure, this small cationic side chain modification is frequently used to fine-tune the molecules membrane permeability and binding specificity, it selectively recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1, with the binding mediated by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking interactions, and electrostatic attraction, and its selectivity has been corroborated through structureactivity relationship (SAR) comparisons, since pyrrolidine derivatives are common in neuropsychiatric drugs, it might be a potential drug candidate targeting CNS receptors, leveraging its fluorescence for high-throughput screening.

RNA information: 5'CGG/3'GGC is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it forms a hairpin with a 1×1 internal loop, closed by CG pairs, adopting non-canonical folding. Functionally, it contributes to FXTAS pathogenesis, likely by sequestering cellular proteins and disrupting normal splicing. As a disease-relevant RNA, it serves as a target for screening therapeutic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: N-[(E)-[10-[(E)-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-ylhydrazinylidene)methyl]anthracen-9-yl]methylideneamino]-4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-amine, also referred to as Bisantrene, is a complex anthracene derivative with known antitumor relevance, where the anthracene ring (three linearly fused benzene rings) is substituted at the 9- and 10-positions with hydrazone/imine-linked 4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazole (imidazolinium) groups; anthracene is a well-known fluorescent chromophore, making this compound highly likely to exhibit strong fluorescence, and the molecule contains two imidazolinium groups and a conjugated hydrazone-imine structure, elements that facilitate binding to biomolecules like DNA, consistent with its use as a DNA-intercalating topoisomerase IIrelated agent, and such compounds are typically designed as DNA-binding fluorescent probes or potential anticancer agents

RNA information: NRQ mutant is a G-base mutant of NRQ that prevents the formation of the G4 structure.
Small_molecule_information: 5-methyl-N-[2-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)ethyl]-2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-10H-indolo[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-amine iodide, also referred to as the N5-methylated analogue of 4c-5 (indoloquinoline core), is an indolo[3,2-b]quinoline quaternary ammonium salt featuring an N-methylpiperazine side chain, and this compound is the N5-methylated analogue of 4c-5 (indoloquinoline core), the piperazine group is included to optimize pharmacokinetic properties such as solubility and target affinity, it is a highly potent candidate for antitumor research due to its cationic and planar nature, this compound showed notable affinity for the NRQ, it showed moderate anti-proliferative activity with notable activity in A375 cells and was ineffective in HeLa cells at the tested concentration.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: N,N-diethyl-N-[5-methyl-7-[2-(1-methylpiperidin-1-ium-1-yl)ethoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl]ethane-1,2-diamine diiodide is a dicationic benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative where the second positive charge is on an N-methyl piperidinium ring. This structure is often utilized to develop high-selectivity G-quadruplex DNA stabilizers or antiviral agents.

RNA information: The r(CAG)₁₀ sequence serves as a comparative motif to test ligand selectivity against repeat RNAs with A–A mismatches instead of U–U mismatches. Structurally, it forms A-form helical RNA with alternating positive and negative electrostatic stripes similar to CUG repeats, leading to comparable overall architectures. Functionally, r(CAG)₁₀ is not associated with myotonic dystrophy but provides a stringent control for assessing molecular recognition. Because many proteins and synthetic ligands show similar affinity toward both CUG and CAG repeats, this sequence demonstrates the challenges of engineering true sequence selectivity in RNA-targeted small-molecule design.
Small_molecule_information: (E,2S,7S)-2,7-bis[[(2S)-6-amino-2-[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]hexanoyl]amino]-N-[(2S)-1-(2-aminoethylamino)-1-oxopropan-2-yl]-N-[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxopropan-2-yl]-N,N-dipropyloct-4-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 9 (2012), is a highly complex asymmetric peptidomimetic bearing the benzo[g]quinoline moiety; while benzo[g]quinoline is a necessary aromatic element for relevant interactions, DCC 9 (2012) has an altered peptide framework that disrupts the orientation required for strong and selective recognition, resulting in a weaker interaction profile, limited ability to discriminate among target sequences, and minimal biological effects, which confirms that benzo[g]quinoline is necessary but not sufficient and that correct positioning within the molecular scaffold is equally critical, highlighting its role as a negative control and supporting evidence for the structureactivity relationship developed across the compound series. The molecules core eight-carbon chain contains a trans (E) double bond, and its most significant feature is the unequal length of the polyamine chains attached to the two termini: one is a 3-aminopropylamine (three carbons) and the other is a 2-aminoethylamine (two carbons); this asymmetric polyamine structure is designed to potentially cause differences in binding affinity or mode with biological targets, and the molecule is symmetrically linked to benzo[g]quinoline-lysine derivatives on both sides, integrating DNA intercalation capability with polycationic electrostatic binding, with this design combining asymmetry and dual-targeting groups aiming to investigate how regulating asymmetric conformation can enhance the selectivity and multi-action efficacy against specific biological targets, holding potential application in multi-target anti-cancer therapy.

RNA information: pre-miR-21 is a stem-loop non-coding RNA precursor with a distinct structure, containing G28-A29 (Dicer-TRBP cleavage site) in the stem and single-stranded bulges/apical loops (small-molecule binding pockets). It processes into oncogenic mature miR-21; ligands like 4a or bPGN  bind specifically, inhibiting Dicer processing, upregulating tumor suppressors (PDCD4, PTEN), and suppressing cancer cell proliferation. It is a druggable target for cancers such as colorectal cancer.
Small_molecule_information: N-(2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridin-6-ylmethyl)aniline, also referred to as the compound that shows moderate binding affinity with unspecified functional activity in Dicer inhibition assays, consists of the 2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine core linked via a methylene bridge to an aniline group; aniline is an aromatic amine where the nitrogen is directly attached to the benzene ring, which lowers the amines basicity, and aniline structures and their derivatives are crucial pharmacophores in many kinase inhibitors and anti-inflammatory drugs, making this molecule a likely candidate for a kinase inhibitor or a signaling pathway modulator, as many successful anticancer drugs utilize aniline-substituted aromatic systems to interact with the ATP-binding site of kinases

RNA information: The HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating stem-loop (FSS) RNA is a highly conserved structured element located within the gag-pol transcript that regulates production of the essential Gag-Pol polyprotein through a −1 ribosomal frameshift. It consists of a stable stem-loop positioned downstream of a slippery sequence that induces ribosomal pausing and promotes frameshifting in about 5–10% of translation events. This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Altering FSS activity disrupts the Gag to Gag-Pol ratio, substantially reducing viral replication and infectivity.
Small_molecule_information: N-[(2S)-1-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-4-[4-[(2S)-3-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-2-[(2-ethylquinoline-3-carbonyl)-methylamino]-3-oxopropyl]triazol-1-yl]-1-oxobutan-2-yl]-2-ethyl-N-methylquinoline-3-carboxamide, also referred to as Compound 4, is a complex, linear peptidic macromolecule which is a lower molecular weight analog retaining the triazole core, designed to improve ligand efficiency while preserving HIV-1 FSS RNA recognition, binds the FSS with sub-micromolar affinity despite simplification of structure compared to earlier leads, reduces viral production in T-cell infection assays although with slightly reduced potency relative to compound 3 and shows notable metabolic effects in cytotoxicity assays suggesting some off-target influence, demonstrating that substantial structural streamlining is possible while maintaining RNA-targeting activity, and its structure contains multiple chiral amino acid residues (e.g., Pro, Phe derivatives), a triazole linking unit, and a quinoline-3-carboxamide group. In terms of biological function, compounds with such complex structures are usually inhibitors or ligands targeting specific biological targets, potentially exhibiting anticancer, antiviral, or signal pathway regulatory activities.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 7-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-8-[(2S,3R,4R,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-2-yl]chromen-4-one, also referred to as Genistin (another isoflavone glycoside in this category is Puerarin, which is found in kudzu root), is a glycoside derivative of Genistein (7-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one), known as Genistin, which is an isoflavone glycoside with the formula C21H20O9; Puerarin exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective effects, improves blood circulation and is studied for cardiovascular and metabolic diseases, while Genistins structure features the Genistein backbone with a glucose molecule (or glucoside moiety, the oxan-2-yl part) attached via a C-C bond at the C8 position, with the specific stereochemistry of the sugar being characteristic of its natural form, and it is the most abundant isoflavone compound found in soybeans, where it predominantly exists in this glycosylated form; in biology, most natural flavonoids exist as glycosides, which typically means they have higher water solubility but lower inherent bioactivity, requiring hydrolysis by gut microbes or intracellular -glucosidases to release the more active aglycone form, Genistein (7-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one), thus, Genistin is often considered a prodrug form of Genistein, sharing similar health benefits such as phytoestrogenic, antioxidant, and potential anticancer activities, but with a different pharmacokinetic profile.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 1-[(2R,3R,4S,5R)-3,4-dihydroxy-5-(phosphonooxymethyl)oxolan-2-yl]pyridin-1-ium-3-carboxylate is a zwitterionic form of Nicotinic Acid Mononucleotide (NaMN), also referred to as Nicotinic acid mononucleotide (NAMN), which is a precursor to Nicotinamide Mononucleotide (NMN), with the formula C11H14NO10P, it is a non-azaaromatic nucleotide that lacks planar azaaromatic features and binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, its core structure is composed of a nicotinic acid moiety (pyridine ring attached to the sugar at N1 and a carboxylate at 3) linked to a ribose phosphate group (a D-ribose ring with a phosphate group at the 5 position, which is consistent with the structure of a nicotinic acid base, a ribose sugar, and one phosphate group linked to the riboses 5-carbon), this molecule is a vital metabolic intermediate found in all living cells, playing a critical role in cellular energy metabolism, functionally, NaMN (also NAMN) is a key intermediate in NAD biosynthesis as well as a step in the synthesis of Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide (NAD), cells convert it to nicotinic acid adenine dinucleotide (NAAD) first, then to NAD, supporting essential redox reactions, and NAD is a necessary coenzyme for hundreds of enzymatic reactions crucial for cell health, energy production, and DNA repair, in recent years, molecules of this class have gained significant attention for their potential in anti-aging research, as they are considered potential agents for boosting cellular NAD levels, improving metabolic function, and potentially slowing down the aging process.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (7S,9S)-7-[(2R,4S,5R,6S)-4-amino-5-hydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxy-6,9,11-trihydroxy-9-(2-hydroxyacetyl)-4-methoxy-8,10-dihydro-7H-tetracene-5,12-dione, also referred to as Epirubicin, is the chemical name for, or a very close analogue of, the anthracycline antibiotic Epirubicin, which is an anthracycline chemotherapeutic agent that intercalates into DNA, inhibiting topoisomerase II and causing DNA strand breaks; Epirubicin is a widely used chemotherapy drug in clinical practice for treating various cancers such as breast cancer and ovarian cancer, and its mechanism involves DNA intercalation and Topoisomerase II inhibition.

RNA information: NRQ mutant is a G-base mutant of NRQ that prevents the formation of the G4 structure.
Small_molecule_information: 5-methyl-2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-N-(2-pyrrolidin-1-ylethyl)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-amine iodide, also referred to as the N5-methylated quaternary salt analogue of 4a-10, is a quaternary ammonium salt built upon a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core, and this compound is the N5-methylated quaternary salt analogue of 4a-10, similar to other large planar cationic molecules, it is expected to exhibit strong DNA binding/intercalation and potential topoisomerase inhibition, making it a target for anticancer drug development, the N5-methylated analogue of 4a-10 showed notable affinity for the NRQ, consistent with other methylated derivatives, it showed poor selectivity, it exhibited notable activity in A375 cells.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[2-[[(2S)-2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoyl-propylamino]propanoyl]amino]ethyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-1NPr, is a structurally complex multi-linked benzimidazole derivative (2H-1NPr is a lysine-based tertiary amide with one N-propyl spacer) belonging to the classic class of DNA minor groove binders. The core active unit is the bis-benzimidazole scaffold, which has high planarity and electron conjugation, making it an ideal structure for interaction with the DNA minor groove and often used in fluorescent probes (like Hoechst dyes). The 4-methylpiperazine group attached to the benzimidazole provides basicity and an additional cationic center, further enhancing the molecules affinity for DNA, while 2H-1NPr showed moderate activity in TR-FRET assays with potency better than very short polyamine analogues but not outstanding compared with optimized scaffolds, and its activity was not strongly emphasized in cellular assays suggesting limited functional rescue, contributing to the structureactivity study by showing that a short NPr length offers measurable but not optimal RNAprotein displacement. This compound links two bis-benzimidazole units in a tandem, asymmetric manner via a peptidomimetic-butyramide-phenoxy linker arm; this multi-linked structure is designed to increase the binding affinity for DNA and enhance cell penetration, therefore, it is highly likely a potent DNA minor groove binder with potential real-world activity as an antitumor, antiviral, or biological fluorescent probe.

Small_molecule_information: Trisodium;5-oxo-1-(4-sulfonatophenyl)-4-[(4-sulfonatophenyl)diazenyl]-4H-pyrazole-3-carboxylate, also referred to as Tartrazine (and known as the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110)), is the chemical name for the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110), a synthetic azo dye with the formula C16H10N2Na3O7[(2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-[(2R,3S,4S,5S,6S)-2-[(1R,2S)-3-[[(2R,3S,4S)-5-[[(2S,3R)-1-[2-[4-[4-[3-[4-[[2-[[2-[[2-[acetyl(propyl)amino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-prop-2-ynylamino]acetyl]amino]butylamino]propylcarbamoyl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]ethylamino]-3-hydroxy-1-oxobutan-2-yl]amino]-3-hydroxy-4-methyl-5-oxopentan-2-yl]amino]-2-[[6-amino-2-[(1S)-3-amino-1-[[(2S)-2,3-diamino-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]-5-methylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino]-1-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-3-oxopropoxy]-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-3-yl]oxy-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-4-yl]carbamate, and Tartrazine is a synthetic yellow azo dye used as a food coloring agent while primarily an industrial colorant, its structure features a pyrazolone core linked by an azo bond (NN) to two benzene rings, both substituted with sodium sulfonate groups, and exists as a trisodium salt, the azo structure imparts a vibrant color, in real-world use, Sunset Yellow is widely employed as an orange-yellow coloring agent in foods, beverages, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics, such as orange juice, sodas, candies, and ice cream, to enhance visual appeal, as a common food colorant, its use is strictly regulated globally to ensure safety within prescribed limits, despite some controversy regarding its potential to affect behavior in sensitive individuals, particularly children.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-N,6-dimethyl-N-[3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl]thieno[2,3-b]pyridine-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Compound 9, is a thienopyridine carboxamide derivative with an N-methylated amide group, modified from initial active analogs targeting HIV-1 RNAs, which binds HIV-1 TAR RNA and RRE RNA key for viral replication, targeting HIV-1 infection by interacting with these regulatory RNAs and showing moderate affinity while lacking further functional validation for anti-HIV-1 activity, and its core is the thieno[2,3-b]pyridine scaffold, a fused thiophene and pyridine system, substituted at the 3-position with an amino group and at the 6-position with a methyl group, with the 2-position linked via an N-substituted carboxamide to a methyl group and a 3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl group, the thienopyridine framework being a significant pharmacophore found in antineoplastic and CNS drugs, the trifluoromethyl (CF3) group being highly lipophilic and electron-withdrawing, often introduced to enhance metabolic stability and membrane permeability, and the N-substituted amide being characteristic of many kinase inhibitors and GPCR antagonists, making this molecule a strong candidate for an enzyme-targeting drug, such as a PDE inhibitor or a kinase inhibitor with anti-inflammatory/anti-cancer activity.

RNA information: The r(CCUG)₁₀ sequence models the pathogenic RNA associated with myotonic dystrophy type 2. Structurally, it contains four-nucleotide repeats that also produce hairpins with mismatches, although their geometry differs slightly from CUG repeats. These motifs create similar alternating stripes of electrostatic potential, making selectivity between CUG and CCUG challenging for both proteins and small molecules. Functionally, r(CCUG)₁₀ sequesters MBNL1 in a manner comparable to CUG repeats, contributing to splicing defects. Its inclusion in binding studies demonstrates the difficulty of achieving discrimination between closely related repeat RNAs and emphasizes the need for ligands that exploit subtle structural differences.
Small_molecule_information: (Z,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-2-(propylamino)propanoyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 6 (2012), is the (Z)-geometric isomer (cis) of the oligo-peptide analogue (a Z-isomer lacking the benzo[g]quinoline moiety that was designed as a control to evaluate the importance of this heteroaromatic group), meaning it possesses the same connectivity but the alkene bond within the core chain has a different spatial arrangement; this minor change in geometry dictates a profound difference in the molecules overall three-dimensional shape and flexibility, and additionally, without the benzo[g]quinoline moiety, the compound maintains only weak interactions with RNA and loses significant selectivity, which confirms that the quinoline system is essential for high-affinity and discriminating recognition of repeat motifs, structurally it emphasizes the critical contribution of aromatic stacking in RNA binding, and functionally it has no significant biological activity, underscoring its role as a negative control and supporting the rationale for incorporating benzo[g]quinoline in active derivatives; in real-world drug discovery, geometric isomerism is a critical factor, as biological targets are highly sensitive to ligand shape; therefore, (Z,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-2-(propylamino)propanoyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide (also referred to as DCC 6 (2012)) is expected to exhibit drastically different biological activity, potency, and target selectivity compared to its (E)-isomer, potentially acting on entirely distinct biological pathways, and is essential for structure-activity relationship studies.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a short, genetically conserved stem–bulge–loop hairpin found at the 5′ end of every viral transcript.  Functional constructs range from minimal 29-nt fragments (containing the essential bulge and loop) up to the canonical 52–59-nt forms seen in full-length genomes; in all cases the active fold is the same: a double-stranded stem interrupted by a three-base pyrimidine bulge (commonly UCU) and capped by a six-nucleotide loop.  This bulge-and-loop motif first captures an arginine-rich segment of the viral Tat protein, inducing a local conformational switch that allows Tat to recruit the host Cyclin T1–CDK9 (P-TEFb) complex.  The resultant Tat–TAR–P-TEFb assembly converts RNA polymerase II from abortive to highly processive elongation, boosting viral gene expression >100-fold, and the same RNA structure also facilitates strand-transfer during reverse transcription.  Because the bulge–loop signature is indispensable and sequence-invariant across strains, TAR acts as a natural “riboswitch-like” sensor that tunes the balance between transcription and genome replication, offering a structurally small yet functionally critical target for therapeutic intervention.
Small_molecule_information: 2-amino-N-[3-[2-(methylamino)-1,3-thiazol-4-yl]phenyl]propanamide, also referred to as N-(2-N-Acetylamino-4-(3-aminophenyl)thiazole)-alaninamide (7b), is a relatively simple linear molecule composed of an alanine residue (2-aminopropanoyl) linked via an amide bond to a 3-[2-(methylamino)-1,3-thiazol-4-yl]aniline moiety, and it is a synthetic nucleobaseamino acid conjugate designed to target the HIV-1 TAR RNA stembulgeloop that replaces the natural ribose-phosphate backbone with a metabolically stable thiazolearyl core and presents a small, hydrophobic alanine side chain intended to probe minimal RNA recognition. The core includes a 1,3-thiazole ring, which is an important pharmacophore present in many drugs, known for antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and CNS activities. The presence of the amino acid segment and the amide bond suggests a potential for interaction with peptides or proteins, and in relevant assays the compound displays only weak affinity, a 1:1 RNA-ligand stoichiometry, no perturbation of the A-form helical structure, low specificity, no antiviral activity in HIV-infected MAGIC-5B cells and no cytotoxicity at the tested concentration, which establishes it as a low-affinity, non-specific TAR ligand whose negligible biological effect underscores the requirement for basic or aromatic functionality in future TAR-directed artificial riboswitch therapeutics. This structure is a classic template for kinase inhibitors, where the amide bond links aromatic and heterocyclic units. It is likely a lead structure for a novel anti-inflammatory or analgesic agent.

The presence of multiple Arginine residues significantly enhances the molecules basicity and capacity for strong nucleic acid binding. Sequences rich in Arginine are characteristic of Cell-Penetrating Peptides (CPPs). Its function is likely designed to be a potent antineoplastic prodrug with enhanced cell membrane permeability for efficient intracellular delivery of the cytotoxic core.

N-[3-[4-[[(2-amino-2-oxoethyl)-[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[2-[[1-[3-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]propyl]triazol-4-yl]methylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]amino]methyl]triazol-1-yl]propyl]-4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, Small_molecule_information: N-(17-methoxy-5,7-dioxa-13-azoniapentacyclo[11.8.0.0{2,10}.0{4,8}.0{15,20}]henicosa-1(13),2,4(8),9,14,16,18,20-octaen-16-yl)-N,N-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine chloride The side chain here is a short N,N-dimethylethane-1,2-diamine (or EDDA) structure. This ethylenediamine backbone is a classic pharmacophore used in pharmaceuticals and coordination chemistry. In terms of real-world activity, short-chain polyamines with dicationic tendencies are often investigated as broad-spectrum biocides or preservatives due to their efficacy in disrupting bacterial and yeast cell walls and membranes. Furthermore, the ethylenediamine structure provides potential for metal chelation, which might enable it to modulate the activity of metalloenzymes, suggesting potential as a lead structure for certain antiviral drugs.

RNA information: Human A-site rRNA is a conserved structural motif within the small ribosomal subunit that forms the decoding center during mRNA translation. It can specifically recognize and bind to the anticodon stem-loop of aminoacyl-tRNA, ensuring accurate codon–anticodon pairing and maintaining translational fidelity. This RNA element also serves as a binding platform for aminoglycoside antibiotics, whose occupation disrupts decoding fidelity and leads to mis-translation, making the A-site rRNA a key regulator of protein synthesis homeostasis in human cells.
Small_molecule_information: N-[4-(1H-benzimidazol-2-yl)phenyl]-5-(4-chlorophenyl)furan-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Benzimidazole 17 (2015), is another complex aromatic heterocycle centered on a benzimidazole core, and Benzimidazole 17 (2015) is a 2-phenyl-benzimidazole-4-carboxamide derivative engineered to selectively bind the toxic r(CUG)109 RNA expansion that causes myotonic dystrophy type 1, the benzimidazole is linked at position 2 via a phenyl ring to an amide bond, which in turn is connected to a furan ring substituted with a 4-chlorophenyl group, compared to N-[4-[6-(furan-2-carbonylamino)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenyl]furan-2-carboxamide, this structure is simpler, but its side chain is more lipophilic, incorporating a chlorine atom (4-chlorophenyl) to as proteins, enzymes, or DNA, the combination of a chlorine-substituted phenyl group and a benzimidazole is a common motif in drug design, often employed to enhance the molecules biological activity, this compound is investigated for potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, or antitumor properties, likely acting by inhibiting specific enzymes or protein-protein interactions, by mimicking the recognition surface of MBNL1, it engages the periodic UU internal loops of the CUG hairpin with notable potency in TR-FRET displacement, displaces MBNL1 from ribonuclear foci, and restores correct alternative splicing of the cTNT mini-gene in DM1 cell models without altering DMPK mRNA abundance or affecting off-target RNAs such as human A-site rRNA or r(GGCC)4, its drug-like physicochemical It combines the enzyme-cleavable Gly-Gly linker with the cell-penetrating properties of arginine. This structure is used in the development of highly targeted and cytotoxic prodrugs, leveraging enzyme-mediated activation for specific release and arginine to enhance cellular uptake and nuclear localization.

RNA information: r(CUG)₈ is an RNA fragment with 8 consecutive CUG repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 8 5′CUG/3′GUC internal loops, U·U mismatches, and optional terminal GC clamps (for reduced "breathing effects"), more stable than shorter CUG repeats. Functionally, it lacks pathogenic long (CUG)ₙ toxicity but binds MBNL1 with higher affinity than r(CUG)₆, has abundant small-molecule binding sites, and serves as an in vitro tool for studying MBNL1-(CUG) interactions and DM1 therapeutic screening.
Small_molecule_information: methyl 7-hydroxy-6-(hydroxymethylidene)-4,9-dioxo-10H-phenazine-1-carboxylate, also referred to as lomofungin, is a derivative of phenazine, a core structure found in natural pigments with anthraquinone-like features, substituted with hydroxyl, keto, and methyl carboxylate groups; it is a natural antimicrobial agent produced by Streptomyces lomondensis that undergoes spontaneous dimerization in DMSO to form dilomofungin, and in real activity, phenazine compounds are well-known for their redox activity, enabling them to generate reactive oxygen species, this compound has potential antimicrobial activity as certain phenazine natural products are potent antibiotics, while it has also been identified as a potent inhibitor of MBNL1-CUG RNA binding, binds selectively to RNA hairpins containing CUG repeats particularly those with UU mismatches and disrupts the interaction between MBNL1 protein and pathogenic CUGexp RNA, in cellular models of myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) it partially restores MBNL1-dependent alternative splicing and increases the diffuse nucleoplasmic localization of MBNL1 indicating functional reversal of RNA toxicity, though its dimeric form dilomofungin, while more potent in vitro, unexpectedly stabilizes CUGexp RNA and leads to its accumulation in nuclear foci highlighting a potential limitation in the therapeutic use of high-affinity RNA ligands, and these findings suggest that it represents a promising scaffold for developing small-molecule therapies against DM1 provided that off-target effects on RNA turnover are carefully managed.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.

This structure is a targeted drug design: the benzofuroquinoline acts as the cytotoxic warhead, and the alkaline peptide acts as a targeting vehicle to enhance cellular uptake and nuclear localization in anticancer drug development.

Small_molecule_information: 3-[[11-[3-(diethylamino)propylamino]-5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]propyl-trimethylazanium diiodide is a benzofuroquinoline bis-quaternary ammonium salt derivative, similar to N-[7-[(1,1-dimethylpiperidin-1-ium-4-yl)methoxy]-5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl]-N,N-diethylpropane-1,3-diamine diiodide. Its structure is based on the benzofuroquinoline scaffold, with the 5-position nitrogen quaternized, and the propane-amine group on the 7-position side chain also quaternized (trimethylammonium), with diiodide as the counterion (molecular formula C31H43N4O22I). This compound exhibits a planar conjugated system and two positive charges. The structural difference from N-[7-[(1,1-dimethylpiperidin-1-ium-4-yl)methoxy]-5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl]-N,N-diethylpropane-1,3-diamine diiodide may influence its spatial orientation and DNA binding mode, making it a powerful potential DNA-targeting anti-cancer agent.

RNA information: G4-EBNA1 is a stable G-quadruplex in the EBNA1 mRNA GAr region. It binds nucleolin (NCL), inhibiting translation and reducing antigen presentation to enable immune evasion. Its formation is length-dependent. Ligands like PhenDC3, PyDH2, and PhenDH2 disrupt NCL binding, restoring EBNA1 expression and antigen presentation. The structure prefers U-shaped ligands that stack on G-tetrads. Targeting G4-EBNA1 exposes infected cells to immune response, offering a therapeutic strategy against EBV.
Small_molecule_information: 2-N,6-N-bis[(E)-(1-methylpyridin-1-ium-4-yl)methylideneamino]pyridine-2,6-dicarboxamide diiodide, also referred to as PyDH1, is a dicationic compound with a pyridine-2,6-dicarboxamide core, linking two 1-methylpyridin-1-ium cationic groups via methylideneamino bonds, while PyDH1 as a cationic bis(acylhydrazone) features a central pyridine core and lateral 1-methylpyridinium groups and exhibits a V-shaped conformation stabilized by water-mediated hydrogen bonds, this overall structure forms a rigid pincer-like receptor, featuring two amide N-H hydrogen bond donors and cationic charges, this pre-organized structure gives it unique applications in supramolecular chemistry, and PyDH1 showed poor G4-RNA binding and stabilization and had no effect on EBNA1 expression in cellular assays, in practical applications, this compound is designed and studied for highly efficient and selective binding of anions (like Cl), and even neutral molecules like glucose and sorbitol in neutral aqueous solution, making it an important chemical sensor for potential use in blood sugar monitoring or anion detection in biological fluids.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-9-chloro-2-(4-fluorobenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 13, is a complex polycyclic derivative based on the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold, substituted with 3-amino, 4-phenyl, and a 2-(4-fluorobenzoyl) group, while featuring a chloro atom at position 9 and both chlorine and fluorine atoms overall; the chloro atom is an electron-withdrawing group, introducing smaller steric hindrance at position 9 compared to the bromo analogue, and this sophisticated planar polycyclic structure, combined with multiple electron-withdrawing groups (fluoro, chloro, carbonyl), results in high conjugation and rigidity, additionally exhibiting strong RNA-binding affinity and being useful in evaluating the synergistic effects of multiple halogens; in medicinal chemistry, this large molecular scaffold is frequently designed as a Topoisomerase inhibitor, and by disrupting Topoisomerase function, the compound can interfere with DNA replication and transcription, thus exerting cytotoxicity, consequently, its activity is mainly focused on the development of anti-tumor drugs.

RNA information: Located at positions 16–30 in the KRAS 5′-UTR. It forms a stable parallel RNA G-quadruplex (RG4), melting temperature (Tm) of 53°C, and ΔG of –4.9 kcal/mol. It is recognized by G4-specific antibodies and stabilized by small molecules like 2a and 2b.
Small_molecule_information: 4,11-bis(2-aminoethylamino)naphtho[2,3-f][1]benzofuran-5,10-dione, also referred to as a furan-fused anthraquinone derivative with bis(2-aminoethylamino) side chains, is a compound based on a Naphthoquinone core fused with a Benzofuran ring, resulting in a Naphtho[2,3-f][1]benzofuran-5,10-dione structure, substituted with two 2-aminoethylamino groups at positions C-4 and C-11. Anthraquinone/Naphthoquinone compounds are a well-known class of DNA topoisomerase inhibitors and DNA intercalators, forming the basis of many chemotherapy drugs (e.g., Mitoxantrone). Additionally, these quinone structures readily undergo redox cycling, generating Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS) that cause DNA damage and cell apoptosis, and this compound also exhibits high cellular uptake, strong stabilization of KRAS RG4s and specific binding affinity, which enables it to effectively inhibit translation and induce apoptosis in pancreatic cancer cells, meaning it is therefore primarily researched as a novel anti-tumor agent with a mechanism involving dual DNA intercalation and ROS-mediated cytotoxicity.

RNA information: The influenza A virus RNA promoter features a bent A-form helical structure with a dynamic (A–A)–U internal loop that serves as a specific binding site for the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) and nucleoprotein complex. This unique structural motif exhibits conformational flexibility that facilitates recognition by viral replication machinery while maintaining overall A-form geometry. The promoter's distinctive architecture and dynamic properties are essential for initiating viral RNA synthesis, making it a critical functional element in the influenza replication cycle and a promising target for antiviral therapeutic development; the influenza A virus RNA promoter is a highly conserved structural element located at the 3' and 5' ends of each viral RNA segment. Its structure, determined by NMR spectroscopy, adopts a panhandle-like conformation characterized by an A-form helix. A key structural feature is a unique (A-A)-U internal loop and a C-A mismatch pair. Furthermore, the promoter exhibits a significant bend of approximately 46 ± 10° near the viral RNA initiation site. Functionally, this specific architecture is specifically recognized by the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp), which binds to it to initiate both transcription and replication of the viral genome. Small_molecule_information: 2-[2-(5-carbamimidoyl-1-benzofuran-2-yl)ethyl]-1-benzofuran-5-carboximidamide is a bis-amidine-benzofuran derivative, with two amidine groups attached to two benzofuran rings. The benzofuran core is often found in molecules with antineoplastic, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. This bis-amidine structure might be an effective DNA minor groove binder, studied for antiparasitic diseases (e.g., leishmaniasis) or antiviral therapy.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 7,8-dimethyl-10-(2,3,4,5-tetrahydroxypentyl)benzo[g]pteridine-2,4-dione, also known as vitamin B2 and also referred to as Riboflavin, is an important water-soluble vitamin whose structure consists of an isoalloxazine aromatic ring and a ribitol side chain, adopting a flat yet slightly flexible molecular configuration; it serves as a precursor for flavin mononucleotide (FMN) and flavin adenine dinucleotide (FAD), two molecules that act as coenzymes in various redox reactions in organisms, playing a crucial role in cellular energy metabolism and biosynthetic processes, and as a weak competitive ligand, riboflavin can enter the aptamer pocket of bacterial FMN riboswitches, and although its affinity is lower than that of FMN, it can still induce conformational changes in RNA. In the article, the binding ability of riboflavin to the FMN riboswitch was studied, revealing its role in regulating gene expression: riboflavin can bind to the FMN riboswitch, thereby regulating the expression of genes related to riboflavin biosynthesis and transport, and partially inhibiting the expression of downstream riboflavin synthesis genes, thereby participating in the negative feedback regulation of its own biosynthesis; when riboflavin or its derivative FMN binds to the riboswitch, it induces the formation of a transcription termination structure, thereby inhibiting the expression of downstream genes, a mechanism that is vital for maintaining the homeostasis of riboflavin levels in cells.

RNA information: FMDV IRES SL3A (Foot-and-mouth disease virus Internal Ribosome Entry Site Stem-loop 3A) is a conserved RNA structural element within the IRES region of the FMDV genome. It specifically binds to host and viral proteins to facilitate cap-independent translation initiation, ensuring efficient viral protein synthesis even when host cap-dependent translation is compromised. SL3A plays a critical role in maintaining the structural integrity and function of the IRES, which is essential for viral replication and infectivity. Its sequence and structure are key determinants of IRES activity, influencing the efficiency of internal ribosome entry and overall viral translation.
Small_molecule_information: 7-[(dimethylamino)methyl]-1-[3-(dimethylamino)propyl]furo[3,2-e]benzimidazol-2-amine, also referred to as IRAB (IRES Aminobenzimidazole Binder), is a compound that contains the benzimidazole scaffold fused with a furan ring, and IRAB is a small molecule based on a benzimidazole scaffold, designed to bind to and modulate the internal ribosome entry site (IRES) of foot-and-mouth disease virus (FMDV), this furo[3,2-e]benzimidazole structure is a common core for many potential antimicrobial and anticancer agents, whose activity stems from interfering with microbial or cancer cell DNA synthesis and topoisomerase activity, holding potential for the development of new anti-infective or anti-tumor drugs, IRAB shares structural similarity with the 2-aminobenzimidazole derivative Isis-11, an inhibitor of hepatitis C virus (HCV) replication, but features an extended aromatic furan ring that eliminates chirality and enhances synthetic accessibility, in vitro, IRAB preferentially inhibits IRES-dependent translation over cap-dependent translation in both FMDV and HCV systems, leading to a significant reduction in viral protein synthesis, structural studies using SHAPE probing and fluorescence binding assays reveal that IRAB binds specifically to the apical region of domain 3 within the FMDV IRES, particularly the SL3A subdomain, inducing local conformational changes that disrupt RNA structure and reduce IRES activity, in cell-based assays, IRAB effectively reduces viral titers and viral protein expression in FMDV RNA-transfected cells, demonstrating potent antiviral activity, these findings suggest that IRAB and its analogs hold promise as novel antiviral agents targeting viral translation initiation.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC)x6: This is a six-repeat control RNA with perfect Watson-Crick base pairing. It is used to evaluate the specificity of compound binding and shows minimal structural perturbation upon ligand interaction, confirming that small molecules do not significantly bind to regular duplex RNA.; The (5'CAG/3'GUC)x6 sequence serves as the extended control duplex. Its repetitive yet perfectly paired structure lacks the distinctive features of disease-repeat RNA, allowing researchers to conclusively prove that compound efficacy is due to specific recognition of the pathological mismatch motif and not general RNA binding.
Small_molecule_information: methyl N-(4-chlorophenyl)-2H-quinoline-1-carboximidothioate, also referred to as CP2, is a quinoline-based molecule featuring a thione and imine functionality (which incorporates a quinoline core linked to a thiocarbamate group and a 4-chlorophenyl ring, with the combination of its planar aromatic system and halogen substituent contributing to its affinity for CAG repeat RNA and efficacy in reducing polyQ aggregation in cellular models, marking it as a promising candidate). Quinoline scaffolds are associated with various anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, and this complex structure is a candidate for research as an enzyme inhibitor, possibly targeting microbial enzymes, due to its specialized reactive groups.

Small_molecule_information: benzene-1,2-diol, also referred to as the benzene derivative with two adjacent hydroxyl groups (commonly known as Catechol), is one of the simplest dihydric phenolic compounds, featuring two adjacent hydroxyl groups attached to the benzene ring, and this compound is structurally a benzene derivative with two adjacent hydroxyl (-OH) groups, belonging to dihydroxybenzenes, and has a planar, polar aromatic structure (not an azaaromatic, unlike the documents focus), Catechol is an extremely important chemical structure, widely present in natural products (such as catechins, dopamine, adrenaline), and possesses potent antioxidant and free radical scavenging capabilities, due to its two adjacent hydroxyl groups, catechol is an excellent bidentate ligand, capable of forming stable chelates with many metal ions (e.g., iron, vanadium, chromium), in reality, catechol and its derivatives are used as raw materials for synthesizing pharmaceuticals (such as catecholamine neurotransmitters), pesticides, fragrances, and preservatives, in medicine, it is a crucial structural unit in the Parkinsons disease treatment drug L-DOPA, functionally, no data links it to yjdF riboswitch binding or gene regulation (the documents key topics); generally, it acts as a metabolite or redox agent, but such roles arent mentioned in the given content.
