Small_molecule_information: 6-pyrrolidin-1-yl-2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine, also referred to as 3c, is built upon the 2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine core, with a pyrrolidin-1-yl group directly attached to the 6-position. The direct attachment of the nitrogen atom allows the pyrrolidine ring to influence the electronic properties and basicity of the pyridine system more directly compared to a methylene-linked structure. This class of nitrogen-containing heterocyclic amines is widely studied as scaffolds for kinase inhibitors or GPCR ligands. The pyrrolidine moiety enhances lipophilicity, which aids in cell penetration and bioavailability, and this compound was not reported to be an active inhibitor, representing an early-stage research molecule potentially for antitumor, anti-inflammatory, or nervous system applications, with its activity defined by its interaction with specific biological targets.

RNA information: RNA1 contains the 1x1 nucleotide UU internal loop motif that is present within the Drosha processing site of pri-miR-96. It serves as the specific binding target for compound 1, with a Kd of 1,300 nM, but does not bind compound 2.
Small_molecule_information: N-(2-azidoethyl)-4-[3-[6-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as Compound 2, is an azide-tagged bis-benzimidazole probe featuring a short two-carbon (ethyl) linker, which is a bis-benzimidazole-based RNA-binding module that Informa predicted and binding studies confirmed selectively targets a 1x1 nucleotide GG internal loop adjacent to the Drosha site in pri-miR-96, while it binds its target RNA motif, it also exhibits high, undesired affinity for an AT-rich DNA hairpin and does not effectively inhibit miR-96 biogenesis in cellular assays when used as a monomer; the shorter linker alters the spatial presentation of the azide group for the subsequent Click Chemistry reaction, and its real-world activity is as a Click Chemistry probe for DNA binding and labeling studies, used to investigate the impact of a close-to-core tag on the probes biological performance.

RNA information: G4-EBNA1 is a stable G-quadruplex in the EBNA1 mRNA GAr region. It binds nucleolin (NCL), inhibiting translation and reducing antigen presentation to enable immune evasion. Its formation is length-dependent. Ligands like PhenDC3, PyDH2, and PhenDH2 disrupt NCL binding, restoring EBNA1 expression and antigen presentation. The structure prefers U-shaped ligands that stack on G-tetrads. Targeting G4-EBNA1 exposes infected cells to immune response, offering a therapeutic strategy against EBV.
Small_molecule_information: 2-N,7-N-bis[(E)-(1-methylpyridin-1-ium-4-yl)methylideneamino]-1,8-naphthyridine-2,7-dicarboxamide diiodide, also referred to as NaphDH1, is a dicationic compound with a 1,8-naphthyridine-2,7-dicarboxamide core, linking two 1-methylpyridin-1-ium cationic groups via methylideneamino bonds, and NaphDH1 features a central 1,8-naphthyridine core with 1-methylpyridinium lateral groups, compared to 2-N,7-N-bis[(E)-(1-methylquinolin-1-ium-4-yl)methylideneamino]-1,8-naphthyridine-2,7-dicarboxamide diiodide, the quinolinium is replaced by the smaller pyridinium, this polycationic conjugated system is a photophysically active molecule, in practical applications, it is studied as a fluorescent probe for intracellular ion detection or as a DNA damaging agent, due to its specific structure and charge distribution, it binds with high affinity and stabilizes G-quadruplex DNA structures, playing an important role in anticancer drug design, NaphDH1 showed weak G4 stabilization and moderate FID affinity but no biological effect. Small_molecule_information: 10-[3-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)propyl]-2-(trifluoromethyl)phenothiazine, also referred to as Trifluoperazine, a phenothiazine antipsychotic drug that primarily acts as a dopamine antagonist, is based on the phenothiazine tricyclic core, features an electron-withdrawing trifluoromethyl group at the 2-position which enhances its lipophilicity, and has a propyl chain connecting an N-methylpiperazine group at the 10-position nitrogen; structurally, this is characteristic of many classic antipsychotic drugs, and this compound is expected to show potent dopamine receptor antagonist activity, particularly towards the central nervous system D2 receptor, making it potentially useful for treating schizophrenia or for its sedative effects.

RNA information: RNA 3 (adjacent to Drosha 515) is an RNA fragment mimicking the region adjacent to the Drosha processing site in pri-miR-515. Its core structural feature is a Compound 1-binding motif unique to pri-miR-515, which is absent in pri-miR-885. It serves as an in vitro tool to test small molecule-RNA binding: monomer Compound 1 binds to it (Kd=7.5±0.7 μM), while dimer Compound 2 does not. Such studies provide key data for revealing Compound 2’s selective binding mechanism to pri-miR-515.
Small_molecule_information: N-(3-azidopropyl)-4-[2,6-ditert-butyl-4-[6-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as Compound 1 (Targaprimir-515/885), is a complex molecular probe based on the bis-benzimidazole (DNA minor groove binder) scaffold and modified with an azide group (-N) and bulky tert-butyl groups, and it is also an azide-containing monomeric small molecule that serves as an RNA-binding module coupling to peptidomimetic scaffolds via Cu-catalyzed azide-alkyne click chemistry, which binds similarly to Drosha processing site motifs of pri-miR-515 and pri-miR-885 plus an adjacent motif of pri-miR-515 to inhibit both miRNAs biogenesis and acts as a lead for selective dimers like Compound 2 to aid RNA-targeting small molecule optimization; in chemical biology, the azide group is the key functional group for Click Chemistry, its real-world activity is designed as a chemically taggable DNA-binding probe, and it is used to study the biological targets of the bis-benzimidazole core by attaching a reporter molecule (e.g., fluorophore or biotin) to the binding site via a highly specific click reaction.

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2-ethylphenyl]phenol, also referred to as Compound 5f (a 3,5,2,6-tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) terphenyl compound with a single ethyl group at the 2 position of the central benzene ring), is a large, polyfunctionalized phenol derivative, which is synthesized via sequential palladium-catalyzed Suzuki couplings; its structure is based on a phenol core substituted at the 4-position with a complex phenyl group, which itself contains a 4-methoxyphenyl, an ethyl, and two 2-aminoethyl groups, the 2 and 6 positions of the phenol core are also substituted with two 2-aminoethyl groups, the central scaffold is composed of two substituted benzene rings connected by a C-C bond, this compound adopts a non-planar structure, containing one phenolic hydroxyl group (which can act as a hydrogen bond donor) and four free amino groups (which can be protonated and positively charged at physiological pH); in terms of biological function, phenolic compounds are generally known for their antioxidant activity, the multiple amino groups confer cationic properties and potential for DNA/RNA binding, it can bind to HIV-1 RRE Bh (ITC-measured), has a certain specificity ratio, inhibits Bh-Rev interaction and shows anti-HIV activity in cell assays, and its structural characteristics may render it a novel drug or biological probe.

Small_molecule_information: 4-(2-naphthalen-1-ylethyl)pyridine, also referred to as CP16, is a coupled aromatic compound containing pyridine and naphthalene moieties, where the naphthalene ring (two fused benzene rings) is linked to the 4-position of the pyridine ring via an ethyl chain, and this flexible alkyl linkage together with the separated aromatic systems is consistent with its general, non-specific interaction with RNA; both the pyridine and naphthalene rings are hydrophobic and planar structures, which help the molecule interact with lipid membranes or hydrophobic pockets, and such structures are often used to design drugs with the ability to interact with membranes or receptors, while the basic nitrogen atom in the pyridine ring can be ionized, influencing its distribution and activity in vivo

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[3-[[3-[[3-[[3-[[3-[2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]ethylamino]-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-5A, is a very high molecular weight organic compound, structurally defined by two identical, substituted benzimidazole cores, each bearing a 4-methylpiperazine group, while 2H-5A itself carries five -alanine residues and did not display improved potency, with its scaffold showing limited suitability as its relevant activity performance remained inferior to the most effective glycine-based constructs, and the article did not emphasize its further characterization as the compound primarily served to complete the -peptide length series with not much additional information available; the distinctive feature of this overall class of compounds is the ultra-long, flexible linker connecting these two aromatic moieties, composed of seven repeating aminopropanoyl units, providing a significant spatial span, and benzimidazole serves as a core known for its planar geometry and charge characteristics, often utilized as a ligand for DNA or RNA, the design of such a long, flexible bicephalic molecule is typically intended to precisely match biological targets with specific spacings, such as nucleic acid macromolecules or protein oligomers, and this compound may be investigated as a novel genetic probe or a multivalent inhibitor in cancer or infectious disease research.

Small_molecule_information: N-[3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl]-2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine-6-carboxamide, also referred to as the amide linker-modified derivative of its parent structure, is an amide-class small molecule heterocycle with the molecular formula C15H12F3N3O; it is formed by linking the carboxylic acid of 2,3-dihydro-1H-pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine via an amide bond to 3-(trifluoromethyl)aniline, and its rigid amide linker modification has resulted in significantly reduced binding affinity, demonstrating the importance of structural flexibility, while the trifluoromethyl (CF3) group is a strongly electron-withdrawing and highly lipophilic moiety, widely used in drug design to enhance metabolic stability and improve the molecules ability to cross cell membranes, the pyrrolo[3,4-c]pyridine scaffold provides a unique fused heterocyclic structure with multiple potential hydrogen bond donors and acceptors, this aromatic amide structure is common in many marketed and developmental small-molecule drugs, frequently designed as ATP-competitive inhibitors targeting intracellular enzymes, therefore, the compound is likely to possess kinase inhibitory activity, regulating key proteins in signal transduction pathways (such as tyrosine kinases), thereby demonstrating potential anti-inflammatory or anti-cancer activity.

RNA information: r(CUG)109 is a pathogenic RNA motif composed of 109 CUG repeats that folds into a stable, elongated hairpin structure containing periodic 1×1 UU internal loops. It functions as a toxic RNA by specifically binding to and sequestering muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, thereby disrupting its normal splicing regulation and contributing to the molecular pathology of myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). Small molecules that selectively bind r(CUG)109 can displace MBNL1, restore aberrant splicing patterns, and alleviate disease-associated phenotypes in cellular models .
Small_molecule_information: 6-nitro-2-[3-(6-nitro-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl)propyl]-1H-benzimidazole, also referred to as Benzimidazole 1 (2015), is a symmetric or near-symmetric molecule composed of two molecule to bind to biological targets in various conformations, while the nitro groups enhance the molecules electron-withdrawing capacity and potential redox activity, in real-world applications, RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 5-methyl-7-[2-(4-methylmorpholin-4-ium-4-yl)ethoxy]-N-(3-morpholin-4-ylpropyl)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-amine diiodide. This is a dicationic compound based on the benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium core with diiodide ions. Its dicationic structure results from the core N-methylation and the 4-methylmorpholin-4-ium quaternary ammonium group at position 7. The morpholine group, a common medicinal scaffold, influences the molecules hydrophilicity and targeting. This compound is researched for antiviral and anticancer applications. Its polycyclic planar structure and cationic charge allow it to interact with viral nucleic acids or host cell DNA, thereby interfering with viral replication or cellular proliferation.

RNA information: 5'CGG/3'GGC is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it forms a hairpin with a 1×1 internal loop, closed by CG pairs, adopting non-canonical folding. Functionally, it contributes to FXTAS pathogenesis, likely by sequestering cellular proteins and disrupting normal splicing. As a disease-relevant RNA, it serves as a target for screening therapeutic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: 2,3-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium, also referred to as Nitidine, is another methylated benzophenanthridine quaternary alkaloid, similar to 1,2-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium but with different methoxy substitution (2,3-positions), and as a benzophenanthridine alkaloid it also possesses antitumor and antimalarial activity, can inhibit topoisomerases and telomerase to disrupt DNA replication, and its fluorescent properties aid in cellular imaging studies; due to its planar, rigid, and positively charged structural features, its primary activity in practice is as a DNA intercalator and cytotoxic agent, exhibiting strong anti-tumor properties, minor changes in the substitution pattern (from 1,2- to 2,3-dimethoxy) are critical in drug design to modulate the molecules charge distribution and steric bulk, which can alter its interaction strength and selectivity for specific DNA sequences or Topoisomerase isozymes, and this makes it valuable in the structure-activity relationship studies of novel anticancer agents.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 6-amino-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]hexanoic acid, this compound is a complex conjugate of a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core with amino acids. This structural class is known for DNA/RNA binding and topoisomerase inhibition, developed as broad-spectrum anticancer agents. Real-world applications may involve their use as novel anti-tumor leads.

RNA information: No loop (loopless RNA), also known as 5'CAG/3'CUG and 5'CAG/3'GUC, is an RNA with a hairpin structure. Structurally, its stem has fully complementary base pairs (no mismatches/deletions) and no internal loops, showing high conformational stability without inter-strand "breathing effect" as a simplified dsRNA model. Functionally, it serves as a key experimental control to distinguish small molecules’ specific/non-specific binding to RNA, with no direct link to specific diseases.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide, also referred to as Compound 20 (DAPI, 4,6-diamidino-2-phenylindole), is a compound containing an indole scaffold and bis-imidamide groups, one imidamide directly attached to the indole ring and the other attached via a phenyl ring at the C2 position of indole, with the formula C16H14N4, and it is also a commercially available guanidino/amidino-containing compound, a routine nuclear stain and chemical progenitor of derivative D6; this indole diamidine combines the lipophilicity of indole with the cationic nature of the diamidine groups, and it can bind influenza A RNA promoter (causes NMR perturbations), AT-rich dsDNA minor groove, and r(CAG)exps 5CAG/3GAC loop, disrupt r(CAG)expMBN1, correct splicing defects in Huntingtons/ataxia type 3 models though it has cytotoxicity that limits direct optimization, this combination also results in an effective DNA minor groove binder with potent antimicrobial and antitumor activity, and it is researched for developing new anticancer agents with high target specificity and enhanced pharmacokinetic profiles.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-9-bromo-2-(4-fluorobenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 16, is a polycyclic heterocyclic ketone featuring a complex four-ring chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one core, this large, planar system is substituted at multiple positions: amino at 3, phenyl at 4, bromo at 9, and a 4-fluorobenzoyl group at 2, the molecules multiple aromatic rings and carbonyl groups contribute to its high planarity and conjugation, this structural motif is the basis for classic DNA intercalators or cell cycle inhibitors, capable of binding to nucleic acids or vital enzymes through stacking and hydrogen bonding, the bromo and fluoro atoms (which are the halogen substitutions of this compound) act as halogen substituents to enhance lipophilicity and metabolic stability, and it is worth noting that this compound displayed strong RNA-binding properties, was among the most potent compounds and reinforced the value of halogen atoms in improving drug-like properties, in practice, similar chromene derivatives are explored for their broad-spectrum anti-cancer activity or as potential photosensitizers for photodynamic therapy.

RNA information: RNA5 is a 29-nt synthetic hairpin RNA based on SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5, containing a single G bulge. It spontaneously folds into an A-form helix with a flexible bulge site, enabling minor-groove recognition. RNA5 is used for GaMD simulations, NMR R2 mapping, and Kd measurements to probe selective ligand binding.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(7-fluoroimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-2-yl)-4-methyl-7-piperazin-1-ylchromen-2-one, also referred to as C30-Me which is a methylated analog of C30 bearing a single methyl on the coumarin C-ring, disrupting BCDE planarity, the steric clash of which impedes minor-groove insertion, abolishing high-affinity binding, and GaMD shows only transient RNA contacts confirming planarity as a critical determinant for potent G-bulge recognition, this compounds core is the coumarin (chromen-2-one) scaffold, substituted at the 3-position with a 7-fluoroimidazo[1,2-a]pyridine, at the 7-position with piperazin-1-yl, and at the 4-position with a methyl group, the free piperazine amine is highly basic and readily protonates at physiological pH, significantly impacting its pharmacokinetics and binding affinity, the combination of coumarin and imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine suggests a compound targeting kinases or GPCRs, commonly researched for oncology or CNS disorders, and the 4-methyl substituent further modulates the electronic properties and steric profile of the coumarin core, aimed at optimizing target selectivity.

RNA information: LM-pbuG is a guanine riboswitch from Listeria monocytogenes. Its structural feature consists of a highly conserved three-stem ring region, which is organized around a three-directional junction point to form the guanine binding site. The function of LM-pbuG is to specifically recognize and bind to guanine through its aptamer region, thereby regulating gene expression. When guanine binds to the aptamer region, it causes a rearrangement of RNA structure. This structural change can lead to the termination or continuation of transcription, thereby controlling the expression of downstream genes. In the experiment, LM-pbuG demonstrated a binding affinity for 2-aminopine (2AP), indicating that it also has a high affinity for guanine. This difference in binding affinity may reflect the response ability of different ribose switches to changes in ligand concentration in vivo, thereby playing a key role in the metabolic regulation and gene expression of bacteria. This regulatory mechanism of LM-pbuG enables it to respond rapidly when the intracellular guanine concentration changes, thereby precisely regulating the expression levels of related genes.
Small_molecule_information: 7H-purin-2-amine, also referred to as 2-aminopine2AP, is a fluorescently labeled purine analogue and a fluorescent derivative of adenine with the 2-position hydrogen replaced by an amino group, which is a nitrogen-heterocyclic aromatic compound, often used to study the ligand binding characteristics of RNA molecules, especially riboswitches. The structure of 2AP is similar to that of adenine and guanine, and it can be embedded in the pbuE ribose switch like adenine, which makes it suitable as a substitute for these bases to study the interaction between RNA and ligands and can be used as a probe to reveal the dynamic-thermodynamic control mechanism of the ribose switch. Functionally, 2AP is used as a probe to detect and quantify the binding affinity of ribose switches for purine ligands. When 2AP binds to the aptamer region of the ribose switch, it causes a significant weakening of 2AP fluorescence. This is because 2AP is completely surrounded by RNA in the ligand binding pocket of the ribose switch. By monitoring the changes in 2AP fluorescence, researchers can infer the affinity of the ribose switch for ligand binding, as well as the changes in the ribose switch structure before and after ligand binding.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 3,5,7-trihydroxy-2-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one, also referred to as Kaempferol, is a natural flavonol compound structurally similar to Quercetin (2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-3,5,7-trihydroxychromen-4-one) and Myricetin (3,5,7-trihydroxy-2-(3,4,5-trihydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one), differing mainly in the number of hydroxyl groups on the B ring, having only one at position 4, with the formula C15H10O6. Kaempferol is found in a variety of plant foods, such as tea, broccoli, grapefruit, and herbs. Functionally, as a dietary flavonol, Kaempferol possesses significant antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and potential anticancer properties, it modulates cell signaling pathways (e.g., PI3K/Akt) and is studied for cardiovascular and neurodegenerative disease prevention, research also suggests it can regulate apoptosis, inhibit angiogenesis, and suppress cell proliferation, thereby potentially inhibiting tumor development, its role in the prevention of chronic diseases and the maintenance of cardiovascular health is particularly studied, although its bioactivity may differ from other flavonols due to the single hydroxyl group on its B ring.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC)x6: This is a six-repeat control RNA with perfect Watson-Crick base pairing. It is used to evaluate the specificity of compound binding and shows minimal structural perturbation upon ligand interaction, confirming that small molecules do not significantly bind to regular duplex RNA.; The (5'CAG/3'GUC)x6 sequence serves as the extended control duplex. Its repetitive yet perfectly paired structure lacks the distinctive features of disease-repeat RNA, allowing researchers to conclusively prove that compound efficacy is due to specific recognition of the pathological mismatch motif and not general RNA binding.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-benzoyl-9-bromo-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 14, is a four-ring heterocycle based on the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold, the molecule is substituted with a phenyl group at 4, a bromo atom at 9, and a benzoyl group at 2, compared to some of its analogues, this compound lacks the para-fluoro atom on the benzoyl group at position 2, this defluorination is a common optimization step in drug discovery used to finely tune the molecules electronic distribution and lipophilicity, due to its inherent planarity and multiple aromatic rings, the structure of this compound suggests it is a potential DNA intercalator or small molecule inhibitor, in research, similar large planar heterocyclic ketones are often explored for their therapeutic potential against proliferative diseases, achieved through high-affinity binding to key intracellular targets, and this compound which contains a bromine atom showed high binding affinity to r(CAG)exp RNA, was effective in biophysical assays and reduced polyQ aggregation in cells.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GAC)x6: This RNA contains six consecutive CAG repeats with AA mismatches, forming multiple internal loops. It is a key target for small molecules due to its structural similarity to pathogenic expanded repeats. It shows high affinity and selective binding to compounds such as pyridocoumarin derivatives, leading to conformational changes and thermal stabilization.; The (5'CAG/3'GAC)x6 RNA contains multiple consecutive A-A mismatch loops. This multi-loop structure enhances conformational stability and ligand binding avidity, more accurately mimicking the expanded toxic repeats found in patient-derived RNA and providing a robust platform for biophysical testing.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-benzoyl-9-chloro-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 10, is a derivative of the complex chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold, characterized by the 3-amino, 2-benzoyl, and 4-phenyl substituents. It features a chloro atom at position 9 (and overall contains a chlorine atom) and displayed significant binding affinity for CAG RNA, with effectiveness in gel shift and PCR stop assays indicating strong interaction with target RNA; the molecule is a highly rigid, planar polycyclic compound with multiple potential hydrogen bonding sites and stacking regions, and the chloro substitution serves to fine-tune the molecules physicochemical properties and steric hindrance. This fused polycyclic structure is an important template in anti-cancer drug design, with its pharmacological mechanism often hypothesized to involve DNA/RNA interference or inhibition of key enzymes regulating the cell cycle, and the chemical profile of this compound makes it a candidate for research as a cytotoxic agent or anti-proliferative drug.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 165ribD is a riboswitch that can specifically bind to FMN (flavin mononucleotide). It maintains the homeostasis of FMN and riboflavin in bacterial cells by regulating gene expression.
Small_molecule_information: 5-[8-(dimethylamino)-7-methyl-2,4-dioxobenzo[g]pteridin-10-yl]pentyl dihydrogen phosphate, also referred to as BRX830 which is a synthetic analog of FMN (flavin mononucleotide), is a phosphate-containing pteridine derivative whose structural design aims to optimize binding ability with the FMN riboswitch; its structure features the 8-(dimethylamino)-7-methylbenzo[g]pteridine-2,4-dione core, with the terminal of the pentyl side chain attached at N10-position being phosphorylated, and the core structure of BRX830 retains an isoalloxazine ring system similar to that of FMN but removes the ribityl chain present in natural FMN, replacing it with a simple alkyl chain, a design intended to reduce dependence on the phosphate group while improving the molecules passive membrane permeability, thereby enhancing its properties as a potential antibacterial agent; the molecular formula is CHNOP; this compound is similar to [5-[8-(dimethylamino)-7-methyl-2,4-dioxobenzo[g]pteridin-10-yl]-2,3,4-trihydroxypentyl] dihydrogen phosphate but lacks hydroxyl groups on the chain, making it moderately hydrophilic, and the phosphate group enhances water solubility; in terms of biological function, it can be used as an intracellular flavin-like molecular probe or a substrate for studying phosphatase activity, and functionally, BRX830 exhibits binding ability similar to FMN, as it can bind to the FMN riboswitch and induce conformational changes, thereby regulating gene expression, and through chemical probe techniques (such as SHAPE analysis) and in vitro transcription termination assays, BRX830 has been shown to effectively bind to the riboswitch and partially mimic the function of FMN, furthermore, the discovery of BRX830 provides an important structural basis and functional guidance for the further optimization of FMN analogs, especially in exploring how chemical modifications can enhance antibacterial activity and selectivity.

RNA information: The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).; The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).·
Small_molecule_information: 5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoic acidA simpler benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline conjugate with a short peptide chain consisting of three Glycine units and one Arginine residue. The potential activity is anticancer driven by the cores mechanism (DNA intercalation/Topoisomerase inhibition) and the Arginines affinity for nucleic acids. This structure is often utilized in drug optimization to determine the minimal peptide length required for retaining biological activity and desired pharmacokinetic properties.

RNA information: The r(CGG)12 sequence is a CGG-repeat RNA motif derived from the FMR1 5′UTR that functions as a protein-binding ribo-regulator. In cells it folds into a hairpin displaying multiple 5′CGG/3′GGC internal loops; these loops act as tandem recognition sites for RNA-processing factors such as DGCR8, Sam68 and hnRNP A2/B1. By sequestering these proteins into nuclear foci, r(CGG)12 perturbs alternative pre-mRNA splicing and, in the absence of an AUG start codon, it also templates repeat-associated non-ATG (RAN) translation to produce toxic poly-glycine-containing peptides. Thus, r(CGG)12 behaves like a disease-linked riboswitch-like element whose “ligand” is the protein machinery it binds, and its homeostatic imbalance drives the molecular pathology of fragile-X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS).
Small_molecule_information: N-[2-[[2-[(2-amino-2-oxoethyl)-[[1-[3-[[4-[5-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)furan-2-yl]benzenecarboximidoyl]amino]propyl]triazol-4-yl]methyl]amino]-2-oxoethyl]-propylamino]-2-oxoethyl]-2-[[1-[3-[[4-[5-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)furan-2-yl]benzenecarboximidoyl]amino]propyl]triazol-4-yl]methylamino]-N-propylacetamide, also referred to as 2AU-2 (a dimeric compound with two AU-azide modules spaced by two propylamine units), is a symmetrical bis-head molecule whose core active unit is an amidine and benzamidine derivative; this compound shows enhanced affinity, positive cooperativity, displaces bound proteins, reduces nuclear foci, mitochondrial dysfunction, and caspase-3 activity in SCA10 cells, and its amidine group is an important cationic functional group, the core active system of this molecule is a polyamidine-containing large conjugated planar system, exhibiting potent DNA minor groove binding ability, similar to classic anti-protozoal drugs like Pentamidine, the molecule links two amidine active units via a long chain containing triazole and polyamide segments, forming a bis-head structure designed to enhance DNA binding affinity and potentially achieve DNA cross-linking, therefore, the predicted real-world activity for this molecule is as a highly effective anti-protozoal, anti-fungal agent or a novel broad-spectrum antibiotic.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide, also referred to as the saturated analog of DAPI (whose full name is 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide), is a compound featuring a dihydroindole scaffold and bis-imidamide groups, with the formula C16H16N4, while DAPI is a fluorescent stain binding AT-rich DNA regions, widely used in microscopy for nuclear labeling and is cell-impermeant unless membranes are compromised, making it useful for viability assays and chromosome visualization; this compound is the saturated analog of 2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide, the saturation of the indole ring removes aromaticity and significantly increases the flexibility and changes the 3D shape of the molecule, this structural change dramatically influences its binding mode and affinity for the DNA minor groove, and this compound is investigated to explore the impact of molecular rigidity/flexibility on the activity of DNA-binding drugs, showing potential for anticancer and anti-infective activity.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[(5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl)amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoic acid is the benzofuroquinoline core conjugated via the glycine-glycine-arginine (Gly-Gly-Arg) tripeptide. It combines the enzyme-cleavable Gly-Gly linker with the cell-penetrating properties of arginine. This structure is used in the development of highly targeted and cytotoxic prodrugs, leveraging enzyme-mediated activation for specific release and arginine to enhance cellular uptake and nuclear localization.

RNA information: The bacterial riboflavin (FMN) riboswitch is located in the 5' untranslated region (5' UTR) of mRNA and consists of an aptamer domain and an expression platform. It can specifically bind to FMN and regulate the expression of the downstream key riboflavin synthesis gene ribB through conformational changes. When FMN is sufficient, the riboswitch forms a terminator/sequestering loop to inhibit gene expression; when FMN is deficient, it switches to an antiterminator/anti-sequestering loop, allowing gene transcription and translation to achieve negative feedback regulation. This riboswitch exists exclusively in bacteria and has no homologous structure in mammals, making it a highly selective target for new antibacterial drugs (such as ribocil).
Small_molecule_information: 2-[1-[[2-(methylamino)pyrimidin-5-yl]methyl]piperidin-3-yl]-4-thiophen-2-yl-1H-pyrimidin-6-one, also referred to as Ribocil, is a synthetic small molecule with an overall U-shaped rigid skeleton, this complex molecule has a substituted pyrimidin-6-one core, linked at the 2-position to a substituted piperidine ring, which is further connected to a 2-(methylamino)pyrimidinyl-methyl group, the 4-position is substituted with a thiophenyl group, its left 6-thienyl-pyrimidinone ring can intercalate into the stacking plane of the RNA double helix, while the right methylamino-piperidinyl group forms weak hydrogen bonds with key guanines, such multi-heterocyclic and complex structures are characteristic of many small-molecule drugs, this compound can highly specifically bind to the aptamer pocket of the bacterial FMN riboswitch, mimicking the conformation of the natural ligand FMN and blocking the binding of FMN to RNA, thereby relieving the negative feedback inhibition of the riboswitch on the riboflavin synthesis gene ribB, leading to impaired bacterial growth due to riboflavin depletion, and importantly, its activity can be completely reversed by exogenous riboflavin, meanwhile this compound is often designed to target specific protein kinases, by inhibiting key kinases, it may find applications in the treatment of cancer, inflammation, or autoimmune diseases.

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 1-[(4-chlorophenyl)-phenylmethyl]-4-methyl-1,4-diazepane, also referred to as Homochlorcyclizine (1-(4-chlorobenzhydryl)-4-methylhomopiperazine), is a derivative of 1,4-diazepane, bearing a (4-chlorophenyl)phenylmethyl group at the N1 position, a common motif found in established antihistamines (e.g., Cetirizine, Chlorcyclizine) and calcium channel blockers, which is a first-generation antihistamine of the diphenylmethane class originally marketed for allergic rhinitis and motion sickness through blockade of histamine H1 receptors, has been re-profiled as a modest inhibitor of HIV-1 replication as it unexpectedly binds to the internal loop of the HIV-1 Rev response element (RRE) subdomain IIB RNA and displaces the Rev3450 arginine-rich helix, thereby disrupting formation of the RevRRE ribonucleoprotein export complex, showing weak inhibition of full-length RRERev complex assembly in electrophoretic mobility-shift assays and antiviral activity against HIV-1 with no measurable cytotoxicity at relevant concentrations, and although less potent than its congener cyproheptadine, it defines a diphenylmethane antihistamine scaffold that specifically recognizes RNA loop IIB nucleotides and may serve as a starting point for developing RevRRE-targeting antivirals, with its potential activity also likely as a Histamine H1 receptor antagonist (antiallergic) or a calcium channel modulator for potential cardiovascular applications.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: [3-[3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-2-oxochromen-7-yl]-3-azabicyclo[3.1.0]hexan-6-yl]azanium, also referred to as SMSM52, is a non-planar coumarin analog and derivative with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system as well as a highly complex bicyclic substituent at position 7: the 3-azabicyclo[3.1.0]hexane group, which carries an azanium positive charge at the 6-carbon atom and makes the compound carry a 1 charge, this bicyclic structure, formed by the fusion of a five-membered ring and a three-membered (cyclopropane) ring, imparts exceptional rigidity and distinct three-dimensional specificity, such bridged-ring structures are often utilized in medicinal chemistry to mimic the secondary structure of peptides, aiming to improve oral bioavailability and metabolic stability, the quaternary ammonium center facilitates increased water solubility and affinity for targets like phospholipids or acidic protein sites, and the compound recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand, while the combination of the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group and the coumarin core suggests broad potential biological activity, for instance, as a highly selective anti-cancer agent with optimized pharmacokinetic properties.

RNA information: The structure of MAPT pre-mRNA contains a hairpin structure located at the junction of exon 10 and intron, which is crucial for splicing regulation. Under normal circumstances, the hairpin structure is stable and helps maintain the correct splicing ratio of exon 10. However, certain mutations (such as C+14U) can disrupt the stability of hairpins, leading to excessive inclusion of exon 10 and subsequently the production of 4R-tau protein, which is associated with the neurodegenerative disease FTDP-17. The article points out that small molecule compounds can promote the excretion of exon 10 by binding to and stabilizing the mutated hairpin structure, thereby restoring the normal proportion of tau protein and having potential therapeutic effects.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[4-(4-carbamimidoylanilino)phenyl]-1H-indole-6-carboximidamide is an indole bis-amidine derivative, containing an indole core, an aniline bridge, and two amidine groups. Indole compounds are widely found in natural products and possess various biological activities. The amidine groups impart a potential for nucleic acid binding, and the molecule is often developed as an antiprotozoal and antiviral agent. Bis-amidine-indole compounds with similar structures have been studied for the treatment of tropical diseases like African trypanosomiasis or as DNA minor groove binding probes.

RNA information: XIST is a long non-coding RNA (lncRNA) crucial for X-chromosome inactivation in female mammalian cells. It functions by coating the future inactive X chromosome and recruiting repressive complexes to silence gene expression. Structurally, XIST contains a non-canonical RNA G-quadruplex (rG4) motif composed of only two G-tetrads, making it less stable than canonical three-tetrad rG4s. In vitro studies, such as reverse transcriptase stalling (RTS) assays, confirm its ability to form an rG4 structure, showing approximately 14% stalling in potassium buffer. Notably, specific G4 ligands like PhenDC3 and PDS can act as molecular chaperones, significantly enhancing the formation and stability of the XIST rG4 structure under unfavorable ionic conditions, highlighting its dynamic nature and regulatory potential.
Small_molecule_information: 4-(2-aminoethoxy)-2-N,6-N-bis[4-(2-aminoethoxy)naphthalen-2-yl]pyridine-2,6-dicarboxamide, also referred to as PhenDC3, is a large, structurally symmetrical bisquinolinium G4 ligand (a pyridodicarboxamide derivative) with the molecular formula C37H37N5O5, centered on a pyridine-2,6-dicarboxamide core; this core is linked via two amide bonds to two identical naphthyl units, which are functionalized with aminoethoxy chains, and its structure further enables chemical derivatization (e.g., PhenDC3-alk for bioorthogonal manipulations). The molecule features multiple aromatic rings (pyridine and naphthyl) providing a hydrophobic, planar surface that allows it to bind rG4s via stacking on G-quartets, and four primary amine groups (-O-CH2CH2NH2) contributing to hydrophilicity and multiple positive charges; this combination of hydrophobicity and multiple positive charges is characteristic of amphiphilic molecules, often designed as DNA minor groove binders or transmembrane transport vectors, and it can interact with DNA bases via - stacking interactions while electrostatically attracting the negatively charged phosphate backbone. Functionally, it acts as a molecular chaperone, inducing folding of canonical (3-quartet, e.g., TERRA) and non-canonical (2-quartet, e.g., XIST) rG4s, stabilizes rG4s and impedes reverse transcriptase by promoting rG4 formation, aiding G4 biology studies, and consequently, this compound has potential as an anti-tumor or antiviral drug by interfering with the replication or transcription of nucleic acids.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: (2E,5E)-2-[(4-morpholin-4-ylphenyl)methylidene]-5-(pyridin-3-ylmethylidene)cyclopentan-1-one is a conjugated bis-enone or bis-chalcone derivative with the molecular formula C24H22N2O2. It is centered on a cyclopentanone core, linked via two (E)-configured double bonds to a morpholinophenyl group and a pyridinyl group. This molecular structure possesses an extended planar conjugated system, which is often key to high biological activity and fluorescent properties. The morpholine ring increases the molecules polarity and solubility, while the pyridine ring introduces an additional basic nitrogen atom capable of participation in hydrogen bonding or coordination. Chalcone-class compounds are known to have broad activities, including anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and antimicrobial effects. The activity of this compound likely stems from its ability to bind to biological targets (such as kinases or tubulin) or by acting as a Michael acceptor to undergo covalent modification with biological thiols.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-amino-4-(3-pyrrolidin-1-ylpropoxy)phenyl]-5,7-dimethoxy-1-methylquinolin-4-one has a side chain terminal amine group that is pyrrolidine (a five-membered ring tertiary amine). Compared to piperidine (a six-membered ring), pyrrolidine offers different ring strain and spatial arrangement. Functionally, its activity profile is based on the inhibitory effect of the quinolinone core and the lysosomal targeting conferred by the basic side chain. In practical applications, this structure is often used in Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) studies to determine which cyclic amine (pyrrolidine, piperidine, morpholine, etc.) provides the optimal potency and pharmacokinetic properties.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 2,3-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium, also referred to as Nitidine, is another methylated benzophenanthridine quaternary alkaloid, similar to 1,2-dimethoxy-12-methyl-[1,3]benzodioxolo[5,6-c]phenanthridin-12-ium but with different methoxy substitution (2,3-positions), and as a benzophenanthridine alkaloid it also possesses antitumor and antimalarial activity, can inhibit topoisomerases and telomerase to disrupt DNA replication, and its fluorescent properties aid in cellular imaging studies; due to its planar, rigid, and positively charged structural features, its primary activity in practice is as a DNA intercalator and cytotoxic agent, exhibiting strong anti-tumor properties, minor changes in the substitution pattern (from 1,2- to 2,3-dimethoxy) are critical in drug design to modulate the molecules charge distribution and steric bulk, which can alter its interaction strength and selectivity for specific DNA sequences or Topoisomerase isozymes, and this makes it valuable in the structure-activity relationship studies of novel anticancer agents.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GAC)x20: This is an expanded CAG repeat RNA with twenty AA mismatches, modeling the longer pathogenic repeats found in advanced stages of Huntington’s disease and SCAs. It shows strong binding to the lead compounds, with nanomolar affinity, and exhibits significant thermal stabilization and structural changes upon ligand binding, confirming its relevance as a therapeutic target.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-(4-methoxybenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 3, shares the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one core with 3-amino-9-bromo-2-(4-methoxybenzoyl)-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one but lacks the bromine atom, and Compound 3 emerged as a lead compound with high affinity and selectivity for r(CAG)exp RNA, its large aromatic and amino-substituted structure suggests high lipophilicity and the potential to interact strongly with biological targets, it is likely investigated as an antiproliferative agent targeting intracellular enzymes or signaling pathways, Compound 3 significantly reduced polyQ aggregation in cellular models, showed low cytotoxicity, and was cell-permeable, localizing primarily in the cytoplasm. RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (2S)-5-hydroxy-2-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxyphenyl)-7-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxy-2,3-dihydrochromen-4-one, also referred to as Hesperidin, is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects commonly found in citrus fruits, and is an extremely complex natural product flavonoid O-diglycoside with a dihydroflavone (flavanone) core substituted at the 7-position with a diglycoside unit (likely involving glucose and rhamnose); the numerous phenolic hydroxyl groups and the bulky glycoside moiety are the primary drivers of its activity, and in practice, such compounds are key functional components in herbal medicines, demonstrating potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging capabilities, while due to its glycosidic structure, it typically has high water solubility and specific oral bioavailability (via gut microbial hydrolysis), and it is widely studied for cardiovascular benefits as well as cardioprotective, antiviral, and hepatoprotective effects, making it a focal point in natural product research.

RNA information: The r(CAG)₁₀ sequence serves as a comparative motif to test ligand selectivity against repeat RNAs with A–A mismatches instead of U–U mismatches. Structurally, it forms A-form helical RNA with alternating positive and negative electrostatic stripes similar to CUG repeats, leading to comparable overall architectures. Functionally, r(CAG)₁₀ is not associated with myotonic dystrophy but provides a stringent control for assessing molecular recognition. Because many proteins and synthetic ligands show similar affinity toward both CUG and CAG repeats, this sequence demonstrates the challenges of engineering true sequence selectivity in RNA-targeted small-molecule design.
Small_molecule_information: (Z,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-2-(propylamino)propanoyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 6 (2012), is the (Z)-geometric isomer (cis) of the oligo-peptide analogue (a Z-isomer lacking the benzo[g]quinoline moiety that was designed as a control to evaluate the importance of this heteroaromatic group), meaning it possesses the same connectivity but the alkene bond within the core chain has a different spatial arrangement; this minor change in geometry dictates a profound difference in the molecules overall three-dimensional shape and flexibility, and additionally, without the benzo[g]quinoline moiety, the compound maintains only weak interactions with RNA and loses significant selectivity, which confirms that the quinoline system is essential for high-affinity and discriminating recognition of repeat motifs, structurally it emphasizes the critical contribution of aromatic stacking in RNA binding, and functionally it has no significant biological activity, underscoring its role as a negative control and supporting the rationale for incorporating benzo[g]quinoline in active derivatives; in real-world drug discovery, geometric isomerism is a critical factor, as biological targets are highly sensitive to ligand shape; therefore, (Z,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-2-(propylamino)propanoyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide (also referred to as DCC 6 (2012)) is expected to exhibit drastically different biological activity, potency, and target selectivity compared to its (E)-isomer, potentially acting on entirely distinct biological pathways, and is essential for structure-activity relationship studies.

RNA information: 5′CGG/3′GGC is a 1×1 GG internal-loop RNA motif of the 5'CNG/3'GNC family, featuring CGG/GCC pairing, no U·U mismatches, high stability, and low "breathing effects". Linked to FXTAS (mutant FMR1 mRNA’s r(CGG) repeats), it acts as a disease-specific RNA switch, recruiting ligands like D2, D6, and 2H-5 . It also serves as a minimal binding unit for affinity characterization and an experimental control for RNA-small molecule binding specificity.
Small_molecule_information: methyl 7-hydroxy-6-(hydroxymethylidene)-4,9-dioxo-10H-phenazine-1-carboxylate, also referred to as lomofungin, is a derivative of phenazine, a core structure found in natural pigments with anthraquinone-like features, substituted with hydroxyl, keto, and methyl carboxylate groups; it is a natural antimicrobial agent produced by Streptomyces lomondensis that undergoes spontaneous dimerization in DMSO to form dilomofungin, and in real activity, phenazine compounds are well-known for their redox activity, enabling them to generate reactive oxygen species, this compound has potential antimicrobial activity as certain phenazine natural products are potent antibiotics, while it has also been identified as a potent inhibitor of MBNL1-CUG RNA binding, binds selectively to RNA hairpins containing CUG repeats particularly those with UU mismatches and disrupts the interaction between MBNL1 protein and pathogenic CUGexp RNA, in cellular models of myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1) it partially restores MBNL1-dependent alternative splicing and increases the diffuse nucleoplasmic localization of MBNL1 indicating functional reversal of RNA toxicity, though its dimeric form dilomofungin, while more potent in vitro, unexpectedly stabilizes CUGexp RNA and leads to its accumulation in nuclear foci highlighting a potential limitation in the therapeutic use of high-affinity RNA ligands, and these findings suggest that it represents a promising scaffold for developing small-molecule therapies against DM1 provided that off-target effects on RNA turnover are carefully managed.

RNA information: TARh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide used as a control. Its structural feature is that it lacks the characteristic internal loop of the HIV-1 RRE subdomain IIB, but contains the UCU bulge structure that binds to HIV-1 Tat. Functionally, it is mainly used as a control in experiments such as SPR. By comparing its binding with target RNA (e.g., ⅡBh), it evaluates the binding specificity of compounds to ⅡBh and assists in distinguishing non-specific binding from specific interactions.; TARh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide used as a control. Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-(2-aminoethyl)-5-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2-methoxyphenyl]-2-(cyclohexylmethoxy)phenyl]ethanamine, also referred to as Compound 5d (a 3,5,2,6-tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) terphenyl compound with a single methoxy group at the 2 position of the central benzene ring), is a derivative of 2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2-methoxyphenyl]phenol, where the phenol is substituted with a cyclohexylmethoxy group, the compound was synthesized via sequential palladium-catalyzed Suzuki couplings and characterized by methods like NMR and SPR, the cyclohexyl group is a saturated alicyclic ring that can significantly increase the molecules lipophilicity but removes the possibility of - stacking, as a tetracationic polyamine, this increase in lipophilicity may enhance its interaction with lipid-rich environments (such as the interior of cell membranes), functionally, Compound 5d binds to the hairpin RNA (Bh) of HIV-1 RRE subdomain IIB with low binding affinity and has corresponding binding specificity relative to control hairpins GGh and TARh, shows higher IC in inhibiting the Bh-Rev interaction and no antiviral activity in cell assays, while such derivatives may be explored for targeting membrane-associated proteins or interfering with lipid metabolism pathways, for instance, as novel antiviral or antifungal agents.

RNA information: HCV IRES subdomain IIa is an evolutionarily conserved RNA structural element that can specifically bind to benzimidazole-class small molecules. It controls the homeostasis of viral protein synthesis by modulating the conformation of the internal ribosome entry site, thereby regulating cap-independent translation initiation.
Small_molecule_information: 6-[3-(dimethylamino)propoxy]-1-[3-(dimethylamino)propyl]benzimidazol-2-amine, also referred to as Benzimidazole 3, is a compound that has a benzimidazole core with two dimethylaminoalkoxy/alkyl side chains, and Benzimidazole 3 is a small-molecule ligand engineered to selectively recognize and bind the subdomain IIa of the hepatitis C virus (HCV) internal ribosome entry site (IRES), an RNA structural element essential for cap-independent viral translation, such structures are often explored in medicinal chemistry for their antifungal, antiviral, or antiprotozoal activity, the benzimidazole motif is similar to natural purines, allowing it to interfere with microbial nucleic acid metabolism, making it a potential antiparasitic or antimicrobial drug, structurally, it is based on a 2-aminobenzimidazole core that is substituted at the N1 and C6 positions with flexible cationic side-chains; these protonatable amines mimic two divalent-metal hot spots detected in the native RNA bulge, allowing Benzimidazole 3 to dock into the conserved internal loop with notable affinity and 10-fold selectivity over non-target RNAs, upon binding, the ligand displaces key purine bases (A6, A7) that normally stack within the bulge, straightening the 90 inter-helical bend characteristic of free subdomain IIa; this conformational switch mis-aligns the downstream IIb hairpin, preventing its correct positioning on the 40S ribosomal E-site and thereby blocking eIF2-GDP release and 80S ribosome assembly, in HCV replicon assays Benzimidazole 3 reduces viral RNA levels with notable potency while exhibiting minimal cytotoxicity, and its potency is maintained in infectious JFH-1 luciferase-reporter systems, because it acts upstream of all protein-coding genes, Benzimidazole 3 offers a conserved, genotype-independent target and provides a validated scaffold for structure-guided optimization toward novel direct-acting antivirals for hepatitis C.

RNA information: The HIV-1 frameshift-stimulating stem-loop (FSS) RNA is a highly conserved structured element located within the gag-pol transcript that regulates production of the essential Gag-Pol polyprotein through a −1 ribosomal frameshift. It consists of a stable stem-loop positioned downstream of a slippery sequence that induces ribosomal pausing and promotes frameshifting in about 5–10% of translation events. This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Altering FSS activity disrupts the Gag to Gag-Pol ratio, substantially reducing viral replication and infectivity.
Small_molecule_information: N-[(2S)-1-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-4-[4-[(2S)-3-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]-methylcarbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-2-[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)-methylamino]-3-oxopropyl]triazol-1-yl]-1-oxobutan-2-yl]-2-ethyl-N-methylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carboxamide, also referred to as Compound 3, is a complex, linear peptidic macromolecule which is a triazole-based derivative with enhanced stability and strong binding affinity for the HIV-1 FSS RNA stem loop, was previously established as a potent modulator of ribosomal frameshifting, altering Gag-Pol levels consistent with increased frameshift frequency, inhibits both laboratory and multidrug-resistant HIV-1 strains, demonstrating antiviral efficacy in cell-based assays, and despite its high molecular weight and charged groups which raise concerns about drug-like properties, remains a benchmark molecule for FSS-targeted RNA binding with the highest affinity among the studied compounds; its structure contains multiple chiral amino acid residues (e.g., Pro, Phe derivatives), a triazole linking unit, and a benzo[g]quinoline-3-carboxamide group, and in terms of biological function, compounds with such complex structures are usually inhibitors or ligands targeting specific biological targets, potentially exhibiting anticancer, antiviral, or signal pathway regulatory activities.

RNA information: The miR-183 precursor is a ~70-nt stem-loop RNA that is co-transcribed with miR-96 and miR-182 from chromosome 7q32.2 as a single pri-miRNA cluster. Unlike riboswitches, no natural small-molecule ligand has been identified that selectively binds the pre-miR-183 hairpin to regulate its own processing; instead, its biogenesis is governed by canonical Drosha/Dicer cleavage and by upstream transcriptional factors such as β-catenin/TCF, GSK3β, MYCN and PI3K/AKT . Thus, while bacterial riboswitches like B. subtilis 165ribD use direct FMN binding to maintain FMN/riboflavin homeostasis, miR-183 homeostasis is achieved through transcriptional and post-transcriptional control of its precursor rather than through ligand-induced structural switching.
Small_molecule_information: N-(3-azidopropyl)-4-[2,6-ditert-butyl-4-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as Benzimidazole 1 (Velagapudi et al., 2014), is a simplified mono-benzimidazole scaffold containing the tert-butyl and azide groups, which is a small-molecule mimic of the FMN riboswitch ligand rationally designed to bind a Drosha-site hairpin in the human pre-miR-96 precursor instead of a bacterial RNA; by replacing the ribityl-phosphate tail of FMN with a 4-(3-aminopropyl)-benzimidazole core, the compound gains chemical stability and enhanced selectivity for the miR-96 stem-loop over other miRNA precursors, can bind the precursor with high affinity in vitro, block Drosha cleavage, reduce mature miR-96 levels, thereby restoring FOXO1 tumor-suppressor expression and inducing apoptosis in breast-cancer cell lines, and in mouse xenograft models, daily oral dosing can decrease tumor growth without measurable gut-microbiota perturbation, indicating it or its analogs may serve as precision antibacterial-sparing agents for miRNA-driven cancers, while its real-world activity is designed as a Click Chemistry probe for bioconjugation and target identification, and such probes are critical for studying the pharmacodynamics and metabolism of new drug candidates by allowing tracking of the molecule and identification of its binding partners in biological systems.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 9-ethyl-3-[2-(4-piperidin-1-ylquinolin-2-yl)ethenyl]carbazole, also referred to as Compound 32 (a green-yellow 1-methylquinolinium derivative), is a large aromatic molecule formed by linking a carbazole core and a quinoline unit via an ethenyl (alkene) bridge, with the quinoline being substituted at the 4-position with a 4-(2-methoxyethyl)piperidin-1-yl group and featuring a 2-(2-(9-ethyl-9H-carbazol-3-yl)vinyl) group at the quinoline 2-position; this structure defines a classic carbazole-quinoline fluorophore with an extended -conjugation system, exhibiting strong and often environmentally sensitive fluorescence, and in terms of its biological activity, SPR assay reveals its affinity for A10-RNA-Wt is lower than that of Compound 24, it was not subjected to the dual-luciferase reporter assay so its impact on ADAM10 translation remains unreported, and no clear cytotoxicity data (IC values) is provided for it in the tested compound tables, while in real applications, it is widely used as a fluorescent probe or dye for bioimaging and cell staining, primarily due to its ability to bind to DNA or RNA through a combination of stacking and electrostatic interactions, and it may also be investigated for antineoplastic activity via DNA intercalation or topoisomerase inhibition.

RNA information: The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).; The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).·
Small_molecule_information: trimethyl-[2-[[5-methyl-11-(3-morpholin-4-ylpropylamino)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]ethyl]azanium diiodide. This is a dicationic benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium derivative with a diiodide counterion. The side chain at position 7 is a trimethylazanium group (shorter than trimethyl-[3-[[5-methyl-11-(3-morpholin-4-ylpropylamino)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]propyl]azanium diiodide by one carbon), and the substituent at position 11 is 3-morpholin-4-ylpropylamino. Changes in side chain length significantly impact the compounds binding mode and affinity for biological targets, such as the minor groove of DNA, thereby fine-tuning its bioactivity. This compound is investigated as a fluorescent probe and a novel chemotherapeutic agent. Due to the presence of quaternary ammonium groups, it exhibits direct bactericidal effects on bacteria and fungi, and could be used to develop new generation antifungal or antibacterial drugs.

RNA information: The NRQ is a 18-nucleotide RNA oligomer corresponding to the wild-type, guanine-rich sequence (5′-GGGAGGGGCGGGUCUGGG-3′) located in the 5′-UTR of the human NRAS proto-oncogene mRNA. This sequence possesses the intrinsic ability to fold into a stable, parallel RNA G-quadruplex structure, which has been previously established to act as a translational repressor by inhibiting the initiation of NRAS protein synthesis. In this study, it serves as the primary target structure for the novel MSQ derivatives. Its confirmed G-quadruplex formation, characterized by a specific CD spectrum with a positive peak at 262 nm that is enhanced in the presence of potassium ions, and its functional role make it essential for evaluating the binding affinity and specificity of potential ligands designed to stabilize this structure and further repress translation.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-N-(2-morpholin-4-ylethyl)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-amine, also referred to as a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline with the p-(methylthio)styryl group at the 2-position and a (2-morpholinoethyl)amino side chain at the 11-position, features the 2-morpholin-4-ylethylamine side chain with a short ethylamine spacer, this structure is commonly used to balance the lipophilicity and water solubility of drug molecules, morpholine-substituted quinoline derivatives are investigated for activity in the central nervous system and as anticancer agents, the design of this compound aims to optimize its pharmacokinetic properties and bioavailability through the morpholine side chain, and it showed strong binding affinity for the NRQ as well as potent anti-proliferative activity in most cancer cell lines including notable activity in A375 cells.

This process allows translation to continue beyond the gag stop codon, producing Gag-Pol proteins such as protease, reverse transcriptase, and integrase that are required for infectious virion assembly. Small_molecule_information: 3-[[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-[(Z,2S,7S)-8-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-azaniumylpropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]carbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-2,7-bis[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]-8-oxooct-4-enoyl]pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]-3-phenylpropanoyl]amino]propylazanium is the cis (Z) isomer of 3-[[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-[(E,2S,7S)-8-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-azaniumylpropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]carbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-2,7-bis[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]-8-oxooct-4-enoyl]pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]-3-phenylpropanoyl]amino]propylazanium, with the double bond in the core oct-4-enediamide scaffold having the cis configuration. This results in a distinct, fixed bend in the molecular structure. The molecule retains all the complex features of its trans counterpart, including the poly-cationic nature and multiple aromatic groups. The change in conformation, dictated by the cis double bond, will profoundly affect the molecules three-dimensional shape and rigidity, and consequently, its interaction with large biological targets. 3-[[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-[(Z,2S,7S)-8-[(2S)-2-[[(2S)-1-(3-azaniumylpropylamino)-1-oxo-3-phenylpropan-2-yl]carbamoyl]pyrrolidin-1-yl]-2,7-bis[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]-8-oxooct-4-enoyl]pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]-3-phenylpropanoyl]amino]propylazanium is a crucial molecule for investigating the structure-activity relationship (SAR) in complex peptidomimetics, particularly for achieving a unique binding mode to the target.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: 6-amino-4-oxo-1-[3-(pyridin-2-ylamino)propyl]-7-(4-pyridin-2-ylpiperazin-1-yl)quinoline-3-carboxylicacid This is a quinoline-3-carboxylic acid derivative featuring a 4-pyridin-2-ylpiperazine substituent at position 7, which lends the molecule considerable basicity and steric bulk. Its substituent at the N1 position is a 3-(pyridin-2-ylamino)propyl chain. Compared to the double-piperazine analogues, this structure employs a simpler secondary amine-linked pyridine ring at N1. This structural simplification is intended to optimize the molecules binding geometry within the target binding pocket and potentially alter its metabolic rate. Based on its quinolone core, this compound may be researched for antimicrobial or anti-cancer applications, with the side-chain design tailored to explore its selectivity for specific receptors or enzymes.
