Small_molecule_information: (2S)-5-hydroxy-2-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxyphenyl)-7-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxy-2,3-dihydrochromen-4-one, also referred to as Hesperidin, is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects commonly found in citrus fruits, and is an extremely complex natural product flavonoid O-diglycoside with a dihydroflavone (flavanone) core substituted at the 7-position with a diglycoside unit (likely involving glucose and rhamnose); the numerous phenolic hydroxyl groups and the bulky glycoside moiety are the primary drivers of its activity, and in practice, such compounds are key functional components in herbal medicines, demonstrating potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging capabilities, while due to its glycosidic structure, it typically has high water solubility and specific oral bioavailability (via gut microbial hydrolysis), and it is widely studied for cardiovascular benefits as well as cardioprotective, antiviral, and hepatoprotective effects, making it a focal point in natural product research.

RNA information: pre-miRNA-210, the precursor of miR-210, has a hairpin structure and contains a Dicer processing site (5' ACU 3'/3' UCA 5'), which can be cleaved by Dicer enzyme to generate mature miR-210. Its core function is regulating hypoxia-related pathways: mature miR-210 inhibits GPD1L expression, impairs PHD activity, preventing HIF-1 from degradation and activating downstream pro-cancer transcriptional responses. It is significantly upregulated under hypoxic conditions and closely associated with tumor metastasis.
Small_molecule_information: N-(3-azidopropyl)-4-[3-[6-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as Targapremir-210 (a bis-benzimidazole small molecule from which derivatives containing chlorambucil cross-linking module and biotin purification module can be synthesized), is an azide-tagged bis-benzimidazole probe (without tert-butyl groups); the subtle difference in the linkers attachment point (meta-position) is an important factor in structure-activity relationship (SAR) studies, and functionally it binds to the Dicer processing site of pre-miR-210, inhibiting its processing into mature miR-210, upregulates GPD1L, downregulates HIF-1, induces apoptosis of hypoxic breast cancer cells, inhibits tumor growth, has good selectivity and tumor localization ability, while its real-world activity is as a Click Chemistry probe used to investigate how minor structural changes influence the molecules DNA binding affinity and selectivity.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 4-amino-1-[(2R,5S)-2-(hydroxymethyl)-1,3-oxathiolan-5-yl]pyrimidin-2-one, also referred to as Lamivudine (3TC) or its enantiomer, is a synthetic cytidine nucleoside analog where the 3 oxygen atom of the ribose ring is replaced by a sulfur atom, forming a 1,3-oxathiolane sugar ring, molecular formula C8H11N3O3S; it is a nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor (NRTI) that incorporates into viral DNA, terminating its synthesis, and in terms of real-world activity, Lamivudine is an extremely important antiviral drug, classified as a Nucleoside Reverse Transcriptase Inhibitor (NRTI), which is widely used clinically for the treatment of Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) infection and Hepatitis B Virus (HBV) infection, with its mechanism of action involving being phosphorylated intracellularly to its active triphosphate form, which is then incorporated into the viral DNA chain, causing chain termination and effectively inhibiting the activity of the HIV reverse transcriptase and HBV polymerase.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(6-methoxy-8-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyrazin-2-yl)-7-[(3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl]chromen-2-one, also referred to as C42, is a complex coumarin derivative (C42 is a non-planar coumarin analog) with a piperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge, structurally similar to its non-planar analog form but with more precise substitutions; it features a chiral quaternary ammonium piperazinium group ((3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium) at the 7-position and a 6-methoxy-8-methyl-substituted imidazo[1,2-a]pyrazine at the 3-position, and it can recognize the single-G bulge of RNA1, driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand, while the introduction of a chiral center is critical in medicinal chemistry, as it can significantly influence the drugs binding specificity and pharmacological effect; this molecule combines a cationic fluorophore with a complex heterocyclic structure, often investigated as a highly selective fluorescent probe, and in drug development, such molecules are explored for their unique structure and potential anticancer or neurological activity, with the chiral center aiming to optimize selectivity for its target.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 17-methoxy-N-(2-phenylethyl)-5,7-dioxa-13-azoniapentacyclo[11.8.0.0{2,10}.0{4,8}.0{15,20}]henicosa-1(13),2,4(8),9,14,16,18,20-octaen-16-amine chloride This complex quaternary ammonium salt features a rigid, highly conjugated pentacyclic core and a lipophilic N-(2-phenylethyl)amine side chain. The molecules large, positively charged structure and its amphiphilic nature (hydrophilic cation head, hydrophobic phenylethyl tail) suggest strong interactions with biological membranes and negatively charged biopolymers like DNA. Real-world applications for compounds with this skeleton often focus on developing broad-spectrum antimicrobial or antifungal agents that disrupt microbial cell integrity, or as DNA intercalators/Topoisomerase inhibitors in anticancer research. The phenylethyl moiety also hints at potential neuro-modulatory activity.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-benzoyl-4-thiophen-2-ylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 4, is a heterocyclic compound based on the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold. The key structural distinction lies in the substituent at position 4: a thiophen-2-yl group replaces the typical phenyl group. Thiophene is a five-membered sulfur-containing aromatic heterocycle, and its incorporation is a strategy of bioisosteric replacement, used to modify the substituents electronic effects and spatial conformation while retaining aromaticity. This structural adjustment is aimed at optimizing the molecular fit with the binding pocket of a target protein (e.g., a kinase). The large planar polycyclic structure of this compound still suggests its potential as a DNA intercalator or enzyme inhibitor, and featuring a thiophene substitution, it displayed moderate RNA-binding activity, which was less potent than the lead compounds but still showed specificity for CAG repeat RNA over control sequences. In practical applications, thiophene-containing drugs are often studied for anti-inflammatory, anti-tumor, and Central Nervous System diseases.

RNA information: B4-25 is a 100-nucleotide RNA aptamer isolated from a random-sequence pool by Ellington & Szostak through in vitro selection against Reactive Blue 4; it contains a 28-nt highly conserved core that folds into a stem–loop–stem motif predicted by GCG-FOLD, presenting a pre-organized pocket whose planar, negatively charged surface complements the anthraquinone and sulfonate groups of the dye, achieving ~600 fM affinity and >50 % column retention with virtually no cross-binding to other dyes.
Small_molecule_information: 1-amino-4-[4-[[4-chloro-6-(4-sulfonatoanilino)-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl]amino]-3-sulfonatoanilino]-9,10-dioxoanthracene-2-sulfonate, also referred to as Cibacron Blue 3G-A, is extremely similar in structure to 1-amino-4-[3-[[4-chloro-6-(3-sulfonatoanilino)-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl]amino]-4-sulfonatoanilino]-9,10-dioxoanthracene-2-sulfonate, also being a trisulfonate salt containing an anthraquinone dye core, and Cibacron Blue 3G-A is the dye employed in the EllingtonSzostak selection, is an anthraquinone-derived organosulfonate that carries two sulfonate and one reactive chlorotriazine groups, the molecules main activities focus on serving as a dye or fluorescent reagent, or non-specific inhibitor of nucleic acids or enzymes in biology, its planar, amphiphilic scaffold mimics the ADP-ribose fold of NAD(P), allowing the 22-nt conserved domain of the CB-42 RNA aptamer to wrap around the dye through a combination of stacking, hydrogen bonding and electrostatic interactions that yield extremely high binding affinity. Small_molecule_information: 7-(1,4-diazepan-4-ium-1-yl)-3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)chromen-2-one, also referred to as SMSM55, is a non-planar coumarin derivative where the substituent at position 7 is the 1,4-diazepan-4-ium-1-yl group, carrying a 1 charge and possessing a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system; this is a seven-membered heterocycle containing two nitrogen atoms, with one of the nitrogens bearing a positive charge, the flexibility of the seven-membered ring aids in accommodating various binding pockets, and the two nitrogen atoms provide additional sites for hydrogen bond donation or acceptance, the quaternary ammonium positive charge is a key feature for improving water solubility and electrostatic binding, the combination of the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group and the coumarin core suggests its potential as a specific kinase inhibitor or receptor ligand, and it can recognize the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, thus serving as a selective RNA ligand, these molecules are often studied as candidates for antipsychotic drugs or anti-cancer agents, given the broad activity profile of the diazepane structure.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[6-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[6-[4-[3-[6-[6-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoylamino]hexyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-HD, is a highly complex bis-benzimidazole dimer (double-headed molecule) where two bis-benzimidazole units are connected by a long, flexible hexamethylene (hexyl) linker, and 2H-HD is based on a hexanediamine spacing motif, making it more extended than ED or BD analogues, dimerization is a strategy used to increase binding affinity or achieve bivalent targeting, this dimer is designed to be an extremely potent DNA minor groove binder, potentially spanning several base pairs, it showed comparable moderate potency to 2H-BD, again suggesting that small changes in linker length influence the ability to bind structured RNA repeats, its potency was lower than the most potent oligoglycine-based derivatives, its potential activity is as a highly effective antineoplastic or antiparasitic agent, cellular assays and further optimization were not reported for this compound, and the molecule remains part of the initial comparative analysis.

RNA information: (CUG)₁₆ is a synthetic RNA oligonucleotide mimicking pathogenic expanded (CUG)ₙ repeats in myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). Structurally, it folds into a stable A-form hairpin with repeating 5'CUG/3'GUC units, featuring U-U mismatches flanked by G-C base pairs. Functionally, it high-affinity binds Muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1), sequestering it to disrupt alternative splicing (DM1’s core gain-of-function mechanism) and serving in vitro for cleavage assays to screen catalytic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: 1-N,4-N-bis[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butyl]benzene-1,4-dicarboximidamide, also referred to as Bisamidinium 1a, combines bis-amidine and triazine structures, which is a monomeric ligand designed with a bisamidinium groove binder and two triaminotriazine recognition units; the diamino substituent on the triazine ring gives it structural characteristics of a folic acid antagonist, similar to inhibitors of dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR); it is a weak inhibitor of the MBNL1-rCUGexp complex, shows low cytotoxicity and can partially disrupt nuclear foci and correct the IR splicing defect at high concentration, improves disease phenotypes in a dose-dependent manner in the DM1 Drosophila model (though requiring a much higher concentration to achieve an effect comparable to dimer 2a) and is well-tolerated in mice at high doses, and the molecule might be developed as an antineoplastic or antimicrobial agent by interfering with the folate metabolic pathway to inhibit the growth of rapidly proliferating cells or pathogens.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[[4-[(2-amino-4-oxo-7,8-dihydro-3H-pteridin-6-yl)methylamino]benzoyl]amino]pentanedioic acid, also referred to as Dihydrofolic acid (DHF), is commonly known as Folic acid, or Vitamin B9, with the molecular formula C19H19N7O6, and Dihydrofolic acid (DHF) is a folate derivative with a structure comprising a pteridine ring, p-aminobenzoic acid (PABA), and glutamate moiety, lacking one reduced hydrogen pair vs. tetrahydrofolate (THF), its structure comprises a pteridine ring, a p-aminobenzoic acid (PABA) residue, and a glutamic acid residue, its pteridine core enables one-carbon transfer reactions, in terms of real-world activity, Folic acid is an essential water-soluble vitamin for humans and many organisms, it is reduced in vivo to the active form, Tetrahydrofolate (THF), which serves as a one-carbon unit carrier, participating in vital biochemical reactions, including the synthesis of nucleic acid precursors like purines (A and G) and thymidine (T), as well as amino acid metabolism, functionally, DHF acts as a key intermediate in folate metabolism, converted to THF by dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR), it supports nucleotide biosynthesis and amino acid metabolism, clinically, Folic acid is used for the treatment of megaloblastic anemia and is crucially administered during early pregnancy to prevent fetal neural tube defects, the folic acid metabolic pathway is also a key target for many anticancer drugs (e.g., Methotrexate) and antimicrobial agents, and its analogs are used as anticancer/antibacterial agents by inhibiting DHFR to disrupt cellular proliferation.

RNA information: r(GGCC)4 is a short, double-stranded RNA motif composed of four tandem GGCC repeats that adopts a fully base-paired hairpin structure. It is commonly used in competition assays as a negative control because it lacks internal loops or single-stranded regions and therefore does not bind to RNA-targeted small molecules such as CB253, which selectively recognize the hairpin form of r(G4C2)exp RNA .
Small_molecule_information: 4-(5-phenylfuran-2-yl)benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Compound 1, is a compound containing a mono-imidamide group and a furan-phenyl structure, with the formula C17H14N2O; it binds to both AU and GC base pairs with high affinity but lacks selectivity, also recognizing RNA internal loops in disease repeats, making it non-ideal for specific targeting. As a mono-cationic imidamide derivative, its biological activity is distinct from diamidines; it likely lacks the potent DNA minor groove binding of its dicationic counterparts. Such compounds are often researched as enzyme inhibitors. In real-world applications, mono-imidamidines are frequently developed as serine protease inhibitors (such as thrombin or trypsin) for potential use in anticoagulant or anti-inflammatory therapy.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 4-(1H-benzimidazol-2-yl)benzenecarbothioamide, also referred to as 2-(4-Thiocarbamoylphenyl)benzimidazole, is a combination of benzimidazole and carbothioamide structures, and 2-(4-Thiocarbamoylphenyl)benzimidazole is a heterocyclic small molecule with a benzimidazole core linked to a thiocarbamoyl-substituted phenyl group, the benzimidazole core is often associated with antiparasitic, antifungal, antiviral, and antineoplastic activities, the carbothioamide group may enable the molecule to act as a metal chelator or enzyme inhibitor, the structure suggests the molecule may serve as a broad-spectrum antimicrobial agent, acting on cell division or specific enzyme systems, unlike myricetin, it lacks reported affinity for CAG-repeat RNAs in HD/SCAs but may interact with structured RNAs via benzimidazole intercalation and thiocarbamoyl H-bonding, it shows no validated activity against polyQ aggregation or mitochondrial dysfunction in HD models, though its benzimidazole motif could potentially target nucleic acid mismatches, making it a speculative scaffold for TRED-focused optimization with further biophysical validation needed. -

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 8-cycloheptyl-1,3,7-trimethylpurine-2,6-dione, also referred to as CP5 which contains a cycloheptane ring connected to a complex imidazolinone and dimethylurea heterocyclic system, is a xanthine derivative similar to caffeine; CP5 has a bulky, multi-heterocyclic structure that showed binding but was likely hampered by poor specificity, preventing its selection for detailed study, while this compound may have bronchodilator, myocardial stimulant, or central nervous system modulatory activity, and is often explored in drug development as a new phosphodiesterase (PDE) inhibitor.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[(7-chloroquinolin-4-yl)amino]-6-(diethylaminomethyl)phenol;dihydrochloride, also referred to as Quinacrine Di HCl, is a quinoline-phenol heterocyclic compound presented as the dihydrochloride salt, which is an antimalarial and antiprotozoal drug with DNA intercalation properties and has been repurposed for cancer and prion diseases due to its ability to inhibit protein aggregation and nucleic acid interactions, its structure featuring a 4-aminoquinoline backbone with a 7-chloro substitution is chemically analogous to classic antimalarial drugs like chloroquine, the presence of both a phenolic hydroxyl group and a diethylaminomethyl group on the phenyl ring provides multiple sites for interaction and protonation, quinolines are a broad class of compounds known for antimalarial, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, due to its salt form the molecule is highly protonated enhancing its water solubility and the functional groups suggest potential for metal ion chelation, in reality this compound is likely explored as a novel antimalarial candidate to combat drug resistance or possibly as an anticancer agent due to its potential to interfere with metal homeostasis or kinase activity.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]-N-[2-[[(2R)-2-[[(2R)-2-[[(2R)-2-[[(2R)-2-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoyl-propylamino]propanoyl]-propylamino]propanoyl]-propylamino]propanoyl]-propylamino]propanoyl]amino]ethyl]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-4NPr with four N-propyl spacers, is a polymeric peptidomimetic based on the benzimidazole-DNA binding unit, 2H-4NPr displayed good potency in TR-FRET experiments while this activity was reasonable but not highlighted for strong cellular performance, and it illustrated that linker extension beyond three NPr units does not yield further improvements and can slightly reduce cellular relevance, remaining part of the comparative analysis and not prioritized for development; this compounds structural feature is the inclusion of four repeating D-alanine-amide units, linked via an ethylenediamine to the butyramide core, the purpose of these repeating units is to elongate the molecule, allowing it to better accommodate or span the DNA minor groove, and to act as a controllable flexible spacer that regulates the spatial distance and orientation of the benzimidazole active groups, due to the multitude of benzimidazole and piperazine groups, this molecule exhibits strong polycationic character and high DNA binding affinity, thus, this molecule is designed as an efficient nucleic acid-targeting drug or probe with potential applications as an anti-cancer or antiviral agent due to its ability to interfere with genetic information.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: S-[2-[3-[[(2R)-4-[[[(2R,3S,4R,5R)-5-(6-aminopurin-9-yl)-4-hydroxy-3-phosphonooxyoxolan-2-yl]methoxy-hydroxyphosphoryl]oxy-hydroxyphosphoryl]oxy-2-hydroxy-3,3-dimethylbutanoyl]amino]propanoylamino]ethyl] ethanethioate, also referred to as Acetyl-CoA, is an extremely complex coenzyme molecule built upon biological structural fragments such as adenine, ribose, pyrophosphate, pantothenic acid, and cysteamine; the core part of the molecule is the Coenzyme A backbone whose terminal thiol group is acetylated to form a thioester bond, and unlike azaaromatic ligands it lacks planar hydrophobic heterocycles and therefore does not bind tightly to the yjdF motif RNA or activate riboswitch-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, while physiologically it functions as a pivotal metabolite extensively involved in the TCA cycle, fatty acid metabolism, and numerous acetylation reactions.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 3,5-diamino-6-chloro-N-(diaminomethylidene)pyrazine-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Amiloride, is a well-known potassium-sparing diuretic that blocks epithelial sodium channels (ENaC) in the kidneys, with the molecule acting by reversibly blocking the epithelial sodium channel (ENaC) in the renal collecting ducts, thereby reducing sodium reabsorption and fluid retention. Clinically, Amiloride is used to treat hypertension, congestive heart failure, and edema, and is often combined with other diuretics to prevent hypokalemia.

RNA information: The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).; The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).·
Small_molecule_information: 3-[[11-[2-(dimethylamino)ethylamino]-5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]propyl-trimethylazanium diiodide, this compound is a benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with long diamine and quaternary ammonium salt side chains. The presence of the quaternary ammonium salt typically confers higher polarity and DNA binding affinity, making it a potential potent anti-tumor agent or a fluorescent probe in biological research.

Small_molecule_information: 4-[5-(1,2,4-triazol-1-ylmethyl)-1H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl]pyridine is a poly-nitrogen heterocyclic compound featuring two 1,2,4-triazole rings and one pyridine ring. One 1,2,4-triazole ring (connected to the pyridine ring) serves as the core, while the other 1,2,4-triazole ring is linked to the 5-position of the core triazole ring via a methylene bridge. The triazole ring is an important pharmacophore, widely present in antifungal drugs and antivirals. This molecule is rich in nitrogen atoms, granting it strong hydrogen bonding and coordination capabilities, suggesting potential biological activities such as antifungal, antiviral, or anticancer effects, or applications as a metal coordinating agent.

RNA information: The influenza A virus RNA promoter features a bent A-form helical structure with a dynamic (A–A)–U internal loop that serves as a specific binding site for the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) and nucleoprotein complex. This unique structural motif exhibits conformational flexibility that facilitates recognition by viral replication machinery while maintaining overall A-form geometry. The promoter's distinctive architecture and dynamic properties are essential for initiating viral RNA synthesis, making it a critical functional element in the influenza replication cycle and a promising target for antiviral therapeutic development; the influenza A virus RNA promoter is a highly conserved structural element located at the 3' and 5' ends of each viral RNA segment. Its structure, determined by NMR spectroscopy, adopts a panhandle-like conformation characterized by an A-form helix. A key structural feature is a unique (A-A)-U internal loop and a C-A mismatch pair. Furthermore, the promoter exhibits a significant bend of approximately 46 ± 10° near the viral RNA initiation site. Functionally, this specific architecture is specifically recognized by the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp), which binds to it to initiate both transcription and replication of the viral genome. The high conservation of its sequence across strains and its intolerance to mutations, which are detrimental to viral replication, establish the influenza RNA promoter as a novel and promising druggable target for antiviral development.; the influenza A virus RNA promoter is a highly conserved structural element located at the 3' and 5' ends of each viral RNA segment. Small_molecule_information: 5-amino-1-[4-(4-amino-6,7-dimethoxyquinazolin-2-yl)piperazin-1-yl]pentan-1-one, also referred to as Compound 4 (a derivative of DPQ/Compound 1 with modifications on the secondary amine of its piperazine ring), is a compound featuring a quinazoline-piperazine scaffold, where the quinazoline moiety is the typical -adrenoceptor antagonist pharmacophore (4-amino-6,7-dimethoxyquinazoline). At the other nitrogen of the piperazine ring, a flexible 5-aminopentanoyl side chain is attached. The presence of the terminal primary amine (NH) makes the compound more polar and hydrophilic, and potentially protonated at physiological pH. This design is often used to modulate the drugs water solubility, pharmacokinetics, or enhance its interaction with polar binding sites. The compound is synthesized via specific steps, can bind to the influenza A virus RNA promoter, exhibits no significant anti-influenza activity and no obvious cytotoxicity, and in practice, it could be researched as a next-generation -adrenoceptor antagonist, with the amino side chain possibly aiming to improve the drugs bioavailability or reduce side effects.

RNA information: r(AUUCU)11: This RNA is an 11-repeat unit that models the pathogenic expansion found in the intron of the ATXN10 mRNA in SCA10. Its secondary structure is characterized by periodically repeating 5'AU/3'UA base pair steps, creating an extended, repetitive binding platform. It is the primary disease-relevant target in the study, and its key functional feature is its ability to be recognized with high affinity and positive cooperativity by the designed dimeric compound 2AU-2. Binding to this RNA disrupts its interaction with proteins like hnRNP K, which is the fundamental pathogenic mechanism. This disruption, demonstrated in cellular models, leads to the amelioration of disease phenotypes, including reduced nuclear foci and improved mitochondrial function.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[5-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)furan-2-yl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Compound 2 (DB75, furamidine), is a bis-imidamide compound where the two benzamidine groups are connected by a furan ring, with the formula C18H16N4O, and it is also a bis-cationic diphenylfuran derivative with a furamidine chromophore and smaller amidine end-groups; the introduction of the furan ring imparts some flexibility to the molecule, allowing for conformational adjustments during DNA binding, and this compound can selectively bind AU base pairs with positive cooperativity, targeting r(AUUCU) repeats and protecting AU sites from RNase cleavage, can bind HIV-1 TAR RNAs GCUCU motif via shape-driven recognition, displace Tat, block trans-activation, stabilize TAR, and has anti-PCP activity/DNA minor-groove binding, with no cytotoxicity below 30 M and serving as a TAR-targeting benchmark; these heterocyclic diamidines are a focus in the development of antiparasitic drugs, leveraging their strong DNA binding ability to effectively inhibit the growth of protozoa like Trypanosoma, and also show potential for antifungal and anticancer therapy.

Small_molecule_information: 3,5-diamino-6-chloro-N-(diaminomethylidene)pyrazine-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Amiloride, is a well-known potassium-sparing diuretic that blocks epithelial sodium channels (ENaC) in the kidneys, with the molecule acting by reversibly blocking the epithelial sodium channel (ENaC) in the renal collecting ducts, thereby reducing sodium reabsorption and fluid retention. Clinically, Amiloride is used to treat hypertension, congestive heart failure, and edema, and is often combined with other diuretics to prevent hypokalemia.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 10-[3-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)propyl]-2-(trifluoromethyl)phenothiazine, also referred to as Trifluoperazine, a phenothiazine antipsychotic drug that primarily acts as a dopamine antagonist, is based on the phenothiazine tricyclic core, features an electron-withdrawing trifluoromethyl group at the 2-position which enhances its lipophilicity, and has a propyl chain connecting an N-methylpiperazine group at the 10-position nitrogen; structurally, this is characteristic of many classic antipsychotic drugs, and this compound is expected to show potent dopamine receptor antagonist activity, particularly towards the central nervous system D2 receptor, making it potentially useful for treating schizophrenia or for its sedative effects.

RNA information: NRQ mutant is a G-base mutant of NRQ that prevents the formation of the G4 structure.
Small_molecule_information: 5-methyl-2-[(E)-2-(4-methylsulfanylphenyl)ethenyl]-N-(2-pyrrolidin-1-ylethyl)-10H-indolo[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-amine iodide, also referred to as the N5-methylated analogue of 4c-6, is a large, planar quaternary ammonium salt based on the indolo[3,2-b]quinoline core, which demonstrated binding affinity for the NRQ, exhibited variable anti-proliferative activity with efficacy in A375 cells but no effect in HeLa cells at relevant concentrations; quaternary salts are often potent DNA intercalators and broad-spectrum antimicrobial agents due to their positive charge and ability to disrupt cell membranes, and this structure is typically investigated for its strong anticancer activity.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 2,6-dimethoxy-4-[2-(1-methyl-4-morpholin-4-ylquinolin-1-ium-2-yl)ethenyl]phenol, also referred to as Compound 2, is a green-yellow cationic fluorophore with a quinoline quaternary ammonium salt core, substituted at the 4-position with a morpholine group, and linked at the 2-position via a vinylene bridge to a 2,6-dimethoxy-4-hydroxyphenyl group (i.e., a 4-hydroxy-3,5-dimethoxystyryl group at the quinoline 2-position), the ether oxygen in the morpholine ring confers moderate polarity, the molecules extended conjugation and phenolic hydroxyl group make it a potential fluorescent probe for bio-imaging and pH sensing, SPR assay shows it has no significant affinity improvement for A10-RNA-Wt vs unsubstituted analogs, it wasnt tested in dual-luciferase assays so its effect on ADAM10 translation is unreported but it has cell toxicity toward HeLa cells, and regarding drug activity, these cationic quinoline derivatives are often studied as selective antimicrobial or anticancer agents due to their affinity for negatively charged cellular components, such as bacteria or mitochondria.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-4-amino-2-[(1S,2S,3R,4S,6R)-4,6-diamino-3-[(2R,3R,5S,6R)-3-amino-6-(aminomethyl)-5-hydroxyoxan-2-yl]oxy-2-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxane-3,5-diol, also referred to as an aminoglycoside antibiotic that binds to the 16S rRNA of bacterial ribosomes, is a member of the aminoglycoside antibiotic class, structurally composed of multiple aminosugar units, including an aminocyclohexyl core linked by glycosidic bonds to other aminopyranose units. Like its congeners, the compound is characterized by multiple primary and secondary amine groups that are protonated and polycationic at physiological pH, a feature essential for its biological function. Its mechanism involves binding to the A-site of the bacterial ribosome (which is equivalent to binding to the 16S rRNA of bacterial ribosomes) to disrupt protein synthesis, causing mistranslation and premature termination of protein synthesis, effectively killing the bacteria. Based on its specific arrangement of sugar units and stereochemistry, this compound corresponds to a specific aminoglycoside drug, possibly related to amikacin or netilmicin. These drugs are commonly used clinically as second-line or combination therapy for serious conditions like endocarditis, septicemia, and pulmonary infections caused by susceptible organisms, and are also used to treat Gram-negative infections, particularly Pseudomonas aeruginosa.

Small_molecule_information: bis(2-acetamidoacetic acid);4-[2-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)iminohydrazinyl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Diminazine, is a complex salt molecule consisting of a large cationic formamidine hydrazonebased diamidine species and two 2-acetamidoacetic acid anions, and the ,-diamidine core connected through a biphenyl and hydrazone bridge corresponds to the class of aromatic diamidine compounds used as antiprotozoal agents that exert activity by binding to the minor groove of DNA, with such cationic diamidines commonly displaying antiprotozoal, antifungal, antiviral, anticancer, or broad-spectrum antimicrobial properties.

RNA information: 5′UAG/3′A_C (SL I): The UAG/A_C motif is located within HCV 3′ UTR stem–loop I. Identified in the Inforna analysis as a ligandable element, it was enriched in binding assays, indicating its potential as a structural determinant for small molecule recognition and functional viral RNA inhibition.
Small_molecule_information: 1-N,4-N-bis[3,5-bis(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)phenyl]benzene-1,4-dicarboxamide, also referred to as Compound 9, is a highly symmetrical polyamine compound built upon a terephthalamide (benzene-1,4-dicarboxamide) core, its notable characteristic is that each side chain phenyl ring bears two 4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl (imidazolinyl) groups at positions 3 and 5, with two such substituted phenyl groups attached to the core, the entire molecule contains a total of four imidazolinyl groups, all of which are strong bases and can be protonated at physiological pH, making it a potent tetracationic system, this structural design, concentrating multiple positive charges on an aromatic scaffold, is a classic strategy for developing DNA minor groove binders, due to the NH groups of the imidazolinyl moieties serving as hydrogen bond donors, the molecule interacts with the minor groove of DNA through a combination of electrostatic forces, hydrogen bonding, and van der Waals interactions, such tetracationic molecules are studied in medicinal chemistry as antifungal, antiprotozoal (e.g., antileishmanial), and anticancer agents, and this compound served as a negative control in RNA binding and antiviral assays, it did not exhibit significant binding to the enriched RNA motifs identified in the combinatorial screening, in agreement, it failed to interact with HCV 3 UTR stemloops I and II and showed no measurable inhibition of viral replication in cellular assays, its inactivity in both in vitro and in-cell experiments validated the specificity of Compounds 7, 8, and 10 for targeting HCV RNA motifs, demonstrating that not all small molecules engage structured RNA with functional consequences.

Small_molecule_information: 2-[(7-chloroquinolin-4-yl)amino]-6-(diethylaminomethyl)phenol;dihydrochloride, also referred to as Quinacrine Di HCl, is a quinoline-phenol heterocyclic compound presented as the dihydrochloride salt, which is an antimalarial and antiprotozoal drug with DNA intercalation properties and has been repurposed for cancer and prion diseases due to its ability to inhibit protein aggregation and nucleic acid interactions, its structure featuring a 4-aminoquinoline backbone with a 7-chloro substitution is chemically analogous to classic antimalarial drugs like chloroquine, the presence of both a phenolic hydroxyl group and a diethylaminomethyl group on the phenyl ring provides multiple sites for interaction and protonation, quinolines are a broad class of compounds known for antimalarial, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, due to its salt form the molecule is highly protonated enhancing its water solubility and the functional groups suggest potential for metal ion chelation, in reality this compound is likely explored as a novel antimalarial candidate to combat drug resistance or possibly as an anticancer agent due to its potential to interfere with metal homeostasis or kinase activity.

RNA information: The influenza A virus RNA promoter features a bent A-form helical structure with a dynamic (A–A)–U internal loop that serves as a specific binding site for the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) and nucleoprotein complex. This unique structural motif exhibits conformational flexibility that facilitates recognition by viral replication machinery while maintaining overall A-form geometry. The promoter's distinctive architecture and dynamic properties are essential for initiating viral RNA synthesis, making it a critical functional element in the influenza replication cycle and a promising target for antiviral therapeutic development; the influenza A virus RNA promoter is a highly conserved structural element located at the 3' and 5' ends of each viral RNA segment. Its structure, determined by NMR spectroscopy, adopts a panhandle-like conformation characterized by an A-form helix. A key structural feature is a unique (A-A)-U internal loop and a C-A mismatch pair. Furthermore, the promoter exhibits a significant bend of approximately 46 ± 10° near the viral RNA initiation site. Functionally, this specific architecture is specifically recognized by the viral RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp), which binds to it to initiate both transcription and replication of the viral genome. The high conservation of its sequence across strains and its intolerance to mutations, which are detrimental to viral replication, establish the influenza RNA promoter as a novel and promising druggable target for antiviral development.; the influenza A virus RNA promoter is a highly conserved structural element located at the 3' and 5' ends of each viral RNA segment. Small_molecule_information: (6-carbamimidoylnaphthalen-2-yl) 4-(diaminomethylideneamino)benzoate is an amidinated naphthyl ester, containing an amidine group on the naphthalene ring and a guanidine group (which is also an amidine) on the benzoate ester. This structure is a potential protease inhibitor, especially for serine proteases (such as thrombin, trypsin), as the amidine group is a common pharmacophore for protease inhibitors. The molecule may have applications in anticoagulation or anti-inflammatory fields.

Small_molecule_information: N-[(E)-[10-[(E)-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-ylhydrazinylidene)methyl]anthracen-9-yl]methylideneamino]-4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-amine, also referred to as Bisantrene, is a complex anthracene derivative with known antitumor relevance, where the anthracene ring (three linearly fused benzene rings) is substituted at the 9- and 10-positions with hydrazone/imine-linked 4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazole (imidazolinium) groups; anthracene is a well-known fluorescent chromophore, making this compound highly likely to exhibit strong fluorescence, and the molecule contains two imidazolinium groups and a conjugated hydrazone-imine structure, elements that facilitate binding to biomolecules like DNA, consistent with its use as a DNA-intercalating topoisomerase IIrelated agent, and such compounds are typically designed as DNA-binding fluorescent probes or potential anticancer agents

Small_molecule_information: 2-(2,4-dimethylphenyl)quinoline-5-carboxylic acid, also referred to as CP14, is a quinoline-5-carboxylic acid derivative substituted at the 2-position with a 2,4-dimethylphenyl group; CP14 is specifically characterized as a quinoline carboxylic acid derivative with a 3,4-dimethylphenyl group, and its carboxylic acid moiety and dimethyl substitution may have hampered its permeability or binding, resulting in relegating it to a moderate candidate. The combination of the quinoline ring and the carboxylic acid is a common motif in Non-Steroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drugs (NSAIDs) or tyrosine kinase inhibitors. The carboxylic acid group, negatively charged at physiological pH, is crucial for ionic interaction with the targets active site. The 2,4-dimethylphenyl group increases lipophilicity and contributes to hydrophobic interactions. This molecule is a likely candidate for an anti-inflammatory, analgesic, or anti-tumor (kinase inhibition) agent, with its activity driven by the interaction of the planar aromatic ring and the carboxylate group with the enzyme pocket.

RNA information: miR-544 precursor is a hairpin-structured non-coding RNA that is specifically processed by the RNase Dicer to generate the mature oncogenic microRNA miR-544. In hypoxic breast-tumor cells it acts as a metabolic sensor: its transcription is driven by HIF-1α, and after Dicer cleavage the resulting miR-544 directly represses mTOR and ATM, thereby redirecting glucose flux and promoting survival under low oxygen. Chemical or genetic blockade of this precursor-to-mature transition derepresses mTOR, sensitizes triple-negative breast-cancer cells to hypoxia-induced apoptosis and 5-fluorouracil, and arrests tumor growth in vivo, demonstrating that the precursor’s controlled processing is essential for tumor adaptation to hypoxic stress.
Small_molecule_information: 1,8-diamino-3,6-dipyrrolidin-1-yl-2,7-naphthyridine-4-carbonitrile, also referred to as Compound 1 (2) (N-(3-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)propyl)-2-(pyrrolidin-1-yl)quinolin-4-amine), is a compound featuring a highly substituted 2,7-naphthyridine scaffold with multiple amino and pyrrolidine substituents, and a cyano group at position 4, with the formula C19H23N7, and Compound 1 (2), identified via Inforna, selectively binds miR-544 precursors Dicer site and r(CUG)exp U loops, naphthyridine cores are often investigated as fluorescent dyes and DNA binders, the high polarity and multiple hydrogen bond sites suggest good water solubility and potential biological activity, it is likely designed as a DNA minor groove binder, aiming to interfere with replication and transcription processes through non-covalent binding, thus having prospects in antimicrobial and anticancer research, it inhibits miR-544 biogenesis, derepresses mTOR, disrupts ATMmTOR axis (induces TNBC apoptosis to a notable extent, with significant efficacy vs antagomirs), displaces MBNL1, improves DM1 splicing/translation, and disperses foci, a single i.p. injection of a low dose abolishes MDA-MB-231 tumor growth (low toxicity), acting as an RNA-directed probe for TNBC and DM1. RNA information: The r(CAG)₁₀ sequence serves as a comparative motif to test ligand selectivity against repeat RNAs with A–A mismatches instead of U–U mismatches. Structurally, it forms A-form helical RNA with alternating positive and negative electrostatic stripes similar to CUG repeats, leading to comparable overall architectures. Functionally, r(CAG)₁₀ is not associated with myotonic dystrophy but provides a stringent control for assessing molecular recognition. Because many proteins and synthetic ligands show similar affinity toward both CUG and CAG repeats, this sequence demonstrates the challenges of engineering true sequence selectivity in RNA-targeted small-molecule design.
Small_molecule_information: (E,2S,7S)-N,N-bis[(2S)-6-amino-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxohexan-2-yl]-2,7-bis[[(2S)-1-(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)pyrrolidine-2-carbonyl]amino]oct-4-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 5 (2012), is the E-isomer counterpart of compound 4, differing only in the configuration of the olefin bridge, and this geometric change alters the positioning of the benzo[g]quinoline group relative to the peptide scaffold, leading to slightly different recognition properties, while it retains overall high binding capability, though its cooperativity and selectivity are reduced compared to the Z-isomer, and structurally, it highlights how subtle stereochemical differences strongly affect RNA recognition, and biologically, it is still active in cellular models and displaces pathogenic complexes, demonstrating that both isomers contribute valuable insights into structureactivity relationships for RNA-binding molecules; it is a highly complex, multi-cationic peptidomimetic structure, its core is the (E)-oct-4-enediamide scaffold, with side chains derived from proline, 2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl, and lysine, the molecule contains multiple primary amino groups, rendering it poly-cationic at physiological pH, the 2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline is a bulky, highly hydrophobic aromatic group, this combination of poly-cationic character, multiple chiral centers, and large hydrophobic groups makes it suitable as an anti-tumor agent, a DNA/RNA binding agent, or a novel antibiotic, and the (E)-alkene provides a specific, rigid conformation.

RNA information: 5'CAG/3'CAG is a pathogenic RNA model linked to polyglutamine diseases like Huntington’s disease (HD) and spinocerebellar ataxias (SCAs). Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with non-canonical A-A mismatches, creating unique structural pockets. Functionally, it mimics expanded CAG repeat-associated structural aberrations and serves as a high-affinity target for small molecules, representing key pathogenic RNA features in related neurodegenerative disorders.
Small_molecule_information: [9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6-(diethylamino)xanthen-3-ylidene]-diethylazanium, also referred to as Rhodamine, is a xanthene derivative, typically classified as a Rhodamine-type fluorescent dye. The structure is based on a xanthene ring, with the molecular formula C28H31N2O3. This compound exhibits a planar conjugated system and is known for its exceptionally high fluorescence intensity and photostability. In biological function, this compound and its analogs (like Rhodamine B) are widely used as fluorescent tracers in biological and medical research, employed for labeling proteins, antibodies, or for cell counting and sorting in flow cytometry, and it is also a fluorescent dye used for staining and imaging in biological research that labels cellular structures while its interaction with nucleic acids (DNA/RNA) is nonspecific and primarily for visualization purposes.

RNA information: A10-RNA-Wt is a guanine (G)-rich sequence located in the 5' untranslated region (UTR) of ADAM10 mRNA, which can form a stable RNA G-quadruplex secondary structure. As a translational inhibitory element, this structure negatively regulates the protein synthesis of ADAM10 by adopting a specific spatial conformation that hinders ribosome binding or scanning. Studies have shown that targeting this structure with antisense oligonucleotides and small-molecule compounds (such as compound 24) can relieve its translational repression effect, up-regulate ADAM41 expression, thereby promoting the production of neuroprotective sAPP伪 and reducing the generation of peptides. This provides a novel strategy for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[2-(1-methyl-4-piperidin-1-ylquinolin-1-ium-2-yl)ethenyl]-4-nitrophenol, also referred to as Compound 7, is a typical quinolinium polymethine dye and a 1-methylquinolinium derivative with a 2-hydroxy-5-nitrostyryl group at C2 and a piperidin-1-yl moiety at C4 of the quinoline ring, synthesized via general procedure B, its core is the quinoline ring, methylated at N1 to form a quaternary ammonium salt, making it permanently positively charged, the C2 position is linked via a vinyl bridge to a nitrophenol ring, and C4 is substituted with a piperidine ring, this structure possesses an extended conjugated system, characteristic of highly efficient fluorescent dyes, its cationic nature predisposes it to target negatively charged cellular components, such as mitochondria (due to their negative membrane potential) or cell membranes, it primarily applies in bioimaging as a fluorescent indicator for membrane potential or a fluorescent tracer for specific organelles in biological and medical research, meanwhile, it binds ADAM10 mRNAs QGRS weakly and shows no translational up-regulation, its piperidine (vs methylpiperazine) and nitro-substituted styryl group hinder optimal RNA G-quadruplex interactions, lacking potency to enhance ADAM10 expression.

RNA information: 5'CGG/3'GGC is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to fragile X-associated tremor/ataxia syndrome (FXTAS), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it forms a hairpin with a 1×1 internal loop, closed by CG pairs, adopting non-canonical folding. Functionally, it contributes to FXTAS pathogenesis, likely by sequestering cellular proteins and disrupting normal splicing. As a disease-relevant RNA, it serves as a target for screening therapeutic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: (2S)-5-hydroxy-2-(3-hydroxy-4-methoxyphenyl)-7-[(2S,3R,4S,5S,6R)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-[[(2R,3R,4R,5R,6S)-3,4,5-trihydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxymethyl]oxan-2-yl]oxy-2,3-dihydrochromen-4-one, also referred to as Hesperidin, is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects commonly found in citrus fruits, and is an extremely complex natural product flavonoid O-diglycoside with a dihydroflavone (flavanone) core substituted at the 7-position with a diglycoside unit (likely involving glucose and rhamnose); the numerous phenolic hydroxyl groups and the bulky glycoside moiety are the primary drivers of its activity, and in practice, such compounds are key functional components in herbal medicines, demonstrating potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging capabilities, while due to its glycosidic structure, it typically has high water solubility and specific oral bioavailability (via gut microbial hydrolysis), and it is widely studied for cardiovascular benefits as well as cardioprotective, antiviral, and hepatoprotective effects, making it a focal point in natural product research.

RNA information: The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).; The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).·
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[6-amino-2-[[2-[(5-methyl-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-11-yl)amino]acetyl]amino]hexanoyl]amino]-5-(diaminomethylideneamino)pentanoic acid is a complex quinoline-peptide conjugate where the benzofuroquinoline quaternary ammonium core is linked to an alkaline peptide chain containing lysine and arginine residues. This structure is a targeted drug design: the benzofuroquinoline acts as the cytotoxic warhead, and the alkaline peptide acts as a targeting vehicle to enhance cellular uptake and nuclear localization in anticancer drug development.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 7-hydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromen-4-one, also referred to as Genistein, a naturally occurring isoflavone (a type of phytoestrogen, with Daidzein being another soy-derived isoflavone with estrogenic and antioxidant properties that is studied for its role in hormone-related conditions), has the formula C15H10O4; its core structure is the chromen-4-one backbone, substituted with a 4-hydroxyphenyl group at C3 and a hydroxyl group at C7, and it is abundant in soybeans and soy-derived products as a principal component of soy isoflavones, in terms of real-world biological activity, Genistein is known for its ability to mimic or antagonize estrogen due to its structural similarity to 17-estradiol thus earning the classification of phytoestrogen often used to alleviate menopausal symptoms, importantly, Genistein is a potent tyrosine kinase inhibitor particularly of the Src family kinases which has led to intense interest in cancer research for its potential to inhibit tumor growth in models of breast and prostate cancer, and it also possesses significant antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

RNA information: (CUG)12 is an RNA model of expanded pathogenic CUG repeats in myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1). Structurally, it forms a stable A-form hairpin with UU mismatches in its double-stranded region, stabilized by base-pairing and phosphate backbone interactions. Functionally, it high-affinity sequesters MBNL1 protein, disrupting alternative splicing, causing ribonuclear foci accumulation and pre-mRNA mis-splicing (e.g., insulin receptor exon 11 exclusion), via a toxic gain-of-function mechanism, making it a DM1 therapeutic target.
Small_molecule_information: 9-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butylamino]-N-[2-[2-[2-[2-[[9-[4-[(4,6-diamino-1,3,5-triazin-2-yl)amino]butylamino]acridine-2-carbonyl]amino]ethoxy]ethoxy]ethoxy]ethyl]acridine-2-carboxamide, also referred to as Acridine 12, is an extremely large, bifunctional molecule whose core structure consists of two acridine rings and is a dimeric ligand with an oligoether linker, its binding affinity was close to that of the monomer, it showed a small bivalent effect and no melting temperature data could be determined for this compound; Acridine is a tricyclic planar aromatic system, the parent structure of famous DNA intercalators, the two acridine units are linked by a flexible tetraethylene glycol (PEG-like) spacer, which enhances the molecules water solubility and biological flexibility, each acridine ring also carries a triazine-butane amine side chain, introducing additional basicity and nucleic acid binding capacity, the structure of this compound represents a classic dimerized drug design, aimed at enhancing its affinity for DNA and cytotoxicity through double intercalation or multivalency effects, in practice, this compound is researched as a potent anti-cancer agent, especially against multi-drug resistant (MDR) cell lines, or as a highly specific fluorescent probe.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 1-cyclopropyl-7-(4-ethylpiperazin-1-yl)-6-fluoro-4-oxoquinoline-3-carboxylic acid, also referred to as Enrofloxacin, is a classic Fluoroquinolone antibiotic with N1-cyclopropyl, C6-fluoro, and C7-piperazine substituents (similar to Ciprofloxacin) that inhibits bacterial DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV, preventing DNA replication, and is used clinically as a broad-spectrum antimicrobial for infections including those of the respiratory and urinary tracts.

RNA information: The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).; The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).·
Small_molecule_information: 3-chloro-N-[2-[4-[4-[2-(diethylamino)ethoxy]anilino]quinazolin-2-yl]phenyl]propanamide, also referred to as a12, is a quinazoline derivative that includes a 3-chloropropanamide group, suggesting its potential use as a covalent or irreversible enzyme inhibitor; while a12 is only confirmed to bind G-quadruplex sequences in vitro and its screening target hVEGF-A is closely associated with tumor angiogenesis, this compound merely serves as a member of the screening library for potential anti-tumor candidates with no further functional or disease-related validation, and is a candidate for targeted anticancer or anti-inflammatory drugs.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 4-amino-2-hydroxybenzoic acid, also referred to as 4-Aminosalicylic acid or p-Aminosalicylic acid (PAS), is a salicylate drug and salicylic acid derivative characterized by an amino group at position 4, a hydroxyl group, and a carboxyl group attached to a benzene ring, with the molecular formula C7H7NO3; it has a strong binding affinity for 108yjdF RNA but does not induce gene expression, suggesting that it may not trigger relevant biological function, and in terms of real-world activity, it is a historically significant antituberculosis drug that played a pivotal role in the early treatment of the disease, currently still used as a second-line agent for treating multidrug-resistant tuberculosis (MDR-TB), its mechanism of action involves acting as a competitive inhibitor of p-Aminobenzoic acid (PABA), thereby disrupting the function of dihydropteroate synthase in Mycobacterium tuberculosis, which ultimately blocks the synthesis of essential folic acid and inhibits bacterial growth, and due to its salicylic acid structure, it also possesses some anti-inflammatory properties.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: [5-[3-(8-fluoro-2-methylimidazo[1,2-a]pyridin-6-yl)-2-oxochromen-7-yl]-5-azaspiro[2.4]heptan-7-yl]azanium, also referred to as SMSM56, is a coumarin derivative whose position 7 is substituted with a highly rigid spirocyclic system, the 5-azaspiro[2.4]heptane group, which bears an additional azanium positive charge at the 7-carbon atom, and SMSM56 itself is a non-planar coumarin analog with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, carrying a 1 charge, this spirocycle merges a highly strained three-membered ring with a five-membered ring, creating a molecular geometry that is both highly unique and rigid, the presence of the quaternary ammonium positive charge is a key design feature for enhancing water solubility and affinity for biomembranes, the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine group is attached at position 3 of the coumarin core, and this compound recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 driven by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, serving as a selective RNA ligand, due to its unique spatial structure and high rigidity, this compound is likely designed as a highly selective ligand for biological targets with complex binding pockets, with the coumarin and imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine fragments suggesting potential anti-tumor or anti-inflammatory activities.

RNA information: 5'CUG/3'CUG is a pathogenic RNA motif linked to myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1), a trinucleotide repeat expansion disorder (TRED). Structurally, it folds into an A-form hairpin with characteristic UU mismatches, stabilized by base-pairing. Functionally, it sequesters Muscleblind-like 1 (MBNL1) protein, disrupting pre-mRNA alternative splicing, which is the core gain-of-function mechanism underlying DM1 pathogenesis.
Small_molecule_information: 3-N,3-N,7-N,7-N-tetramethyl-4-nitro-3H-phenothiazine-3,7-diamine, also referred to as the compound with a tricyclic phenothiazine core where a sulfur atom bridges two benzene rings, bearing 3,7-tetramethyldiamino groups and a 4-nitro substituent and exhibiting aromatic, electron-deficient, and polar features, is a nitro-substituted phenothiazine derivative, structurally similar to Methylene Blue; functionally, no links to RNA targets (e.g., pre-miR-21, r(GC)) or related biological activities (e.g., RAN translation inhibition) are reported, leaving its specific roles uncharacterized in the given context, and it is used in reality as a redox indicator, biological stain, antimalarial drug, and a potential therapeutic for inhibiting Tau protein aggregation in Alzheimers disease.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: N-[2-(3-aminopropoxy)-5-(5,7-dimethoxy-1-methyl-4-oxoquinolin-2-yl)phenyl]-4-(dimethylamino)butanamide, also referred to as Phenylquinolone 3c, is a disubstituted derivative synthesized from its monosubstituted precursor 2c by introducing a second cationic side chain through acylation with 4-(dimethylamino)butyric acid (DMABA), and it features a complex substitution pattern: the primary amine on the phenyl ring is replaced by a 4-(dimethylamino)butanamide, and the side chain is a 3-aminopropoxy (primary amine); the molecule contains amide, primary amine, and tertiary amine functional groups, forming a complex network of basicity and hydrogen bonding, with the amide bond being a key structural feature of many protease inhibitors, while the addition of the second protonable group proved highly detrimental to its activity, as the compound was completely inactive (N/A) in the fluorescence quenching assay, demonstrating no ability to inhibit the Tat/TAR complex formation, a result that strongly supports the critical structure-activity relationship finding that a single, not dual, cationic side chain is essential for effective target binding in this series of 2-phenylquinolone inhibitors; functionally, this multi-functional structure may be designed as a multi-target inhibitor, simultaneously inhibiting multiple biological targets like kinases/topoisomerases and proteases, with potential use in the treatment of multidrug-resistant cancer.

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-[3,5-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-hydroxyphenyl]phenol, also referred to as 3,3,5,5-tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) biphenyl with 4,4-diol groups (Compound 2), is a polyamine-substituted tetrasubstituted biphenol structure featuring four 2-aminoethyl groups and two phenolic hydroxyls, which is synthesized via sequential palladium-catalyzed Suzuki couplings and nitrile reduction, binds to HIV-1 RRE Bh with low affinity (ITC-measured), has no specificity for Bh over GGh, inhibits Bh-Rev interaction via a different mechanism and shows weak anti-HIV activity; this multiple amine/hydroxyl structure confers high charge and hydrophilicity at physiological pH, and since polyamines are known in biology (e.g., spermidine, spermine) for their ability to bind DNA/RNA and regulate cell proliferation, this compound, with its multivalent positive charge, is researched in the real world as a potential polyamine metabolism regulator or a DNA/RNA binding agent, with potential use in anticancer therapy (by interfering with polyamine synthesis) or as a non-viral gene delivery vector due to its ability to bind to negatively charged nucleic acids for gene therapy research.

RNA information: TCT8-4 has two conserved regions (5'-AUACCA-3' and 5'-CCUUGG(C/A)AG-3') . It binds theophylline  in a Mg-dependent way, showing 10,900-fold higher affinity for theophylline than caffeine. It aids in diagnosing diseases like asthma/bronchitis, where theophylline is used as a bronchodilator, by distinguishing theophylline from similar compounds.
Small_molecule_information: 7-methyl-3H-purine-2,6-dione, also referred to as 7-Methylxanthine, is one of the core structures of 7-Methylxanthine and an oxopurine alkaloid in which the hydrogen at N-7 of the xanthine ring is replaced by a methyl group, making it an obligatory intermediate in the plant biosynthetic pathway to caffeine; this compound is found as a plant and mammalian metabolite, and its single methyl substitution abolishes the N-7 proton that the TCT8-4 RNA aptamer uses for high-affinity hydrogen bonding, resulting in much weaker binding to the aptamer than theophylline, which demonstrates that an intact N-7 proton is essential for tight recognition by the evolved RNA sensor, and it is also one of the main metabolites of caffeine in humans and is significant in pharmacokinetic and toxicological studies.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 3-amino-2-benzoyl-4-phenylchromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one, also referred to as Compound 1, is one of the parent derivatives of the chromeno[4,3-b]pyridin-5-one scaffold, relatively simple with only amino at 3, benzoyl at 2, and phenyl at 4, lacking the halogen atom at position 9 of the core, and it is a pyridocoumarin derivative that showed moderate binding affinity to the disease-related r(CAG)exp RNA, with a higher preference for the AA-mismatched RNA motif over the canonical AU-paired RNA and demonstrated potential in reducing polyglutamine aggregation in cellular models, though it was not among the top leads; this unsubstituted structure is typically used to establish the intrinsic biological activity of the scaffold, due to its large planarity and conjugated system, this compound is considered a chromophoric molecule, in medicinal chemistry, its structure suggests it could be a DNA intercalator or a broad-spectrum bioactive small molecule, while in photophysics and photochemistry, it may be investigated as a fluorescent probe or photosensitizer for bioimaging or photodynamic therapy.

Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine.
