It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: (2S)-2-[[4-[(6aR)-3-amino-1-oxo-5,6,6a,7-tetrahydro-2H-imidazo[1,5-f]pteridin-10-ium-8-yl]benzoyl]amino]-5-hydroxy-5-oxopentanoate, also referred to as 5,10-methenyl tetrahydrofolate (5,10-methenyl THF), is a complex folic acid structural analog or metabolite, with the molecular formula C20H20N7O7, and is a non-azaaromatic folate derivative that has a methenyl group (CH-) bridging the 5th and 10th positions of the tetrahydrofolate (THF) pteridine ring and lacks planar azaaromatic features; the structure incorporates a highly reduced and cyclized pteridine scaffold and an anionic group derived from glutamic acid, its most notable feature is the quaternary ammonium structure (-10-ium) giving it a positive charge at physiological pH, it binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate its-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, its core role is acting as a one-carbon carrier in folate metabolism to transfer carbon units for purine and thymidine synthesis which is unrelated to azaaromatic sensing, and in terms of real-world activity, this charged folic acid derivative is likely a specific ligand for folate receptors or an inhibitor of folate-dependent enzymes, and due to its unique cyclization and charged nature, it may possess distinct transport mechanisms or cellular targeting and is being studied as a novel folate antagonist, for instance to target tumor cells that rely on increased folate uptake for growth.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: 4-amino-1-[(2R,5S)-2-(hydroxymethyl)-1,3-oxathiolan-5-yl]pyrimidin-2-one, also referred to as Lamivudine (3TC) or its enantiomer, is a synthetic cytidine nucleoside analog where the 3 oxygen atom of the ribose ring is replaced by a sulfur atom, forming a 1,3-oxathiolane sugar ring, molecular formula C8H11N3O3S; it is a nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor (NRTI) that incorporates into viral DNA, terminating its synthesis, and in terms of real-world activity, Lamivudine is an extremely important antiviral drug, classified as a Nucleoside Reverse Transcriptase Inhibitor (NRTI), which is widely used clinically for the treatment of Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) infection and Hepatitis B Virus (HBV) infection, with its mechanism of action involving being phosphorylated intracellularly to its active triphosphate form, which is then incorporated into the viral DNA chain, causing chain termination and effectively inhibiting the activity of the HIV reverse transcriptase and HBV polymerase.

RNA information: HIV-1 RRE IIBh is a hairpin RNA oligonucleotide derived from the subdomain IIB of the HIV-1 Rev Response Element (RRE). Its core function is to serve as a high-affinity binding site for the viral Rev protein, particularly its arginine-rich α-helical region (Rev₃₄₋₅₀). This interaction is essential for triggering the nuclear export of unspliced or incompletely spliced viral RNA, thereby ensuring the production of viral particles during the late stage of the HIV-1 life cycle.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-(2-aminoethyl)-5-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2-methoxyphenyl]-2-phenylmethoxyphenyl]ethanamine, also referred to as Compound 5a (a 3,5,2,6-tetrakis(2-aminoethyl) p-terphenyl compound with a single methoxy group at the 2 position of the central benzene ring), is a derivative of 2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-[4-[2,6-bis(2-aminoethyl)-4-methoxyphenyl]-2-methoxyphenyl]phenol, where the phenol is substituted with a benzyloxy group, and Compound 5a is synthesized via sequential palladium-catalyzed Suzuki coupling reactions and was characterized by methods such as Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) and Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR); the benzyloxy group is an aromatic side chain that increases lipophilicity, and Compound 5a functionally can bind to the hairpin RNA (Bh) of the HIV-1 RRE subdomain IIB, has limited selectivity for Bh over DNA, exhibits a high half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC) in inhibiting the interaction between Bh and Rev, and shows no antiviral activity in cellular assays; as a tetracationic polyamine, its primary mode of action remains strong interaction with negatively charged biomolecules (nucleic acids, membranes), and in practical applications, this benzyloxy-substituted polyamine is often used in Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) studies to evaluate the effect of simple aromatic lipophilic side chains on the polyamine cores cytotoxicity, transfection efficiency, and selectivity, to find the optimal drug design.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: 4-[5-(4-carbamimidoylphenyl)furan-2-yl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as Compound 2 (DB75, furamidine), is a bis-imidamide compound where the two benzamidine groups are connected by a furan ring, with the formula C18H16N4O, and it is also a bis-cationic diphenylfuran derivative with a furamidine chromophore and smaller amidine end-groups; the introduction of the furan ring imparts some flexibility to the molecule, allowing for conformational adjustments during DNA binding, and this compound can selectively bind AU base pairs with positive cooperativity, targeting r(AUUCU) repeats and protecting AU sites from RNase cleavage, can bind HIV-1 TAR RNAs GCUCU motif via shape-driven recognition, displace Tat, block trans-activation, stabilize TAR, and has anti-PCP activity/DNA minor-groove binding, with no cytotoxicity below 30 M and serving as a TAR-targeting benchmark; these heterocyclic diamidines are a focus in the development of antiparasitic drugs, leveraging their strong DNA binding ability to effectively inhibit the growth of protozoa like Trypanosoma, and also show potential for antifungal and anticancer therapy.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a short, genetically conserved stem–bulge–loop hairpin found at the 5′ end of every viral transcript.  Functional constructs range from minimal 29-nt fragments (containing the essential bulge and loop) up to the canonical 52–59-nt forms seen in full-length genomes; in all cases the active fold is the same: a double-stranded stem interrupted by a three-base pyrimidine bulge (commonly UCU) and capped by a six-nucleotide loop.  This bulge-and-loop motif first captures an arginine-rich segment of the viral Tat protein, inducing a local conformational switch that allows Tat to recruit the host Cyclin T1–CDK9 (P-TEFb) complex.  The resultant Tat–TAR–P-TEFb assembly converts RNA polymerase II from abortive to highly processive elongation, boosting viral gene expression >100-fold, and the same RNA structure also facilitates strand-transfer during reverse transcription.  Because the bulge–loop signature is indispensable and sequence-invariant across strains, TAR acts as a natural “riboswitch-like” sensor that tunes the balance between transcription and genome replication, offering a structurally small yet functionally critical target for therapeutic intervention.
Small_molecule_information: N-pentan-3-yl-4-[5-[4-(N-pentan-3-ylcarbamimidoyl)phenyl]furan-2-yl]benzenecarboximidamide, also referred to as DB226 (2,5-bis-[4-(N-isopentylamidino)phenyl]furan), is a bis-amidine compound containing biphenyl and furan structures, which is a bis-cationic diphenylfuran congener in which the terminal amidines carry branched isopentyl side-chains; its structure is based on the benzene and furan parent skeletons: two benzene rings are linked by a furan ring, and the para-position (position 4) of each benzene ring is substituted with an N-pentan-3-ylcarboximidamide group; in terms of biological function, amidine compounds are often used as antiparasitic agents (such as for Trypanosoma and Plasmodium), antibacterial agents, or as DNA binding agents, and this compound was originally optimized for anti-Pneumocystis carinii (PCP) therapy, with its lipophilic modification strengthening minor-groove binding to AT-rich DNA and preserving high anti-PCP potency in rat models, while when tested against the HIV-1 TAR RNA target, the extra bulk reduces RNA affinity, showing no measurable suppression of Tat-driven trans-activation in the relevant cellular assay, and melting-temperature experiments record a modest Tsubm/sub for the DB226TAR complex, with RNase A footprints requiring a certain concentration for detectable protection of the GCUCU site opposite the bulge, confirming low-occupancy binding; like its analogues, DB226 recognizes the three-dimensional cavity created by the pyrimidine bulge rather than specific nucleotidesmutation of certain sites has little influence, whereas deletion of the bulge abolishes interaction; the compound is non-toxic to reporter cells below a specific concentration and retains full anti-PCP efficacy, illustrating that enlarging the alkyl-amidine substituent tips selectivity toward DNA while diminishing RNA recognition, thus, although DB226 itself is not an effective anti-HIV lead, its profile further defines structureselectivity relationships guiding the design of next-generation TAR-targeted diphenylfurans.

RNA information: The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).; The IRES-A (WT) sequence is capable of forming a stable G-quadruplex structure. This structure is essential for the initiation of cap-independent translation of the human vascular endothelial growth factor (hVEGF).·
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[5-methyl-11-(3-piperidin-1-ylpropylamino)-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-5-ium-7-yl]oxy]-N-(3-piperidin-1-ylpropyl)acetamide iodide is a disubstituted, dicationic [1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline derivative with two 3-piperidin-1-ylpropylamine side chains. The dicationic charge and highly conjugated planar core classify it as a potent polycationic intercalator. These compounds are used in the development of DNA minor groove binders and bifunctional topoisomerase inhibitors, exhibiting high anticancer and broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity, particularly against drug-resistant strains.

RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: (3R,4S,5S,6R,7R,9R,11R,12R,13S,14R)-6-[(2S,3R,4S,6R)-4-(dimethylamino)-3-hydroxy-6-methyloxan-2-yl]oxy-14-ethyl-7,12,13-trihydroxy-4-[(2R,4R,5S,6S)-5-hydroxy-4-methoxy-4,6-dimethyloxan-2-yl]oxy-3,5,7,9,11,13-hexamethyl-oxacyclotetradecane-2,10-dione, also referred to as Erythromycin, is an extremely complex macrolide structure, representing Erythromycin or its derivatives, and Erythromycin is a macrolide antibiotic with a 14-membered lactone ring core linked to two deoxy sugars (desosamine and cladinose), its structure features a large cyclic lactone scaffold with glycosidic attachments, enabling specific bacterial ribosome binding, macrolides are important clinical antibiotics that inhibit bacterial protein synthesis by binding to the 50S ribosomal subunit, functionally, it inhibits bacterial protein synthesis by targeting the 50S ribosomal subunit, blocking peptide bond formation, and exhibits broad activity against Gram-positive bacteria, being widely used to treat respiratory and skin infections while resisting certain drug-resistant strains.

RNA information: (5'CAG/3'GUC): This RNA motif represents a standard Watson-Crick base-paired duplex with CAG pairing with GUC. It serves as a control structure to compare against mismatched or disease-relevant RNA motifs. It exhibits typical A-form helical geometry and is used to assess the selectivity of small molecules for non-canonical structures.; 5'CAG/3'GUC is a classic paired RNA sequence used as a control.; In contrast, (5'CAG/3'GUC) forms a standard, stable duplex with canonical Watson-Crick base pairing (A-U and G-C). The absence of mismatches makes it a structurally bland control, crucial for demonstrating the binding specificity of compounds towards the aberrant A-A motif.
Small_molecule_information: 3-N,3-N,7-N,7-N-tetramethyl-4-nitro-3H-phenothiazine-3,7-diamine, also referred to as the compound with a tricyclic phenothiazine core where a sulfur atom bridges two benzene rings, bearing 3,7-tetramethyldiamino groups and a 4-nitro substituent and exhibiting aromatic, electron-deficient, and polar features, is a nitro-substituted phenothiazine derivative, structurally similar to Methylene Blue; functionally, no links to RNA targets (e.g., pre-miR-21, r(GC)) or related biological activities (e.g., RAN translation inhibition) are reported, leaving its specific roles uncharacterized in the given context, and it is used in reality as a redox indicator, biological stain, antimalarial drug, and a potential therapeutic for inhibiting Tau protein aggregation in Alzheimers disease.

RNA information: RNA1 is a structured RNA oligonucleotide derived from the SARS-CoV-2 5′UTR SL5 region, engineered to contain a single G bulge. It adopts a hairpin structure and serves as a model RNA for studying selective small-molecule binding, especially with coumarin derivatives, using fluorescence polarization and MST assays.
Small_molecule_information: 3-(4-fluoro-2-methyl-1,3-benzoxazol-6-yl)-7-[(3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium-1-yl]chromen-2-one, also referred to as SMSM17, is a non-planar coumarin analog with a 4-methylpiperazine A-ring and imidazopyridine DE-fused system, bearing a 1 charge, which has the coumarin core, whose C3 substituent is the 4-fluoro-2-methyl-1,3-benzoxazol-6-yl group, 1,3-Benzoxazole is an aromatic heterocycle fused from a benzene and an oxazole ring, known for its good fluorescence properties and use in anti-inflammatory and anti-tumor drugs, and whose C7 position carries the quaternized (3S)-3-methylpiperazin-4-ium moiety, this compound recognizes the single-G bulge of RNA1 mediated by minor-groove H-bonds, -stacking and electrostatics, confirmed by FP and SAR comparison, representing a weak-selective RNA binder, and combining the fluorescence of coumarin and benzoxazole with the positive charge of the quaternary ammonium salt makes it an effective fluorescent probe, due to its benzoxazole core, it is also explored as a potential anti-tumor agent or for diagnostic imaging, particularly in detecting proteins with hydrophobic binding pockets.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₂ (r(5′CUG/3′GUC)12) is a pathogenic RNA motif modeling myotonic dystrophy type 1 (DM1)-causing repeats. Structurally, it forms a stable hairpin with 12 CUG repeats, featuring a C-G paired stem and periodic 1×1 UU internal loops that create specific binding pockets. Functionally, it sequesters MBNL1 splicing regulator to disrupt pre-mRNA splicing (DM1’s core pathogenesis), serves as a non-cognate control for binding specificity assays, and is a target for small molecules that displace MBNL1 to restore RNA processing.
Small_molecule_information: N-[2-[[(2R)-2-[methyl-[(2R)-2-[methyl-[(2R)-2-[methyl-[(2R)-2-[methyl-[4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]propanoyl]amino]ethyl]-4-[3-[5-[5-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]-1H-benzimidazol-2-yl]phenoxy]butanamide, also referred to as 2H-4NMe, is a highly symmetrical and N-methylated bis-benzimidazole oligopeptide conjugate whose core scaffold is a five-residue N-methylated (2R)-alanine oligopeptide connected by an ethylenediamine bridge, with identical bis-benzimidazole-butanoyl complex substituents attached at both ends of the peptide chain; this compound, which exhibited much weaker potency compared to other PTAs as its poor performance indicated that increasing the N-methyl content significantly compromises binding efficiency, had no strong cellular or stability advantages reported and was among the least effective of the tested compounds, mainly demonstrating the negative effect of this particular scaffold design, while it also combines the high metabolic stability and enhanced cell membrane permeability provided by N-methylation with the high affinity derived from the two potent DNA-binding moieties, and this symmetrical design aims to optimize pharmacokinetics while maintaining high molecular activity, typically being used as a molecular probe to study DNA minor groove interactions or developed as a novel, orally bioavailable DNA-targeting anti-tumor drug.

RNA information: The duplex sequence is a designed RNA composed of complementary CUG and CAG repeats that form a stable double helix. Structurally, it lacks the mismatches present in expanded repeats, instead adopting a continuous A-form conformation that eliminates the irregular binding pockets exploited by proteins or ligands in single-stranded hairpins. Functionally, the duplex acts as a control to demonstrate whether ligands bind specifically to mismatched hairpin structures or indiscriminately to generic RNA helices. Its inclusion highlights the importance of structural context in ligand recognition and helps identify molecules with true selectivity for pathogenic RNA conformations.
Small_molecule_information: (Z,2S,7S)-2,7-bis[[(2S)-6-amino-2-[(2-ethylbenzo[g]quinoline-3-carbonyl)amino]hexanoyl]amino]-N-[(2S)-1-(2-aminoethylamino)-1-oxopropan-2-yl]-N-[(2S)-1-(3-aminopropylamino)-1-oxopropan-2-yl]-N,N-dipropyloct-4-enediamide, also referred to as DCC 8 (2012), is an asymmetric peptidomimetic with a cis (Z) double bond in its core eight-carbon chain, which forces the molecule into a rigid bent conformation; while DCC 8 (2012) contains the benzo[g]quinoline unit, it features a scrambled peptide sequence that alters the relative spatial positioning of functional groups, a design that tests whether the orientation of the heteroaromatic group and backbone is essential for recognition, and though the compound can interact with RNA, its selectivity is poor and its affinity reduced compared to ordered analogs, structurally demonstrating that sequence context is crucial for cooperative interactions and functionally failing to show strong biological rescue, thus serving as a control that validates the need for precise arrangement of components to achieve productive recognition and therapeutic activity, and this specific cis geometry of the main peptidomimetic is crucial for this molecule with asymmetric polyamine side chains (two- and three-carbon), as it determines the relative spatial position and distance of the two different side chains, thereby influencing target interaction, with both sides of the molecule also linked to benzo[g]quinoline-lysine groups, and it is a key molecule for studying the effect of conformational locking on the activity of asymmetric multifunctional molecules, whose target activity remains DNA binding and topoisomerase inhibition, but whose activity is expected to exhibit unique selectivity due to its characteristic Z-conformation.

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: (2R,3R,4R,5R)-4-amino-2-[(2S,3R,4S,6R)-4,6-diamino-3-[(1R,2S)-2-amino-6-(aminomethyl)cyclohexyl]oxy-2-hydroxycyclohexyl]oxy-5-methyloxane-3,5-diol, also referred to as Sisomicin Sulphate, is a complex aminoglycoside compound featuring multiple sugar rings and an aminocyclohexitol structure linked by glycosidic bonds. The molecule contains numerous amine (NH2) groups, rendering it polycationic at physiological pH. Aminoglycosides are a critical class of broad-spectrum antibiotics that function by irreversibly binding to the 30S subunit of the bacterial ribosome, disrupting protein synthesis by binding to rRNA, inhibiting protein synthesis. Similar to clinical antibiotics like Streptomycin or Gentamicin, this molecule is primarily used to treat severe Gram-negative bacterial infections, though its therapeutic use must consider inherent ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity side effects.

RNA information: G4-EBNA1 is a stable G-quadruplex in the EBNA1 mRNA GAr region. It binds nucleolin (NCL), inhibiting translation and reducing antigen presentation to enable immune evasion. Its formation is length-dependent. Ligands like PhenDC3, PyDH2, and PhenDH2 disrupt NCL binding, restoring EBNA1 expression and antigen presentation. The structure prefers U-shaped ligands that stack on G-tetrads. Targeting G4-EBNA1 exposes infected cells to immune response, offering a therapeutic strategy against EBV.
Small_molecule_information: 2-N,7-N-bis[(E)-(1-benzylquinolin-1-ium-4-yl)methylideneamino]-1,8-naphthyridine-2,7-dicarboxamide dibromide, also referred to as NaphDH3, is a dicationic molecule with a 1,8-naphthyridine-2,7-dicarboxamide core, and NaphDH3 features a central naphthyridine core with 1-benzylquinolinium lateral groups and had good G4 stabilization and moderate FID affinity but no biological activity, the 1,8-naphthyridine scaffold is formed by the fusion of two pyridine rings in an ortho arrangement, resulting in two close-proximity nitrogen atoms, which gives this core potential for metal coordination, the side chains are the quaternized quinoline moieties attached via the 4-position of the quinoline, this compound combines dicationic character, a planar aromatic scaffold, and potential metal coordination sites, compared to the pyridine- or pyrimidine-based cores, the rigidity and shape of the naphthyridine core provide a more distinct affinity profile for DNA minor groove binding and may influence its sequence selectivity with nucleic acids, molecules like this are important in anticancer drug research because their activity may not only stem from DNA binding but also be enhanced by forming metal complexes that can cause DNA damage, for example, through the generation of reactive oxygen species. RNA information: Bacillus subtilis 108yjdF RNA is a ribose switch with a unique structure and function, composed of 108 nucleotides, forming a four-stem ring structure, including four stem ring structures P1, P2, P3 and P4. Among them, the P1 and P2 stem-ring structures play a key role in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA can bind to a variety of azaaromatics, such as proflavine, chelerythrine and staurosporine. These compounds induce almost identical RNA structural changes when binding, indicating that their binding pockets have a high degree of universality in recognizing these compounds. Specifically, disrupting the base pairing of P1 (M1 mutant) will significantly reduce the binding affinity of propanin, while restoring the base pairing of P1 (M2 mutant) will bring the binding affinity back to a level close to that of the wild type. Furthermore, altering the conserved A or G nucleotides in the P2 loop (M3 and M4 mutants) completely eliminates the binding of propanin and riboflavin, further confirming the significance of these conserved nucleotides in ligand binding. 108yjdF RNA regulates gene expression by controlling the accessibility of ribosomal binding sites (RBS). When ligands bind, the RNA structure changes, making RBS accessible and thereby activating gene expression. Although the natural ligand of 108yjdF RNA has not yet been determined, the universality of its binding pocket and the mechanism of gene expression regulation provide important clues for studying its physiological functions within cells. It is speculated that it may have evolved into a universal sensor capable of recognizing multiple natural azaaromatics. These compounds may be toxic to cells, and the YjdF protein may be involved in the detoxification process.
Small_molecule_information: 2-(methylamino)-1,7-dihydropurin-6-one, also referred to as 6-Hydroxy-2-methylaminopurine, is a simple purine derivative with a bicyclic heterocyclic core (fused pyrimidine and imidazole rings), specifically a xanthine (1,7-dihydropurin-6-one) analog substituted with a methylamino group at the 2-position and containing a hydroxyl group at C6, which is non-planar and lacks planar azaaromatic features, and its structure is closely related to natural nucleobases like guanine; it binds weakly to the yjdF motif RNA (azaaromatic riboswitch) and fails to activate riboswitch-mediated gene expression in Bacillus subtilis, not involved in azaaromatic sensing, and in real-world activity, purine analogs are often studied to interfere with cellular purine metabolism pathways, this compound might act as an inhibitor of certain metabolic enzymes, such as guanine deaminase, potentially functioning in nucleic acid metabolism-related processes and showing research interest in antiviral or immunomodulatory applications.

RNA information: The bacterial riboflavin (FMN) riboswitch is located in the 5' untranslated region (5' UTR) of mRNA and consists of an aptamer domain and an expression platform. It can specifically bind to FMN and regulate the expression of the downstream key riboflavin synthesis gene ribB through conformational changes. When FMN is sufficient, the riboswitch forms a terminator/sequestering loop to inhibit gene expression; when FMN is deficient, it switches to an antiterminator/anti-sequestering loop, allowing gene transcription and translation to achieve negative feedback regulation. This riboswitch exists exclusively in bacteria and has no homologous structure in mammals, making it a highly selective target for new antibacterial drugs (such as ribocil).
Small_molecule_information: 2-[1-[[2-(methylamino)pyrimidin-5-yl]methyl]piperidin-3-yl]-4-thiophen-2-yl-1H-pyrimidin-6-one, also referred to as Ribocil, is a synthetic small molecule with an overall U-shaped rigid skeleton, this complex molecule has a substituted pyrimidin-6-one core, linked at the 2-position to a substituted piperidine ring, which is further connected to a 2-(methylamino)pyrimidinyl-methyl group, the 4-position is substituted with a thiophenyl group, its left 6-thienyl-pyrimidinone ring can intercalate into the stacking plane of the RNA double helix, while the right methylamino-piperidinyl group forms weak hydrogen bonds with key guanines, such multi-heterocyclic and complex structures are characteristic of many small-molecule drugs, this compound can highly specifically bind to the aptamer pocket of the bacterial FMN riboswitch, mimicking the conformation of the natural ligand FMN and blocking the binding of FMN to RNA, thereby relieving the negative feedback inhibition of the riboswitch on the riboflavin synthesis gene ribB, leading to impaired bacterial growth due to riboflavin depletion, and importantly, its activity can be completely reversed by exogenous riboflavin, meanwhile this compound is often designed to target specific protein kinases, by inhibiting key kinases, it may find applications in the treatment of cancer, inflammation, or autoimmune diseases.

Small_molecule_information: quinolin-8-ol, also referred to as 8-hydroxyquinoline, is another important isomer of quinolinol, with the hydroxyl group positioned at the 8-position, adjacent to the nitrogen atom, and this compound is structurally an azaaromatic compound with a quinoline scaffold (fused benzene and pyridine rings) and a hydroxyl group at the 8-position, showing planar and hydrophobic traits typical of azaaromatics, this ortho relationship allows the hydroxyl oxygen and the quinoline nitrogen to form a perfect five-membered ring structure, making quinolin-8-ol a recognized, highly selective chelating agent for various divalent and trivalent metal ions (e.g., Al3, Zn2, Mg2, Fe3, Cu2), this chelating ability has multiple real-world applications: it can be used as a precipitating agent or chromogenic agent in analytical chemistry for the quantitative analysis of metal ions, in medicine, its derivatives (such as Clioquinol) have been used as anti-amoebic and antifungal drugs, more recently, quinolin-8-ol derivatives have gained attention in the development of Alzheimers disease drugs, as they may reduce neurotoxicity by chelating abnormal metal ions (such as copper and zinc) in the brain, functionally, though the document focuses on azaaromatics binding yjdF riboswitches, no data on its riboswitch binding affinity or gene expression effects is provided. 8-RNA riboswitch

RNA information: A distinct class of RNAs containing 1×1 nucleotide internal loops within expanded trinucleotide repeats has been implicated in trinucleotide repeat expansion disorders (TREDs), a group comprising over 20 neurodegenerative and neuromuscular disorders.
Small_molecule_information: Trisodium;5-oxo-1-(4-sulfonatophenyl)-4-[(4-sulfonatophenyl)diazenyl]-4H-pyrazole-3-carboxylate, also referred to as Tartrazine (and known as the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110)), is the chemical name for the food additive Sunset Yellow FCF (E110), a synthetic azo dye with the formula C16H10N2Na3O7[(2R,3S,4S,5R,6R)-2-[(2R,3S,4S,5S,6S)-2-[(1R,2S)-3-[[(2R,3S,4S)-5-[[(2S,3R)-1-[2-[4-[4-[3-[4-[[2-[[2-[[2-[acetyl(propyl)amino]acetyl]-propylamino]acetyl]-prop-2-ynylamino]acetyl]amino]butylamino]propylcarbamoyl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]-1,3-thiazol-2-yl]ethylamino]-3-hydroxy-1-oxobutan-2-yl]amino]-3-hydroxy-4-methyl-5-oxopentan-2-yl]amino]-2-[[6-amino-2-[(1S)-3-amino-1-[[(2S)-2,3-diamino-3-oxopropyl]amino]-3-oxopropyl]-5-methylpyrimidine-4-carbonyl]amino]-1-(1H-imidazol-4-yl)-3-oxopropoxy]-4,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-3-yl]oxy-3,5-dihydroxy-6-(hydroxymethyl)oxan-4-yl]carbamate, and Tartrazine is a synthetic yellow azo dye used as a food coloring agent while primarily an industrial colorant, its structure features a pyrazolone core linked by an azo bond (NN) to two benzene rings, both substituted with sodium sulfonate groups, and exists as a trisodium salt, the azo structure imparts a vibrant color, in real-world use, Sunset Yellow is widely employed as an orange-yellow coloring agent in foods, beverages, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics, such as orange juice, sodas, candies, and ice cream, to enhance visual appeal, as a common food colorant, its use is strictly regulated globally to ensure safety within prescribed limits, despite some controversy regarding its potential to affect behavior in sensitive individuals, particularly children.

RNA information: r(CUG)₁₀ is an RNA fragment with 10 consecutive CUG repeats, structurally forming a stable hairpin with 10 5′CUG/3′GUC internal loops, U·U mismatches, and optional GC clamps (5′/3′ ends) for enhanced stability. Functionally, it lacks toxicity of pathogenic long (CUG)ₙ  but binds MBNL1 with high affinity, interacts specifically with lomofungin  and dilomofungin . It serves as an in vitro tool for studying MBNL1-(CUG) interaction mechanisms and screening DM1 therapeutic small molecules.
Small_molecule_information: 8-(9-carboxy-4-formyl-1,3,6-trihydroxyphenazin-2-yl)-6-formyl-4,7,9-trihydroxyphenazine-1-carboxylic acid, also referred to as Dilomofungin, is an extremely complex dimeric molecule, consisting of two highly substituted phenazine units linked by a carbon-carbon bond, which is a spontaneous dimer of lomofungin formed in DMSO and structurally features a carboncarbon bond between two lomofungin monomers. The phenazine scaffold is a heterocyclic system made of two benzene rings fused with two nitrogen atoms, and many natural phenazine derivatives (like pyocyanin) are pigments produced by microorganisms with potent antibiotic and biological redox activities. This compound possesses multiple functional groups, including hydroxyl, carboxyl, and formyl groups, which lend the molecule multiple hydrogen bond donor/acceptor capabilities and high water solubility, and it exhibits higher potency than lomofungin in inhibiting MBNL1-CUG RNA binding in vitro, while also having strong binding affinity and the ability to partially rescue mis-splicing in DM1 cell models, though it has the unintended effect of stabilizing toxic CUG repeat RNA, leading to its accumulation in nuclear foci and reduced transcript turnover, which limits its therapeutic potential for myotonic dystrophy type 1. The phenazine rings establish it as a potent redox-active molecule capable of mediating electron transfer in biological systems. In reality, such structurally complex phenazine derivatives are often explored as novel antibiotics or anticancer agents, where the mechanism of action might involve DNA intercalation or the generation of reactive oxygen species to induce apoptosis. This molecule could also serve as a chelating agent or redox indicator in biochemical analysis.

RNA information: HIV-1 TAR RNA is a conserved stem-bulge-loop hairpin at the 5′ end of all HIV-1 transcripts, existing as 29–59-nt fragments. Its structure features a double-stranded stem, a 3-base pyrimidine bulge (e.g., UCU), and a 6-nt loop. Functionally, it binds Tat’s arginine-rich segment, recruits P-TEFb, boosts RNA Pol II processivity (viral gene expression >100-fold), aids reverse transcription, and is a key therapeutic target for HIV infections.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[3-amino-4-(3-piperidin-1-ylpropoxy)phenyl]-1-methylquinolin-4-one, also referred to as Compound Phenylquinolone 5, this compound is a quinolinone derivative (it is also a 2-phenylquinolone derivative featuring an N1-methyl group (R Me), unsubstituted positions at both C5 and C7 (R H, R H), an oxygen linker (X O), a three-carbon chain (n 3), and a terminal piperidine group (R -N)), its activity may be explored in the areas of antifungal or antiproliferative action, similar to potential antimalarial, antimicrobial, or anti-tumor drugs, and it exhibits potent in vitro inhibitory activity against the Tat-TAR interaction, demonstrates significantly improved cellular uptake compared to lead compounds, as well as a residual dose-dependent anti-HIV-1 activity coupled with low cytotoxicity, representing a promising balance between target inhibition and cell penetration.

RNA information: The IRES-A (MU1) is a mutant with a G-tract mutation. While it still has the potential to form a G-quadruplex, its structural stability is lower compared to the wild-type sequence.
Small_molecule_information: 2-[[5-(diaminomethylideneamino)-2-[[2-[[2-[[7-[(1-methylpiperidin-4-yl)methoxy]-[1]benzofuro[3,2-b]quinolin-11-yl]amino]acetyl]amino]acetyl]amino]pentanoyl]amino]-3-hydroxypropanoic acidThis conjugate combines the benzofuro[3,2-b]quinoline core with Arginine and Serine. Serine is a hydroxyl-containing amino acid susceptible to phosphorylation, a vital regulatory process in cellular signaling. This compounds potential activity is antineoplastic, with the Serine residue possibly utilized to improve water solubility and biocompatibility or serving as a site for enzymatic modification (e.g., by kinases), thereby regulating the drugs activity or release.
